Yet, the pedagogies associated have not been thoroughly investigated, and the pedagogical environment has not been formed. The significant advancement of technological advances in computing has resulted in the adoption of fuzzy system theories as a fundamental element of artificial intelligence (AI) in domains such as medicine, finance, economics, and commerce. As a result, the study presents a multifaceted fuzzy decision-making (MFFDM) built on AI to shorten the assessment procedure and offer a successful structure for analyzing instructional approaches. The structure incorporates human-type language utilizing fuzzy directions, taking into account three assessment views (the managerial phase, teachers, and learners), and applies the improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) method. Following the teaching professional has decided the scores for every variable, they are passed into the enhanced search technique and resolved by employing an impartial mechanism to evaluate the evaluation's outcome. It includes the learners' motion process and transfer vector disassembling, which are built according to operational requirements. An approach for measuring teaching standards was built using the suggested framework with PSO. The outcomes show that the suggested method received the most favorable values in various evaluation groups, with the mean skill effectiveness of 97.1%, learning advancement of 87.4%, socialization skills of 93.5%, participation proportion of 95.1%, student contentment of 95.5%, and teaching social theory of 96.9% effectiveness. The suggested system's efficacy in improving social theory teaching techniques was proved through contrasting its outcomes with conventional approaches. It helps to advance pedagogical approaches in the discipline.

title: Automated Seizure Detection: A Hybrid Spatio-Temporal Architecture
authors: Vinay Babu Vemulapalli, Abdulla Mohammed, Saswat Kumar Ram, Banee Bandana Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Epilepsy is a neurological disorder that affects millions worldwide, which features seizures. Though the Electroencephalogram (EEG) is the standard test for manual processing, it is expensive, time-consuming, and liable to error because of humanity’s limited endurance compared to machines’ capacity to process data continually. Even though there is "class imbalance," where rare seizure occurrences result in poor sensitivity in clinical models. This research proposes a robust "Seizure detection framework using the TUH EEG Seizure Corpus." (v2.0.3). In this research, we propose a Hybrid that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks to extract spatial features and Long Short-Term Memory networks to capture temporal dependencies. A significant contribution of this research is a data balance approach through under-sampling for handling the lack of seizure data. The developed model performed well, achieving 91.0% Recall and 94.7% accuracy on the validation set, considerably better than the baseline of around 30% from previous works. This shows that dealing with data imbalance is a crucial step in the process of developing effective screening devices that are of a clinical grade to help neurologists.

title: PathwayVis: Interactive Visualization of Decision Pathways in a Tree-Based Machine Learning Model
authors: Yuanzhe Jin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Decision trees are among the most widely used machine learning models, valued for their interpretability and computational efficiency. However, existing visualization approaches typically present static representations, making it difficult to trace the decision paths of individual samples within the tree. In this paper, we introduce an interactive visualization tool called “PathwayVis” that enables users to explore decision paths in trees generated by the scikit-learn library. Through case studies on public datasets, we demonstrate the tool's effectiveness and discuss how it can support tasks such as model debugging and transparent communication of machine learning decisions. This work contributes to the growing field of interactive visual analytics for interpretable machine learning.

title: Secure UAV-Assisted ISAC with Vehicular Jamming and Trajectory Optimization
authors: Yong-Zhe Lu, Chien-Chun Wang, Szu-Liang Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key technology for 6G UAV-enabled IoT services, yet the strong line-of-sight air-ground channels also heighten eavesdropping risks. This paper studies a secure UAV-assisted ISAC system where a dual-functional UAV performs radar sensing and communication, and a nearby vehicle provides cooperative jamming against an external eavesdropper (EVE). Both largescale path-loss and small-scale fading are modeled for realistic transmission links. We jointly optimize the UAV trajectory, ISAC power allocation, vehicular jamming power, and vehicle location to maximize the secrecy rate. The resulting non-convex problem is solved via an alternating optimization framework combining semidefinite relaxation (SDR) and successive convex approximation (SCA). Simulation results show that the proposed joint design substantially enhances secrecy performance over baseline schemes, while satisfying radar sensing requirements.

title: Prefrontal EEG Signal-Based Insomnia Classification Using EMD and Hybrid Features
authors: P.Mamta, S.V.A.V Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: One of the most prevalent signs of depression is insomnia, which refers to the inability to fall or stay asleep, and can worsen the severity and duration of depressive episodes. Early diagnosis and effective treatment of insomnia are crucial to prevent related health issues. In this work, we present an empirical mode decomposition (EMD) based methodology to identify insomnia from prefrontal EEG signals available in the CAPSleep database. The EEG signals are decomposed with EMD to produce Intrinsic Mode Functions (IMFs), from which features are extracted for each IMF band. These features are then reduced via principal component analysis (PCA). Four machine learning techniques, such as Ensemble KNN (EKNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), k-nearest neighbor (KNN), and Decision Tree (DT), are used to classify the resultant feature vectors and evaluation through 5-fold cross-validation. The study reports a 79.7% accuracy with the Decision Tree classifier, highlighting the potential of prefrontal EEG signals in diagnosing insomnia.

title: Elevating GNSS-R Data Reliability: A Deep Learning Framework for Robust RFI Anomaly Detection
authors: Qianyang Shen, Yuhang Li, Pengbo Ju, Qiming Li, Deguo Zeng, Zhiyin Wang, Xuanpeng Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System Reflectometry (GNSS-R) data is highly vulnerable to radio frequency interference (RFI), which originates from the reflections of regional GNSS and Satellite-Based Augmentation Systems (SBAS), and external L-band interference signals from the ground. These influences show different textures in the delay-Doppler Map (DDM) of GNSS-R, but existing RFI anomaly detection methods cannot effectively distinguish their characteristics, resulting in high false positives and false negatives. To address these limitations, we propose a deep learning method that integrates contrastive self-supervised pretraining (SimCLR) on large-scale unlabeled DDMs using DDM-aware augmentation and a Swin backbone, followed by supervised fine-tuning of a Swin Transformer classifier under class-imbalance-aware optimization. The self-supervised pretraining stage extracts intrinsic DDM representations robust to scene and constellation variability, and the subsequent fine-tuning leverages limited labeled data to sharpen decision boundaries and curb both FPR and FNR. Experimental results show that the proposed method elevates RFI anomaly detection accuracy from approximately 25% to over 95%, with strong generalization across multiple GNSS constellations and operational scenarios. This approach substantially outperforms traditional mechanisms, reducing false alarms and missed detections, and thereby offering a reliable solution for real-world GNSS-R applications.

title: Software Procurement Duplicate Detection Using Fuzzy Matching and LLM Verification
authors: Cheng-En Liao, Kun-Da Wu, Li-Ya Wang, Shih-Chih Wang, Ying-Hsu Chen, Chia-Pei Lee, Yung-Hsing Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Software procurement processes in governments and large enterprises often suffer from duplicate or highly similar product entries, leading to redundant purchases, unnecessary spending, and inefficiencies in budget allocation. This study proposes a high-accuracy duplicate detection system that integrates three major components: (1)ChineseEnglish tokenization for heterogeneous software product descriptions, (2)fuzzy weighted similarity matching to compute cross-field semantic proximity, and (3)local large language model (LLM)-based verification module to refine borderline cases. Experiments were conducted on a dataset of 5,000 software product records containing product names, specification descriptions, vendors, service periods, and quoted prices. Human-validated results show that the fuzzy weighted matching method independently achieves an accuracy of 98%. When combined with the LLM verification step, overall accuracy reaches 100%, demonstrating that traditional algorithms and modern AI reasoning can form a complementary hybrid framework with industry-level performance. The system has been deployed in Taiwan's governmental procurement workflow, supporting large-scale similarity analysis for over 100,000 software product entries in 2025. This deployment significantly reduced manual comparison workloads and prevented potential duplicate purchases, proving that the proposed system is both scalable and practically valuable. Our study demonstrates that combining deterministic fuzzy matching with LLM-based semantic assessment can yield a robust, noise-tolerant solution for real-world procurement auditing.

title: Magnetic Properties Measurement and Analysis of Ultra-thin Grain-oriented Silicon Steel Considering Uniaxial and Biaxial Stress Loads
authors: Yu Dou, Xueying Zhu, Tao Chen, Jiapeng Zhou, Yongjian Li, Jianguo Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Ultra-thin grain-oriented (GO) silicon steel is gradually being applied in high-power medium-frequency transformers and reactors due to its excellent magnetic properties. However, during assembly and operation, mechanical components such as clips and bolts impose complex stresses on the core material. Although these stresses significantly influence magnetic properties of the core materials, the stress behavior of magnetic materials is often overlooked during the electrical equipment design phase due to testing difficulties. To decrease the design errors and enhance equipment reliability, it is necessary to construct a magnetic properties tester considering stress conditions and conduct corresponding tests. In this paper, piezoelectric actuators are employed to apply uniaxial and biaxial stresses to the samples. The hysteresis loops and core loss of ultra-thin GO steel GT100 are measured under various stress conditions to investigate the magneto-mechanical mechanisms. In addition, the measured core losses are separated into hysteresis losses, classical eddy current losses, and excess losses based on the loss separation theory. This approach allows for quantifying the influence of stress on loss components of ultra-thin GO silicon steel.

title: Intrusion Detection System Using Distributed Deep Learning: An Optimized Pipeline to Speed Up High-Dimensional Data Learning
authors: Nour El-Din Fathy, Yasmine N.M. Saleh, Khaled Mahar
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: For efficiently detecting and investigating malicious activity in network traffic, intrusion detection systems are among the most widely used and well-known detection methods. In either online or offline modes, the intrusion detection system can identify malware and anomalous activity through data processing. The main challenges arise from the high-dimensional features of massive datasets and the complexity of large deep learning models with numerous layers, which are extremely difficult to train on a single device. Therefore, for much faster training and better testing accuracy, an effective strategy with minimal communication overhead is required. A distributed technique for training deep neural networks that performs effectively for memory-intensive tasks is pipeline parallelism. In this paper, an enhanced pipeline parallelism approach is proposed using multiple GPUs for resolving the idle stages problem. The UNSW-NB15 high-dimensional data set has been used to test this approach. This suggested model achieved a high detection rate of 93% and 2.5 X speedup in training time compared to other distributed approaches using model parallelism. Furthermore, the suggested model demonstrated a 40% reduction in memory usage and a noticeable decrease in overhead and communication costs. The comparison with the state-of-the-art techniques shows that the proposed approach is more effective and suitable for complex data and large models.

title: Combined Experimental-Numerical Spatio-Temporal Mapping of RF-EMF Exposure in City Environments
authors: Matthias Leeman, Kenneth Deprez, Jeroen Van Der Straeten, Mart Verlaek, Michel Goethals, Karen Van Campenhout, Günter Vermeeren, Wout Joseph
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rollout of 5G has increased spatial and temporal variability in RF-EMF exposure due to higher frequency bands, beamforming, and denser deployments, challenging conventional maps that rely only on simulations or sparse measurements. We present a hybrid framework that fuses deterministic ray-tracing with heterogeneous data (static sensor node data gathered over a two-year period, a mobile spectrum analyzer (SA) campaign, and personal exposimeter measurements) to generate high-resolution spatio-temporal exposure maps for a dense urban area of 2.5 km2. At its core is the Mean-Matched Gradient-Preserving Optimization (MMGPO), which estimates a smooth multiplicative correction field over the simulated grid to remove bias while preserving logarithmic gradients. When validated against 28 independent SA measurements, MMGPO achieves an RMSE = 5.47 dB and correlation r = 0.70 for the total electric field exposure over all frequency bands. By incorporating temporally varying node data, this approach provides policymakers, researchers, and the public with reliable, high-resolution exposure assessments across multiple time windows, capturing spatial detail while reflecting temporal variations in modern wireless networks.

title: Need-Oriented VR Interaction Design for Adults with Intellectual Disabilities
authors: Lihui Liu, Yiran Peng, Xin Lyu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: Virtual reality (VR) is increasingly used in rehabilitation and assistive technologies, yet many deployments remain technology-driven, emphasizing immersion and novelty over the everyday needs of people with disabilities. This paper asks how VR systems for rehabilitation and vocational training can be designed around practical user needs rather than technical capabilities alone. We propose a need-oriented design framework with three layers that link practical needs, usage context and interaction design, and demonstrate it through a case study of a VR vocational training system for adults with intellectual disabilities. The results show that integrating VR into bakery training roughly doubled productivity compared with conventional training. Task-structured, multimodal and error-tolerant interactions helped trainees rehearse complex workflows safely, reduced cognitive load and supported their self-confidence. Compared with general accessibility and universal design frameworks, the proposed three-layer framework focuses on step-by-step reasoning from everyday disability-related needs to concrete interaction choices in a specific training setting. This work offers HCI and VR researchers a practical way to translate disability-related needs and real-world constraints into concrete, accessible interaction designs.

title: Joint-Specialist Learning with Endoscopic Descriptor Guidance for Ulcerative Colitis Grading
authors: Sakib Ahammed, Robert Chandler, Moi Hoon Yap, Xinqi Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate endoscopic grading of ulcerative colitis (UC) is essential for disease monitoring and treatment planning. Most existing approaches focus on predicting either the Mayo endoscopic score (MES) or ulcerative colitis endoscopic index of severity (UCEIS) independently, without modeling their clinical relationship. In the work, we propose a multi-task joint-specialist learning framework with endoscopic descriptor guidance (JSL-EDG) for comprehensive UC grading. Our method employs a shared encoder to jointly learn representations for both MES and UCEIS, while two dedicated specialist models further enhance specific scoring for each task. We present an endoscopic descriptor loss that supervises metadata to strengthen clinically meaningful learning for UCEIS. Additionally, we introduce a descriptor-UCEIS consistency loss to align the predicted UCEIS scores with metadata, using severity combinations derived from vascular patterns, bleeding, and erosion/ulcer. At inference, we incorporate a regularization mechanism that enforces clinically plausible severity transitions via a MES-UCEIS relationship based on the empirical conditional distribution. Experiments on the EndoUC challenge leaderboard demonstrate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance for MES and competitive results for the more challenging finegrained UCEIS grading task.

title: Dispersion Engineered On-Chip Silicon Nitride Fabry-Perot Resonators
authors: Z. Saleh, F. Rinaldo Talenti, R. Prosopio, Y. Yang, S. Edmond, E. Cassan, D. Melati, D. Marris-Morini, L. Vivien, C. Alonso-Ramos
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: On-chip dispersion engineering is instrumental for nonlinear applications in communications, sensing, and quantum technologies. We demonstrate dispersion control in silicon nitride Fabry-Perot resonators with distributed Bragg reflectors. We experimentally show our approach enables switching between normal and anomalous dispersion regimes, independent of intracavity waveguide dispersion.

title: 2kV SiC Full-Bridge Converter Power Stage Design for SST-Based Extreme Fast Chargers
authors: Tohfa Haque, Wensong Yu, Srdjan Lukic
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a power stage design for a 2 kV SiC full-bridge converter that minimizes parasitic inductance while meeting thermal, isolation, and mechanical constraints. Using cost effective 2 kV SiC discrete devices in a side-by-side and back-to-back configuration optimizes current distribution and reduces switching transients. A dual local decoupling capacitor structure and strategically placed DC-link capacitors suppress voltage overshoot and extend capacitor lifespan. Flux cancellation within interleaved PCB layers and multiple parallel decoupling capacitors enhances electromagnetic compatibility. Advanced thermal management with Aluminum Nitride (AIN), phase-change materials, pressed rubber, and optimized heatsinks ensure better efficiency. A 60 kW Active-Front End (AFE) prototype at 50 A and 1.5 kV validates reduced loop inductance, voltage overshoots less than 17% in a continuous test, and improved performance, making this approach ideal for scalable SST with medium voltage DC (MVDC) extreme fast charging (XFC) stations.

title: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection
authors: Jihyeon Baek, Seunghoon Lee, Gitaek Kwon, Doohyun Park
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Out-of-distribution (OOD) detection is essential for building reliable AI systems, as models that produce outputs for invalid inputs cannot be trusted. Although deep learning (DL) is often assumed to outperform traditional machine learning (ML), medical imaging data are typically acquired under standardized protocols, leading to relatively constrained image variability in OOD detection tasks. This motivates a direct comparison between ML and DL approaches in this setting. The two approaches are evaluated on open datasets comprising over 60,000 fundus and non-fundus images across multiple resolutions. Both approaches achieved an AUROC of 1.000 and accuracies between 0.999 and 1.000 on internal and external validation sets, showing comparable detection performance. The ML approach, however, exhibited substantially lower end-to-end latency while maintaining equivalent accuracy, indicating greater computational efficiency. These results suggest that for OOD detection tasks of limited visual complexity, lightweight ML approaches can achieve DL-level performance with significantly reduced computational cost, supporting practical real-world deployment.

title: Cold-Start Recommendation via Hybrid Matrix Factorization and Cluster-Based Association Rules
authors: Sumitha T, Aswin R, Lakshmanan V, Krishna Charan K V S
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: As we get accustomed to recommender systems, one issue that thrived since time immemorial is the cold-start issue. Lack of demanding interaction data for collaborative filtering while confronting new users, is found to be the cause of this problem. Although CFCAI aids with sparsity handling, it turns a blind eye to the latent preferences. Our research proves to be fruitful in solving this issue by using a hybrid approach that blends Matrix Factorization with Cluster-based association rules and content features. We have a combination of K-Means clustering, Association rule Mining, Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and Ridge Regression resulting in efficient recommendations even when the user history is scarce. In cold-start conditions (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k=1$</tex> seed item per user), our results have a Precision@10 of 19.3% and Recall@10 of 4.5 %, which significantly outperforms the existing one, which is conducive to our intriguing idea of blending rulebased and latent factor methods. Its utilization can be multi-fold, ranging from supporting larger datasets to efficiently aligning with neural models and production systems.

title: Hierarchical Multi-Scale Graph Learning with Knowledge-Guided Attention for Whole-Slide Image Survival Analysis
authors: Bin Xu, Yufei Zhou, Bolin Song, Jingwen Sun, Yang Bian, Yikai Chen, Cheng Lu, Ye Wu, Jianfei Tu, Xiangxue Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We propose a Hierarchical Multi-scale Knowledge-aware Graph Network (HMKGN) that models multi-scale interactions and spatially hierarchical relationships within whole-slide images (WSIs) for cancer prognostication. Unlike conventional attention-based MIL, which ignores spatial organization, or graph-based MIL, which relies on static handcrafted graphs, HMKGN enforces a hierarchical structure with spatial locality constraints, wherein local cellular-level dynamic graphs aggregate spatially proximate patches within each region of interest (ROI) and a global slide-level dynamic graph integrates ROI-level features into WSI-level representations. Moreover, multi-scale integration at the ROI level combines coarse contextual features from broader views with fine-grained structural representations from local patch-graph aggregation. We evaluate HMKGN on four TCGA cohorts (KIRC, LGG, PAAD, and STAD; <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}=513$</tex>, 487, 138, and 370) for survival prediction. It consistently outperforms existing MIL-based models, yielding improved concordance indices (10.85% better) and statistically significant stratification of patient survival risk (log-rank <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>).

title: Multi-Port DCX Converter for EV On-Board Charging and Low-Voltage Power Distribution
authors: Héctor Sarnago, Óscar Lucía
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Modern battery electric vehicles (EVs) include a 400/800 V high-voltage network, mainly used for charging and motor drive purposes, and a 12/48 V low-voltage network, used to power all the low voltage loads in the vehicle. Current power conversion schemes use different power converters and architectures, leading to complex and costly implementations. On the one hand, on-board chargers (OBC) are used to provide ac-charging in an efficient and convenient way. On the other hand, the low-voltage battery and network are managed by dc-dc converters, playing a key role in providing a reliable low-voltage dc-network for ancillary loads. However, new trends on bidirectional power conversion and low-voltage battery elimination, as well as the need for cost-effective and high-power-density power conversion, require redesigned power conversion architectures. The main aim of this paper is to provide an integrated power converter consisting of a quadruple-port implementation featuring a dc-transformer power conversion scheme (QDCX). It enables improved implementation with high power-density that simultaneously provides on-board charging capabilities and low-voltage power distribution. The topology is experimentally validated on an 11 kW, 2×400V OBC prototype supplying simultaneously a 12-V low-voltage generation.

title: An Explainable Human-in-the-Loop Agentic AI Framework for Continuous Financial Planning and Analysis Beyond Forecast Accuracy
authors: Ramsundernag Changalva
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Recent developments in artificial intelligence have enhanced the accuracy of financial forecasting, but most Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) studies have been preoccupied with predictive AI-powered decision systems design as opposed to the reliability of AI-powered forecasting. The missing element in real research relates to the lack of an AInative architecture of the full FP&A process that would consolidate uncertainty-aware predictive acts, explainable predictive reasoning, and operational responsibility, along with human governance. This paper suggests an Explainable Human-in-the-Loop Agentic AI model making FP&A not a one-off forecasting exercise, but an ongoing, AI-aided decision-making process. It has a combination of the probabilistic forecasting modules that are coupled with the explainable AI modules, multi agent workflow orchestration and structured human approval points to support transparency and accountable financial decision making. Autonomous agents implement the data ingestion coordination, and scenario simulation and budget optimization workloads and ensure auditability and compliance throughout the planning lifecycle by maintaining integrated governance layers. In contrast to the conventional methods that engage in the assessment of success based on accuracy of prediction, the suggested framework proposes decision adoption measures to determine whether AI-powered insights are accepted and used by the leaders of the financial industry. The architecture thus strives to bring FP&A to a scalable, interpretable, and governable AI ecosystem with the power to enable planning, budgeting, and strategic reforecasting on an ongoing basis.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning (ARL) Control of a Multi-port Resonant Converter in UAV Systems
authors: Erdem Asa, Kerim Colak
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This study presents an adaptive reinforcement learning (ARL) control framework for a multi-port resonant converter used in hybrid unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) power systems. The converter integrates high-frequency half-bridge input ports connected to a rectified engine–generator set and a battery energy storage system, along with a semi-bridgeless active rectifier supplying the propulsion load. A deep RL agent is trained to dynamically regulate inter-port phase-shift commands in real time based on flight conditions and load power demand. The ARL controller autonomously identifies phase-shift combinations that maximize conversion efficiency while maintaining stable and coordinated power flow, even under rapidly varying operating scenarios. This data-driven approach eliminates the need for explicit system modeling or extensive manual tuning and enables coordinated control among multiple power ports without inter-port communication. Experimental results validate that the ARL based strategy achieves reliable power sharing and consistently high-efficiency operation across diverse UAV operating conditions.

title: Cyber Modelling of Advanced Metering Infrastructure using ns-3
authors: Issa Mlimbila, Amit Kumar, Himanshu Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Digital communication is an integral component of smart grids. However, while physical flow of electricity has been modelled for decades, it is difficult to find instances of simulating the communications network for smart grid planning and operations. This paper addresses this gap by presenting an approach to simulate advanced metering infrastructure (AMI) communication model, firewalls and cyberattacks in ns-3. The performance of the developed model is evaluated on several IEEE-13, 34, 37, and 123 node feeder models. Based on the simulations performed using these models it was observed that propagation delay and system loss are key parameters that influence the performance of the AMI system. Results for the impact of cyberattacks with and without the consideration of firewalls show significant differences in the time taken for data to be received at the desired locations. Further, the wall-clock time required to simulate the IEEE 123-node feeder is around 19 seconds, compared to 3 seconds it takes to simulate the IEEE 13-node feeder. Although the amount of data exchanged increased 11 folds, the time taken increased only 6 times, demonstrating the scalability potential of ns-3 for modelling AMI systems.

title: Dual-Wavelength SiN-Waveguide Coupled High-Speed Ge Photodiodes in a Photonic BiCMOS Process
authors: S. Lischke, R. M. Pattery, C. Mai, S. Marschmeyer, A. Peczek, A. Kroh, L. Zimmermann
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: We demonstrate directly silicon-nitride waveguide coupled high-speed germanium photodiodes for first-window <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(850 ~\text{nm})$</tex> and O-band applications, that are monolithically integrated in a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.25 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> photonic BiCMOS platform offering SiGeHBTs with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{f}_{\mathbf{T}} / \mathbf{f}_{\text {max }}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 3 0} / \mathbf{2 8 0 ~ G H Z}$</tex>.

title: Low-Loss Pavement-Embedded Multi-MHz Capacitive Wireless Power Transfer System using Optimized Curved Couplers
authors: Syed Saeed Rashid, Raquel Sarabia-Soto, Dheeraj Etta, Khurram K. Afridi
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) systems for electric vehicles (EVs) enable dynamic charging while in motion and offer a promising solution to concerns of limited driving range and long charging times. Although inductive WPT systems have been widely studied, they are bulky, costly, and constrained by ferrite losses. Capacitive WPT systems avoid ferrites and are lighter and less expensive, but when embedded in pavements they suffer high dielectric losses due to fringing electric fields at the coupler edges. This work proposes novel curved couplers that suppresses fringing electric fields, thereby reducing losses in pavement materials. A 6.78-MHz, 1-kW capacitive WPT system using the proposed curved couplers is designed and analyzed. In simulations, the optimized curved couplers achieve a 70% reduction in pavement losses and a 40% reduction in surrounding fringing fields compared to conventional flat couplers. Two 6.78-MHz 1-kW experimental prototypes, one using flat couplers and the other using the proposed curved couplers, are constructed and tested within asphalt. The experimental prototype using curved couplers embedded in pavement achieves 45% loss reduction in asphalt slab compared to the conventional flat couplers system.

title: Arabic Economic Text News Summarization: An AI-driven Extractive Approach
authors: Iyas Qaddara, Insaf Kraidia, Mohammad O. Hiari, Nida Al-Shafi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: This article discusses AI-extractive summarization of Arabic economic news based on the use of massive data sets obtained using two major Arabic news outlets, Al Jazeera and Al Arabiya. In the paper under consideration, the problem of summarizing the Arabic economic text is discussed, its peculiarities include a large volume of numerical information, named entities, and specific terminology in the field. The suggested system uses the feature extraction and cosine similarity sentence scoring pipeline with TF and IDF as its foundation to produce extractive summaries. ROUGE-1 and ROUGE-2 metrics were used to analyze the model and estimate precision, recall, and $\mathbf{F 1}$-score. Rough estimates indicated that ROUGE-1 influenced better performance than ROUGE-2 in both datasets, and the average precision of the results was about 0.82, the average recall was about 0.74, and the average F1-score was about 0.78 in the case of Al Jazeera, and was slightly better in the case of Al Arabiya (meaning Al Arabiya had a higher average precision of 0.84 and a higher average recall of 0.75). The results emphasize the usefulness of extractive summarization of Arabic news, particularly in shorter and more factual content, along with the potential of AI-based extractive summarization to improve the accessibility of news and assist with financial analysis, and also reveal the future opportunities to integrate abstractive neural network models with domainconscious embedding.

title: State Space Modeling of User Intent Drift for Confidence-Calibrated News Recommendation
authors: N M Vedhinee, Rajalakshmi D, Janisa Ria, Prakratee Singh
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: News Article suggestion models these days often rely on user click histories that are based on static preference estimates without any measure of the confidence level of their recommendations. Problems arise when user interests change dynamically, and their news article recommendations are not aligned with their preferences. We aim to tackle this situation with the help of the State Space Model (SSM) using Large Language Model (LLM) that keeps a hidden state, which tracks evolving user interest and preference changes over time. Using the MIND dataset, the LLM grasps the meaning of the new articles and stores them as dense vectors. Along with this, the user's progress as well as their changes in articles of interest are tracked using SSM's hidden state. While traditional recommendation engines show you articles ranked by relevance alone, our model additionally includes a second dimension: quantifying how confident it is about its recommendation. This shift reduces the over-confidence in the model's predictions, which proves valuable for readers with sporadic changes in topics of interest. By building uncertainty into the engine itself, our goal is to recommend news articles to users without hiding how the choices were made, even when new trends emerge rapidly.

title: Hybrid Preprocessing and Deep Learning for Real-Time Aerial Accident Detection Using Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Rajae Ziki, Amine Zeguendry, Majdoulin Sabri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Accidents in urban and road environments pose significant risks to human life and property. Traditional monitoring systems, such as fixed cameras and manual inspections, have limitations in coverage and response speed. Drones equipped with computer vision systems provide a flexible solution for real-time monitoring and rapid accident detection. This work presents a drone-based framework for accident detection using state of the art object detection models. A hybrid image preprocessing pipeline, combining noise reduction and motion blur correction, is applied to enhance detection accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv8 achieves 92% precision with 18.3 ms inference time, significantly outperforming RT-DETR-L which achieves 62% precision. The proposed preprocessing approach improves detection performance by 4.7% in mean Average Precision while maintaining real-time processing capability. Comparative analysis reveals that YOLOv8 is particularly suitable for real-time drone applications due to its superior precision-speed trade-off. The study provides evidence that proper preprocessing is essential for aerial imagery, with our hybrid method substantially improving detection confidence across different model architectures. This approach demonstrates the effectiveness of drones in incident monitoring and offers practical insights for deploying aerial surveillance systems in urban and road environments.

title: ST-SAM: A Spatio-Temporal Enhanced Segment Anything Model for Echocardiogram Video Segmentation
authors: Ningshu Li, Minglang Chen, Bin Pu, Jingqiao Li, Xiaomeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Echocardiogram video segmentation is crucial for enabling rapid, objective, and reproducible quantification of cardiac structures, which is the key for the diagnosis and treatment of cardiovascular diseases. Although recent SAM-based methods have achieved promising results, they often struggle to effectively capture spatio-temporal features in echocardiogram videos, resulting in limited performance for multi-structure segmentation. To this end, we propose the Spatio-Temporal Segment Anything Model (ST-SAM)—a novel framework comprising a Spatial Topology Module (STM) and a Temporal Graph Enhanced Module (TGEM) that jointly enhance spatial topological relationships and temporal consistency in echocardiogram videos. Experimental results demonstrate that ST-SAM outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods by an average of 3.0% Dice across four-chamber segmentation tasks. The source code can be found at https://github.com/xmed-lab/ST-SAM.

title: Federated Learning with Layer-Wise Contribution Estimation and Consensus-Based Parameter Personalization for Medical Image Classification
authors: Ming Cai, Tianpeng Deng, Jing Li, Zaiyi Liu, Ying Wang, Guoqiang Han, Chu Han
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Personalized Federated Learning (PFL) presents a promising paradigm for mitigating privacy concerns and addressing data heterogeneity in medical images. However, the presence of data heterogeneity can introduce bias into the global model. As a result, the biased global model may degrade local performance in the personalization phase. To tackle this challenge, we propose FedLP, a novel framework that enhances global aggregation fairness and improves personalized performance. First, we propose a layer-wise aggregation strategy on the server, where it dynamically estimates each client's contribution based on the gradient discrepancies. Consider the aggregated global model may not consistently align with local distributions, we then introduce an adaptive parameter personalization process on the client side. By selectively instituting local parameters with the global model parameter with maximizing consensus optimization, clients can better retain global knowledge they need and maintain important model parameters to local data distributions. Experimental results demonstrate that FedLP outperforms state-of-the-art methods and achieves fairer performance for several medical image classification tasks.

title: From Volumes to Slices: Computationally Efficient Contrastive Learning for Sequential Abdominal CT Analysis
authors: Po-Kai Chiu, Hung-Hsuan Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The requirement for expert annotations limits the effectiveness of deep learning for medical image analysis. Although 3D self-supervised methods like volume contrast learning (VoCo) are powerful and partially address the labeling scarcity issue, their high computational cost and memory consumption are barriers. We propose 2D-VoCo, an efficient adaptation of the VoCo framework for slice-level self-supervised pre-training that learns spatial-semantic features from unlabeled 2D CT slices via contrastive learning. The pre-trained CNN backbone is then integrated into a CNN-LSTM architecture to classify multi-organ injuries. In the RSNA 2023 Abdominal Trauma dataset, 2D-VoCo pretraining significantly improves mAP, precision, recall, and RSNA score over training from scratch. Our framework provides a practical method to reduce the dependency on labeled data and enhance model performance in clinical CT analysis. We release the code for reproducibility. <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>1https://github.com/tkz05/2D-VoCo-CT-Classifier

title: Research on the Application of Russian Translation Based on Mel Spectrogram and Phonetic Translation in Russian Cultural Communication
authors: Lu An, Zhu Feng
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Aiming at the accuracy, efficiency and quality issues of Russian translation in cross-cultural communication, this study uses Mel spectrograms to extract and represent speech features, builds a translation model through a bidirectional long short-term memory network, and introduces an attention mechanism to improve it. A Russian translation model based on Mel spectrogram and speech translation is designed. The results show that in the LibriSpeech dataset, the average quality evaluation accuracy of the proposed model is 88.6%, and in the MUST-C dataset, the average quality evaluation accuracy of the proposed model is 85.5%, which is higher than the average quality accuracy of other models in the two datasets, proving that its translation quality is high. The Russian translation model proposed by the institute has superior performance and can effectively promote communication and integration between different cultures.

title: Beyond Dichotomization: Ordinal Prediction of Functional Stroke Outcomes from 4D CTP and Clinical Metadata Using Deep Learning
authors: Kimberly Amador, Anthony J. Winder, Jens Fiehler, Philip A. Barber, Matthias Wilms, Nils D. Forkert
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Predicting functional outcomes after acute ischemic stroke is critical for prognosis and treatment planning, as it could help identify patients most likely to benefit from interventions. These outcomes are typically measured by the 90-day modified Rankin Scale (mRS90), a seven-level disability score. However, most deep learning models for functional outcome prediction reduce this to a binary variable, losing information about intermediate recovery levels and limiting clinical interpretability. So far, only a few studies have attempted to model the full mRS90 range using multiclass classification, ignoring the ordered nature of the scale. To address this, we propose a multimodal deep learning model that predicts mRS90 from pre-treatment 4D computed tomography perfusion (CTP) imaging and clinical metadata using CORAL-based ordinal regression. Using data from 111 thrombectomy-treated patients, our results suggest that the proposed ordinal approach outperforms standard multiclass and linear regression models by better leveraging the ordered nature of stroke outcomes.

title: Multi-Method Explainability for Similarity Assessment in AI-Denoised PET Imaging
authors: Mélanie Champendal, Théo Coutaudier, Daphné Faist, Maeva Ryser, Josh Schaefferkoetter, Maurizio Conti, Mario Jreige, Adrien Depeursinge, Lluis Borras Ferris, Cláudia Sá dos Reis, John O. Prior, Henning Müller, Ricardo Ribeiro
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Deep learning-based denoising has enabled substantial reductions in radiotracer dose for PET imaging, yet the absence of full-dose ground truth presents challenges in evaluating image fidelity. This study proposes a supervised classification framework to assess the similarity between AI-denoised and standard-dose PET images without relying on ground truth. A total of 872 features, including radiomic descriptors, quantitative metrics, and visual quality assessments, were extracted at patient and lesion levels. Binary classifiers were trained to predict image similarity across six dose levels. Ground truth labels were derived from a structured visual scoring grid co-developed with expert users and validated against objective similarity metrics. Explainability was integrated using global and local SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), calibrated similarity scores, and textual feature descriptions. The most predictive features included anatomical detail, artefact presence and textural radiomics in the lungs (patient level), and SUV skewness, detection score, and texture strength (lesion level). The combined approach enables interpretable evaluation of AI-denoised PET reconstructions in settings where objective references are lacking, supporting transparency and clinical trust.

title: Comprehensive Control of Bipolar DC Solid-State Transformers Based on ISOP-QAB Converters
authors: Jonghun Yun, Seongmin Moon, Jaesang Yoo, Giovanni De Carne, Shenghui Cui
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a comprehensive control strategy for bipolar dc solid-state transformers (SSTs) based on input-series output-parallel (ISOP) quad active-bridge (QAB) converters. While existing works have addressed controls for SST in ISOP structure, or for QAB converters, individually, an integrated control scheme that harmonizes the entire system has remained unaddressed. The proposed strategy ensures both voltage and power balancing of the bipolar dc SST by leveraging a straightforward conversion of the power flows into phase-shift angles within each QAB module. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through experimental tests on an SST prototype with ±750 V / 36 kW ratings, composed of six QAB modules.

title: Weakly Supervised Counterfactual Spatio-Temporal Multiple Instance Learning for Inflammation Detection in Endoscopic Inflammatory Bowel Disease Assessment
authors: Bisi Bode Kolawole, Ujwala Chaudhari, Irene Zammarchi, Giovanni Santacroce, Rocio Del Amor, Pablo Meseguer, Cecilia Pugliano, Valery Naranjo, Subrata Ghosh, Marietta Iacucci, Enrico Grisan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) significantly impacts patients' quality of life, and accurate endoscopic assessment is critical for effective management. Conventional deep learning methods require frame-level annotations, which are labor-intensive and impractical for large video datasets. We propose a weakly supervised counterfactual spatio-temporal attention multiple instance learning (CFSpatioMIL) framework that captures spatial and temporal dependencies using only video-level labels. To enhance robustness and interpretability, we introduce a top-<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex> instance loss to encourage attention on multiple discriminative frames, and a counterfactual consistency mechanism that enforces stable predictions when highly attended frames are removed or replaced. We evaluate our approach on two multi-center IBD video datasets, training on one and externally validating on the other. CFSpatioMIL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art MIL baselines in F1 score and AUC, demonstrating its potential as a scalable approach for standardized inflammation assessment.

title: Physics-Infused Neural MPC of a DC-DC Boost Converter with Adaptive Transient Recovery and Enhanced Dynamic Stability
authors: Tahmin Mahmud
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: DC-DC boost converters require advanced control to ensure efficiency and stability under varying loads. Traditional model predictive control (MPC) and data-driven neural network methods face challenges such as high complexity and limited physical constraint enforcement. This paper proposes a hybrid physics-informed neural network (PINN) combined with finite control set MPC (FCS-MPC) for boost converters. The PINN embeds physical laws into neural training, providing accurate state predictions, while FCS-MPC ensures constraint satisfaction and multi-objective optimization. The method features adaptive transient recovery, explicit duty-ratio control, and enhanced dynamic stability. Experimental results on a commercial boost module demonstrate improved transient response, reduced voltage ripple, and robust operation across conduction modes. The proposed framework offers a computationally efficient, physically consistent solution for real-time control in power electronics.

title: Instantaneous Spectra Analysis of Pulse Series - Application to Lung Sounds with Abnormalities
authors: Fumihiko Ishiyama
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: The origin of the “theoretical limit of timefrequency resolution of Fourier analysis” is from its numerical implementation, especially from an assumption of “Periodic Boundary Condition (PBC),” which was introduced a century ago. We previously proposed to replace this condition with ‘Linear eXtrapolation Condition (LXC),” which does not require periodicity. This feature makes instantaneous spectra analysis of pulse series available, which replaces the short time Fourier transform (STFT). We applied the instantaneous spectra analysis to two lung sounds with abnormalities (crackles and wheezing) and to a normal lung sound, as a demonstration. Among them, crackles contains a random pulse series. The spectrum of each pulse is available, and the spectrogram of pulse series is available with assembling each spectrum. As a result, the time-frequency structure of given pulse series is visualized.

title: A Continuous and Interpretable Morphometric for Robust Quantification of Dynamic Biological Shapes
authors: Roua Rouatbi, Juan-Esteban Suarez Cardona, Alba Villaronga-Luque, Jesse V. Veenvliet, Ivo F. Sbalzarini
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We introduce the Push-Forward Signed Distance Morphometric (PF-SDM) for shape quantification in biomedical imaging. The PF-SDM compactly encodes geometric and topological properties of closed shapes, including their skeleton and symmetries. This provides robust and interpretable features for shape comparison and machine learning. The PF-SDM is mathematically smooth, providing access to gradients and differential-geometric quantities. It also extends to temporal dynamics and allows fusing spatial intensity distributions, such as genetic markers, with shape dynamics. We present the PF-SDM theory, benchmark it on synthetic data, and apply it to predicting body-axis formation in mouse gastruloids, outperforming a CNN baseline in both accuracy and speed.

title: DINOV3-Guided Cross Fusion Framework for Semantic-Aware CT Generation From MRI and CBCT
authors: Xianhao Zhou, Jianghao Wu, Ku Zhao, Jinlong He, Huangxuan Zhao, Lei Chen, Shaoting Zhang, Guotai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Generating synthetic CT images from CBCT or MRI has a potential for efficient radiation dose planning and adaptive radiotherapy. However, existing CNN-based models lack global semantic understanding, while Transformers often overfit small medical datasets due to high model capacity and weak inductive bias. To address these limitations, we propose a DINOv3-Guided Cross Fusion (DGCF) framework that integrates a frozen self-supervised DINOv3 Transformer with a trainable CNN encoder-decoder. It hierarchically fuses global representation of Transformer and local features of CNN via a learnable cross fusion module, achieving balanced local appearance and contextual representation. Furthermore, we introduce a Multi-Level DINOv3 Perceptual (MLDP) loss that encourages semantic similarity between synthetic CT and the ground truth in DINOv3's feature space. Experiments on the SynthRAD2023 pelvic dataset demonstrate that DGCF achieved state-of-the-art performance in terms of MS-SSIM, PSNR and segmentation-based metrics on both MRI → CT and CBCT → CT translation tasks. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to employ DINOv3 representations for medical image translation, highlighting the potential of self-supervised Transformer guidance for semantic-aware CT synthesis. The code is available at https://github.com/HiLabgit/DGCF.git.

title: Fuzzy Logic Control for Coordinated FES-Assisted Knee Swing with Powered Knee Orthoses
authors: Harith Firdaus Mustapha, Zakaria Hussain, Emilia Noorsal, Mohd Suhaimi Sulaiman
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Rehabilitation for lower limb mobility often relies on Functional Electrical Stimulation (FES) to get paralyzed or weak muscles firing again. But there is a massive drawback. Stimulated muscles fatigue incredibly fast. Plus, figuring out exactly how much electrical stimulation a muscle needs at any given second is notoriously difficult because the human neuromuscular system is highly non-linear and unpredictable. Integrated the exact hardware dynamics of a Faulhaber 3284 brushed DC motor. This meant factoring in real-world constraints like armature resistance, rotor inertia, and specific torque constants to see how the robotic joints would actually behave under a physical load.

title: EPSQA: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Structured Knowledge for Electric Power Safety Question Answering
authors: Xiang Yuan, Chutian Yu, Lijun Zhang, Junjie Sun, Qingxin Yan, Rui Pan, Xiao Yang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Electric power safety question answering demands both precision and domain expertise, yet existing systems often rely on unstructured documents that are difficult to maintain and prone to retrieval inconsistencies. We present EPSQA, a domain-specific Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that uniquely leverages structured electric power safety question banks curated by experts. EPSQA incorporates large language model–based keyword extraction and SQL-driven database retrieval to align query intent with relevant domain knowledge. By introducing adaptive matching strategies, EPSQA enables fine-grained control over retrieval precision and coverage. Evaluated on a real-world electric power safety dataset, EPSQA achieves a correct answer rate of approximately 57.44%, representing a relative improvement of about 72% over the unstructured baseline.

title: SiC-MOSFET/Si-IGBT Hybrid Dual-Side Auxiliary Edge Resonant Cells Soft-Switching PWM Inverter
authors: Tomokazu Mishima, Kazuki Sato, Shigehiro Yamamoto, Ching-Ming Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A soft switched PWM inverter topology with SiC-MOSFET/Si-IGBT hybridized resonant cells and its pulse patterns are explored in this paper. The dual-side active edge-resonant cells(DARC)-assisted auxiliary resonant commutation pole (ARCP) is the topological feature where no large chemical capacitors are demanded, which contributes for a high power density and long life cycle dc-ac inverter applications. The basic and extended pulse patterns are comparatively studied in terms of reactive power and recycling processes in the ARCP, whereby power conversion efficiency and commutation are theoretically revealed. Performances and practical effectiveness of the newly-developed soft switching half-bridge PWM inverter with 40 kHz SiC/Si power transistors hybrid implementation are demonstrated by experiments with a mini-scale prototype, and compared with the hard switching and RCD snubber PWM inverters.

title: Analytical Modeling of Common-Mode and Differential-Mode Voltage Spectra for a Two-Level, Three-Phase Inverter
authors: Hassan Abdallah, Ryan Olson, Tianchen Li, Robert M. Cuzner
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The adoption of wide-bandgap (WBG) devices has improved the power density and enabled higher switching frequencies of converters, but these advantages come with elevated electromagnetic emissions due to fast voltage transitions and parasitic interactions. This paper introduces an analytical prediction approach for electromagnetic interference (EMI) spectra of a two-level, three-phase inverter systems. The proposed approach focuses on synthesizing the conducted emission spectra by capturing the effects of dominating parasitics and the modulation-dependent switching behavior to obtain high frequency accuracy within the models. Common-mode (CM) and differential-mode (DM) voltage spectrum models are developed using refined waveform synthesis and equivalent circuit transformations. Hardware measurements are used to confirm the accuracy of the predicted spectra, demonstrating that the proposed method is capable of enabling early-stage EMI filter synthesis without the need for iterative hardware prototyping.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Workforce Decision-Making in Enterprise Systems
authors: Monu Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is now becoming a more popular option with regards to decision making related to workforce, such as talent acquisition, workforce employee retention prediction, performance assessment, and workforce planning. Nevertheless, the intensive usage of black-box models has triggered alarm among the stakeholders of the enterprise in the areas of transparency, trust, fairness, and compliance with regulations. This paper will discuss how Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) can be used to overcome these problems in workforce prediction systems. A explainable machine learning system is built based on SHAP and local interpretability methods to offer significant results about the real-world workforce information, both on the global and features levels. According to the experimental findings, explainability is a key factor that enhances the level of expert trust in model outputs; in fact, the level of trust went up by more than 35 percent in contrast to non-explainable methods. Moreover, the suggested framework makes it possible to conduct successful human-AI interaction with the domain experts being able to examine and verify predictive results, with explainability being the key to responsible enterprise AI implementation.

title: Design and Optimization of a PI-Controlled Forward-Flyback Converter Using Matlab Simulation
authors: Prerna Bhingare, Girish K. Mahajan, Nilesh S. Wani, Rohini More, Kalyani Chavan, Ashwini J. Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This paper presents a PI-Controlled based forward-fly back converter designed for LED driver applications. The operating principle of the proposed converter is analyzed and experimentally validated using a 24 W laboratory prototype. The converter is tested with and without a balancing capacitor. This shows that adding the balancing capacitor makes it possible for stable forward and flyback operation over the whole input voltage range. As a result, the magnetizing offset current is much lower, which means less energy is lost in the core, and the transformer takes up less space. The prototype is evaluated under pulse-generator (PG) control and proportional-integral (PI) control, with both approaches producing similar primary-current and outputcurrent waveforms. However, the magnetizing current is notably lower when PI control is applied. The results show that the proposed converter does a better job of suppressing magnetizing current and transferring power throughout the switching cycle. It has less core loss, works more efficiently, and works reliably with different input conditions. Simulation results show an efficiency of 92.4 %, power factor of 0.98, and total harmonic distortion (THD) of 6.5% under rated conditions. The results confirm that PI control provides better dynamic response and stability compared to the Pulse Generator method. The suggested balanced forward-flyback converter is a great choice for LED driver applications that need a lot of power.

title: 3D DoseGLA: Gated Linear Attention with Wavelet-Domain Constraint for Efficient 3D Radiotherapy Dose Prediction
authors: Ku Zhao, Xianhao Zhou, Wenjun Liao, Shichuan Zhang, Shaoting Zhang, Guotai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Dose calculation, as the core of radiotherapy, remains a timeconsuming and experience-dependent process. While deep learning methods have been proposed to improve efficiency, they often face challenges in global modeling and computational complexity, especially with 3D data, limiting their clinical applicability. Moreover, their predicted dose maps are often over-smoothed due to pixel-wise loss functions such as L1 loss that ignore inter-pixel relationship. To address these issues, we propose an efficient framework DoseGLA for 3D dose prediction. First, we propose a Gated Linear Attention-based UNet (GLAUNet) that incorporates an improved gating mechanism inspired by Mamba into linear attention to enhance global modeling capacity while retaining linear computational complexity. Second, a Wavelet-domain Constraint Loss (WDCL) is proposed to guide the model in learning inter-pixel relationship with better prediction contrasts in frequency domain. Experiments on the OpenKBP dataset show that our framework significantly outperforms the state-of-theart model with around 80% fewer parameters, achieving a Dose Score of 2.33 and a DVH Score of 1.46.

title: Direct Power Control for the Battery Port of a Multisource Inverter
authors: Chang Liu, Bing Li, Guozhu Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The multisource inverter is promising in integrating batteries with renewable energy sources, as the DC-DC converter normally required for battery power control can be eliminated. This single-stage conversion structure can reduce system cost and improve overall efficiency. This paper proposes a direct power control strategy for the battery connected to a multisource inverter. The proposed control strategy is directly implemented within the modulation process. A zero-loop-width hysteresis controller is employed to regulate the battery current by injecting an appropriate positive or negative zero-sequence signal. The method eliminates the need for control parameter design and features simple implementation and low computational burden, making it promising for practical applications. Steady-state and dynamic experimental results under different degrees of capacitor voltage imbalance demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: A Data-driven Loss Model of GaN-HEMT-based Synchronous Buck Converter with Critical Operating Frequency
authors: Subhradip Mukherjee, Mahesh K. Mishra, N. Lakshminarasamma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Wide bandgap (WBG) semiconductor devices such as gallium nitride (GaN) high electron mobility transistors (HEMTs) have significantly advanced power electronics by enabling higher switching frequencies, compact form factors, greater power density, and improved efficiency. Despite these advantages, accurately modeling losses in GaN-based converters remains a key challenge, especially under varying operating conditions. This paper presents a data-driven loss modeling approach for a GaN HEMT-based buck converter, combining empirical simulation data with theoretical analysis to estimate both fixed and variable losses across a range of switching frequencies and load levels. Curve-fitting techniques are employed to develop analytical loss functions for key parameters, enabling precise prediction of total power loss. The model also facilitates analysis of the trade-offs among switching frequency, efficiency, and thermal performance. Based on this analysis, an optimal switching frequency is identified that minimizes total power loss while maintaining thermal and operational reliability.

title: End-to-End Optimization of Sparse Ultrasound Linear Probes
authors: Sergio Urrea, Adrian Basarab, Hervé Liebgott, Henry Arguello
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging faces a trade-off between image quality and hardware complexity caused by dense transducers. Sparse arrays are one popular solution to mitigate this challenge. This work proposes an end-to-end optimization framework that jointly learns sparse array configuration and image reconstruction. The framework integrates a differentiable Image Formation Model with a HARD Straight Thought Estimator (STE) selection mask, unrolled Iterative SoftThresholding Algorithm (ISTA) deconvolution, and a residual Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The objective combines physical consistency (Point Spread Function (PSF) and convolutional formation model) with structural fidelity (contrast, Side-Lobe-Ratio (SLR), entropy, and row diversity). Simulations using a 3.5 MHz probe show that the learned configuration preserves axial and lateral resolution with half of the active elements. This physics-guided, data-driven approach enables compact, cost-efficient ultrasound probe design without sacrificing image quality, and it is expandable to 3-D volumetric imaging.

title: Lasagna-U-Net: Revisiting U-Shape Architecture for Histopathology Image Segmentation
authors: Junlai Qiu, Yunzhu Chen, Jiaqi Zhao, Yawen Huang, Fubo Wang, Yuexiang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Histopathology images serve as the gold standard for clinical diagnosis, making their accurate analysis essential in medical practice. Although U-Net and its variants have been widely applied to histopathology image segmentation, most fail to effectively address the challenge of multi-scale lesion representation. In this paper, we propose Lasagna-U-Net, a novel U-Net variant that integrates convolutional neural network (CNN) and vision Transformer (ViT) within a unified U-shaped architecture. Two key modules-the multi-scale grouped convolution module and the grouped residual Transformer module-jointly enhance the model's capacity for local feature extraction and long-range dependency modeling. Evaluations on the public GlaS and KPIs datasets demonstrate that Lasagna-U-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance, particularly in segmenting lesions of varying sizes (https://github.com/Q4CS/Lasagna-U-Net).

title: SFTD-Net: Structure-Aware Frequency and Sparse Attention Network for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Tianqi Huang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation plays a crucial role in computer-aided diagnosis for ophthalmic and cardiovascular diseases. However, accurate segmentation remains challenging due to complex backgrounds, low contrast, and large vessel-scale variations. In addition, conventional encoder-decoder networks are limited in local feature modeling, skip-feature fusion, and detail preservation during reconstruction. To tackle these issues, we present a structure-aware frequency and sparse attention network (SFTD-Net) for retinal vessel segmentation. SFTD-Net follows an encoder-decoder architecture with three modules. A PConv-Conv Attention Module (PCAM) is inserted in the encoder to strengthen multi-scale feature representation. An In-dex-Selected Sparse Attention module (ISSA) is integrated into the skip pathways to selectively transmit encoder features and suppress redundant background noise. A Dual-Branch Refinement Module (DBRM) is introduced before decoder upsampling and fusion to restore structural details and suppress texture interference. Results on the Digital Retinal Images for Vessel Extraction (DRIVE) and Child Heart and Health Study in England database 1 (CHASE_DB1) datasets further demonstrate the robustness of SFTD-Net in retinal vessel segmentation. On DRIVE, the proposed method achieves an accuracy (ACC) of 0.9698, an F1-score (F1) of 0.8236, and an area under the ROC curve (AUC) of 0.9863. It also obtains competitive performance on CHASE_DB1. These results highlight the potential of SFTD-Net for computer vision, advanced medical imaging, and intelligent diagnosis tasks

title: Selective Frame Processing for Real-time Marine Acoustic Monitoring using Progressive Multi-Stage MFCC Computation
authors: N Kirubakaran, D Mohana Priya, K Sivasakthi, V Anitha, G Senthil Kumar, Vishali Muralidharan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Anthropogenic activities pose a threat to marine ecosystems, demanding an adequate cetacean population monitoring system for conservation purposes. This paper implements an approach on creating a multistage framework for calculating Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficient (MFCC) to extract the audio features. At first, the cetacean audio is divided into respective frames of equal length. Each frame is then compared with the energy threshold and if it exceeds, the MFCC is calculated. If the frequency threshold is also met, then the higher level MFCC is calculated to provide more accurate characteristics. The thresholds of both energy and frequency are adaptive and depends on the level of background noise. This approach is more optimized as it ignores the frames that does not meet the threshold needs, making it suitable for real-time computations. Our suggested model increases accuracy to $\mathbf{9 0 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{9 5 \%}$, while traditional MFCC models only attain an accuracy of $\mathbf{7 5 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{8 0 \%}$ and frequently struggle with irrelevant frames and background noise. As the MFCC characteristics are only calculated for pertinent frames, the suggested model reduces processing time by 25% to 30%. Our model performs exceptionally well by segmenting audio based on energy and frequency thresholds, retaining high precision compared to standard model.

title: Hyperspectral Band Selection via Spectral-Spatial Embedding and Graph Regularization
authors: Mingqing Liu, Jingyu Wang, Shengzhao Guo, Xinru Zhang, Pengfei Huang, Zhenyu Ma
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Band selection is an important technique to extract crucial features and reduce redundancy in hyperspectral images (HSI). Hyperspectral images inherently exhibit correlations in both spatial and spectral domains. However, most existing band selection methods fail to jointly leverage these complementary characteristics for enhanced feature representation. Hence, in this work, we propose a novel Spectral-Spatial Embedding and Graph Regularization (SSEGR) band selection method. First, a spectral-spatial embedding module is introduced to improve the ability to simultaneously explore spectral and spatial correlations of self-representation learning, from which a sparse coefficient matrix can be obtained. Then, to maintain the structure smoothness of the learned coefficient matrix, a graph regularization term is employed to preserve the intrinsic similarity relationships within HSI data. The experiments on benchmark HSI datasets demonstrate the feasibility of our proposed SSEGR method.

title: Hybrid-Projection with Subspace Recycling for Sequential and Limited-Angle CT: A Training-Free Baseline for Biomedical Imaging
authors: Honghui Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Modern CT workflows in biomedical imaging increasingly involve sequences of closely related reconstruction tasks: repeated exams, multi-slice stacks, dynamic series, and geometry-constrained limited-angle acquisitions. While deep learning and unrolled variational networks have shown strong performance for sparse-view and low-dose CT, they rely on training data, protocol-specific tuning, and careful safeguards for robustness, which motivates fast, interpretable, training-free baselines that exploit sequential structure. We study hybrid-projection-based reconstruction with subspace recycling for 2D parallel-beam CT. Building on existing HyBR/HyBRrecycle solvers, we reuse Krylov subspaces across systems with shared geometry and couple this with practical parameterchoice rules: discrepancy principles with known or estimated noise, and stabilized weighted GCV. Using a unified testbed with standard phantoms, full-view and missing-angle geometries, and additive noise, we (i) instantiate a CT-oriented hybrid projection and recycling framework (HyBR-Rec) tailored to sequential and limitedangle settings, and (ii) systematically compare it against filtered backprojection (FBP) and a TV-regularized baseline. Experiments on representative sequential and limited-angle scenarios show that subspace recycling consistently accelerates convergence: in our tests, time-to-best is reduced by about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15-22 \%$</tex> while relative errors are preserved or increase by only <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 3 \%$</tex> in the challenging missing-angle case. The resulting pipeline is training-free, lightweight to implement, and provides a strong, reproducible reference for evaluating learned and model-based CT reconstruction methods in biomedical imaging.

title: Temporal Evolving Graph Neural Networks for Intrusion Detection in Large- Scale IoT Networks
authors: Bommy M, Pandipathi Thilak, Shaik Hussain Basha, Baigari Vamsi Krishna, Malathi Vikas Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Cyber-attacks in large-scale IoT networks exhibit time-evolving behavior, where attack patterns change progressively across multiple time intervals. This temporal evolution poses significant challenges for conventional intrusion detection systems that rely on static traffic representations. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a temporal graph-based intrusion detection framework that explicitly models the evolution of network behavior over time. Network flows from the TON-IoT telemetry dataset are pre-processed and transformed into a sequence of temporal graph snapshots, where nodes represent traffic instances and edges encode temporal continuity. A Temporal Evolving Graph Neural Network (TE-GNN), is employed to adaptively learn evolving attack patterns by updating graph representations across successive snapshots. The proposed approach supports intrusion detection at both graph and node levels, to identify attack-dominant time periods and behavioral interpretation. Experimental evaluation across 58 temporal snapshots demonstrates that temporal graph-level learning provides stable and reliable intrusion detection under highly imbalanced conditions. Node-level performance highlights the inherent difficulty of precise attack localization during peak intrusion phases. Thus, temporal visualization of attack progression offers interpretable insights into slow-staged and multi-phase attacks, supporting effective monitoring and decision-making in dynamic IoT environments.

title: A High-Frequency Planar Transformer with Medium-Voltage Isolation and Embedded Thermal Management
authors: Shivangi Sinha, Zayed Ahmed, Jeffery Diebold, Dragan Maksimović
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents the analysis and design of a compact high-frequency planar transformer with medium-voltage (MV) isolation, suitable for a wide range of modular MVAC to DC architectures. The transformer uses high-voltage PCB windings fully enveloped by low-voltage windings in a modified 12-layer stack-up with embedded polyimide layers. Thermal management components provide high conductivity to an enclosure, and the entire assembly is potted with high-dielectric-strength epoxy. The transformer enclosure, including thermal management components, achieves a power density of 4.6kW/l. It is used in a 10kW, 800–960V dual-active-bridge converter operating at 200 kHz with 98.2% measured efficiency at 6kW and 96.5% measured efficiency at full load power of 10.5kW. A peak temperature rise of 20°C is recorded within the core window at full load. A hipot tested isolation of 26 kV is achieved between the primary and secondary windings of the proposed transformer assembly.

title: Synthesizing Brain Magnetic Resonance Images for Myelin Assessment
authors: Nikhil N. Chaudhari, Nahian F. Chowdhury, Emily Dennis, Elisabeth Wilde, Andrei Irimia
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
