Consequently, accurate junction-temperature estimation is essential, and electro-thermal modeling is widely applied on the basis of the power-device loss model and the thermal impedance of the module packaging and cooling system. Previous studies have focused on developing various models to improve accuracy, while few studies emphasize computation efficiency, which is equally important considering reliability assessment with a yearlong mission profile. This paper proposes a computationally efficient algorithm to compute the junction temperature of the power module with transient ripple components. First, a decoupled framework is introduced where the loss model, dependent only on junction temperature, is separated from the thermal model, eliminating the need for co-simulation. Second, an algorithm to decouple the average temperature through a one-dimensional thermal-resistance model and the ripple component through a thermal-impedance model, along with an adaptive simulation-time-resolution setting is proposed. Finally, a case study of a T-type power module for solar applications demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method, achieving a normalized root mean square error of 1.51% and a simulation time speedup of 700 ×.

title: Pretext Task Adversarial Learning for Unpaired Low-Field to Ultra High-Field MRI Synthesis
authors: Zhenxuan Zhang, Peiyuan Jing, Zi Wang, Ula Briski, Coraline Beitone, Yue Yang, Yinzhe Wu, Fanwen Wang, Liutao Yang, Jiahao Huang, Zhifan Gao, Guang Yang, Peter J. Lally
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Given the scarcity and high cost of high-field MRI, synthesizing high-field images from low-field MRI offers great potential for augmenting limited data in downstream tasks (e.g., segmentation). Low-field MRI typically suffers from low SNR and spatial resolution, making cross-domain synthesis challenging due to feature misalignment and anatomical distortion. To address this, we propose a Pretext Task Adversarial (PTA) framework for high-field MRI synthesis. It consists of: (1) a Slice-wise Gap Perception (SGP) network for aligning inter-slice inconsistencies via contrastive learning; (2) a Local Structure Correction (LSC) network for refining local details from rotated and masked inputs; and (3) a pretext-guided adversarial training that enhances realism through additional supervision and discrimination. Experiments on low-to-ultra-high-field MRI demonstrate state-of-the-art performance (FID =16.892, IS =1.933, MS-SSIM =0.324), generating anatomically consistent high-field-like data to improve downstream training. The code is available at: https://github.com/Zhenxuan-Zhang/PTA4Unpaired_HF_MRI_SYN.

title: LNO piezoelectric resonator temperature characteristic
authors: Alex Marques, Martin Lott, Alexandre Rheinhardt, Marie Bousquet, Gaëtan Perez, Ghislain Despesse
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: One of today’s challenges in the field of power electronics is to increase power-density, which is mainly achieved by increasing the switching frequency. New semiconductor technologies, such as GaN HEMT devices, allow us to increase the switching frequency and achieved greater power-density. Nevertheless, limits are reached with passive materials such as inductors. In this context, piezoelectric resonators (PR) are used to offer an interesting alternative compare to inductor beyond MHz switching frequency. Nevertheless, there is a lack of information on the practical limits in terms of the maximum current that Lithium Niobate (LNO) PRs can withstand in steady state with and without air cooling and on their frequency shift under self-heating induced by current. This paper aims to provide insights on the temperature-dependent shift in the characteristics of LNO resonators and to quantify the current limit of PRs under various practical operating conditions.

title: Optimizing Usability and Security: A Study of Multi-Factor Authentication in Academic Environments
authors: Ahmad Shalaldeh, Seyed Hosseini, Shahbaz Pervez, Ayman Alnsour
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The educational platforms today are exposed to personal information of students and lecturers, rendering them victims of different cyber-attacks that include phishing, dictionary attacks, and brute-force attacks. The conventional authentication that had only included the use of passwords was no longer adequate to offer adequate security. Meanwhile, overly complex security systems may decrease usability levels and cause users to abandon the system. The objective of the study is to prepare an authentication prototype of e-learning platforms, which will incorporate maximum security, as well as the comfort of the user. The solution proposed will contain easy to use password policy with only slight length condition, password salting and hash using of Argon2id algorithm and adaptive multi-factor authentication (MFA) with choice between email, SMS or Google Authenticator. The system was tested in terms of security and usability by applying the John the Ripper tool to imitate a dictionary attack, the heuristic rules created by Nielsen, and the NIST SP 800-63B guidelines. The findings demonstrate that the most basic passwords (e.g., qwerty or password123) are still susceptible, but the extra protection can be ensured by MFA to secure the system. The prototype also meets 17 out of 20 recommendations provided by NIST and all 10 usability heuristics. These results prove that a balance between the security and usability of academic platforms is possible. The given prototype serves as a viable demonstration of how the current cryptography solutions and user-focused design can be implemented in the form of a practical educational authentication system.

title: Data-Driven Prediction and Explainability of Hybrid Nanofluid Density for Thermal Engineering Applications
authors: Sakthi K, Ramya V, Mahalakshmi A, Keerthiga G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Hybrid nanofluids have gained significant attention as advanced heat transfer fluids due to their enhanced thermophysical properties compared to conventional fluids and singleparticle nanofluids. Accurate prediction of hybrid nanofluid density is crucial for the design, optimization, and safe operation of thermal systems such as heat exchangers, solar thermal collectors, electronic cooling devices, and automotive cooling systems. However, experimental determination of density is often costly, time-consuming, and limited to specific operating conditions, highlighting the need for reliable data-driven predictive approaches. In this study, an explainable machine learning and deep learning framework is developed to predict hybrid nanofluid density using a dataset of 436 samples collected from peer-reviewed literature. Key physical and thermophysical parameters, including nanoparticle type, base fluid type, temperature, nanoparticle volume concentration, base fluid density, densities of primary and secondary nanoparticles, and volume mixture ratios, are considered. Four predictive models—Random Forest, XGBoost, CatBoost, and an Artificial Neural Network (ANN)—are evaluated using R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, RMSE, and MAE metrics. Ensemble-based tree models, particularly XGBoost and CatBoost, achieve superior accuracy with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> values exceeding 0.99. Explainable artificial intelligence techniques, namely SHAP and permutation importance, are employed to identify influential features, revealing base fluid density, nanoparticle volume concentration, and temperature as the dominant factors. The proposed framework provides accurate, interpretable, and physically consistent predictions for hybrid nanofluid density estimation.

title: Shared-Weight Segmentation with Mask-Conditioned Risk Classification for Carotid Ultrasound
authors: Yujie Sun, Zexuan Fan, Haoyu Wu, Xiaowu Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stroke is a leading cause of mortality and long-term disability worldwide, with carotid atherosclerosis serving as a major contributor to ischemic events. Carotid ultrasound is routinely used for plaque morphology assessment. However, automated plaque risk grading remains challenging due to small plaque size, low image contrast, and indistinct plaque-lumen boundaries. Moreover, longitudinal and transverse views provide complementary anatomical information that requires coherent cross-view reasoning, which is insufficiently leveraged in the existing automated pipelines. To address these challenges, we propose a two-stage, two-view framework that integrates semi-supervised plaque and vessel segmentation with mask-informed risk classification. The model is trained using 200 labeled cases and 800 unlabeled cases. On the challenge evaluation, our method achieves an F1-Score of 65.50 and a Seg-Score of 62.99, outperforming the official baseline (F1 33.96, Seg-Score 54.71) by 31.54 and 8.28, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of integrating multi-view anatomical priors with semi-supervised learning for the precise grading of carotid atherosclerotic risk. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/06Yujie/CSV2026_Challenge.

title: Seamless Power Transfer of Bidirectional Converters in Microgrids Applications
authors: Emanuel Serban, Cristian Lascu
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Power converters utilizing bidirectional topologies are essential for maintaining continuous microgrid operation and ensuring reliable power delivery to both DC and AC loads. This paper introduces a hybrid AC–DC microgrid architecture that delivers reliable power to various loads while providing enhanced operational flexibility and robust resilience under dynamic and fault conditions. The design and inclusion of a bidirectional DC-DC converter provides critical functionalities such as prioritization of essential loads (e.g., life-support systems), extension of the DC voltage operating range, and galvanic isolation.During dynamic microgrid’s operating conditions, the high-frequency transformer within the DC–DC converter can exhibit increased sensitivity to pulsewidth modulation (PWM) mismatches, saturation and delayed response in power transfer detection. To address these challenges, a microgrid controller is proposed and integrated within the DC–DC converter to enable seamless power transfer during charge and discharge transitions. The proposed communication-free control strategy employs a voltage gradient detection method for rapid event identification and smooth bidirectional power transfer. Each module within the DC microgrid autonomously monitors local parameters and, based on positive or negative voltage gradient variations, determines its operating mode accordingly. The effectiveness of the proposed strategy is validated through experimental laboratory studies conducted under dynamic charging and discharging scenarios.

title: AI-Driven Clinical Decision Support for Laser Tattoo Removal: A Review and Framework
authors: Sarah Abdul Zahra Kadim, Omar Nameer Mohammed Salim, Ghaidaa A. Khalid, Esraa M. Hashem
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Laser removal of tattoos is a complex clinical protocol affected by several interacting parameters, such as pigment composition, skin phototype, laser parameter choice and inter-individual variability in the immune clearance process. It might be that clinically-oriented “one-size-fits-all” dosing guidelines are not sensitive enough to make sure that all patients with rare diseases experience a similarly safe and efficient therapy. With recent developments in the field of artificial intelligence (AI), better choice of parameters, better prediction and management of outcomes and complication especially post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation are possible. This survey specifically covers the AI-assisted applications that are related to laser tattoo removal, including robotic and automated designs for intervention delivery, sensor-based strategies to optimize parameters, predictive methods to estimate treatment outcomes, and physics-informed simulations. This need for a hybrid CDSS system is based on the shortcomings reported in previous work such as fragmented data sets and restricted generalization over various phototypes and populations, insipid integration into clinical workflow. As a result, it is proposed to synthesize a conceptual hybrid model in which predictive AI modules are combined with safety guardrails (rule-based) as well as explain ability outputs to ensure safer, personalized treatment planning. Future lines include implementing standardized artificially annotated databases, external validation and enhanced clinical alignment for integration of real life setting in dermatology practice.

title: Non-Active Power Control of Single Phase Dual Active Bridge for improved performance
authors: Amrithesh Kumar, Jayakumar P
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Dual Active Bridges (DABs) are widely used in various power electronics applications. Controlling the nonactive power associated with active power flow will be highly beneficial for the effective utilization of the converter. In this paper, strategies for managing the inductor current and nonactive power in a single-phase DAB are attempted. Non-active power flow control using single phase shift (SPS) modulation, dual phase shift (DPS) modulation, were presented. The triple phase shift (TPS) modulation technique for the control of active and non- active power without compromising efficiency or increasing system complexity is found to be more beneficial.

title: A 6.78-MHz kW-Level 15-cm Air-Gap Misalignment-Tolerant Capacitive Wireless Power Transfer System for EV Charging Employing Roadway-Vehicle DC-DC Converters
authors: Asif Mushtaq Bhat, Ayush Dixit, Sreyam Sinha
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In capacitive wireless power transfer (CWPT) systems for EV charging, coupling variations arising from roadway-to-vehicle coupler misalignment or varying vehicle ground clearance leads to significant change in output power and increased losses. This paper introduces a roadway-vehicle DC-DC converter (RVDC) based CWPT system that mitigates this issue by using two DC-DC converters, one on the roadway-side and one on the vehicle-side. These converters control the input and output DC link voltages of the system during coupling variations to maintain a constant output power while ensuring a resistive loading of the inverter and thereby reducing losses. A comprehensive design methodology for the proposed RVDC based CWPT system is presented. A 6.78-MHz, 15-cm air-gap, 2.2-kW RVDC-based CWPT prototype suitable for EV charging is designed and built. Experimental results with 1-kW output power demonstrate that the inverter loading phase and output power remain constant for up to 50% lateral misalignment between roadway and vehicle-side coupler plates.

title: FreshNet 360: A Hybrid Clustering-Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Stage Food Freshness Grading Using Image-Based Quality Signatures
authors: Sampurna Roy, Tanupriya Choudhury, S Christalin Nelson, Minakshi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Maintaining the freshness of perishable food products is a very imperative issue in the field of retail, supplychain management as well as consumer safety. The conventional methods of freshness determination also largely depend on manual examination which is subjective, inconsistent and laborious. To obtain multi-stage food freshness grading, this paper suggests FreshNet 360, a unified, hybrid based machine learning architecture that combines both unsupervised clustering and deep machine learning. An image pipeline was designed with a systematic order that consisted of noise removal, grayscale conversion, edge detection, and generalized augmentation which produced a solid dataset of various types of fruits and vegetables. To obtain natural groupings in the data, three clustering algorithms (K Means, Agglomerative Hierarchical Clustering and DBSCAN) were used and the results were mapped onto five freshness levels Fresh, Slightly Aged, Stale, Spoiled and Rotten. Eight deep leaning models, VGG16, VGG19, MobileNetV2, ResNet50, DenseNet121, InceptionV3, Xception, and a custom AlexNet were trained and benchmarked with these labels. Experimental results demonstrate that MobileNetV2 achieves the highest performance with K-Means clustered data (accuracy: 0.77), while AlexNet excels with Agglomerative clustering (accuracy: 0.84). The study highlights the effectiveness of clustering-enhanced label generation in improving classifier stability, enabling scalable, reliable, and automated food freshness detection for realworld applications. The full link to the github repo of this is https://github.com/sampurna7373/FreshNet-360.

title: Submodule-Level Decentralized Control for Cascaded Quadruple Active Bridge-Based Medium-Voltage Converters
authors: Bowen Yang, Vikram Roy Chowdhury, Soham Dutta, Satish Tadala, Manish Tathode, Brian Johnson, Luca Corradini, Dragan Maksimovic, Gab-Su Seo
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: High-power, low-voltage DC (LVDC) loads, such as AI data centers, electric vehicle fast-charging hubs, and hydrogen electrolyzers, are accelerating the deployment of solid-state transformers (SSTs) that directly interface medium-voltage AC (MVAC) to LVDC, streamlining power delivery. This paper proposes a submodule-level decentralized control strategy for an SST comprising a cascaded H-bridge (CHB) active rectifier on the MVAC side and per-module quadruple-active-bridge (QAB) converters on the LVDC side with local controllers. The proposed strategy eliminates the need for high-fidelity, real-time cross-isolation communication by synchronizing all active bridges to a common heartbeat signal and enabling the primary- and secondary-side controllers to generate independent local control signals (phase shifts). The resultant phase shifts naturally govern average and double-line frequency power transfer between active bridges, enabling each side to meet its control objectives using only local measurements. Detailed controller design considerations are presented based on a stability analysis. Comprehensive electromagnetic-transient simulations demonstrate tight DC-link voltage regulation, high-quality grid currents, and robust operation under load transients and grid disturbances, without real-time communication among the submodule controllers. These results indicate that the proposed scheme can significantly enhance the reliability, scalability, and flexibility of cascaded bridge-based medium-voltage converters.

title: A Calibrated Memorization Index (MI) for Detecting Training Data Leakage in Generative MRI Models
authors: Yash Deo, Yan Jia, Toni Lassila, Victoria J Hodge, Alejandro F Frangi, Chenghao Qian, Siyuan Kang, Ibrahim Habli
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Image generative models are known to duplicate images from the training data as part of their outputs, which can lead to privacy concerns when used for medical image generation. We propose a calibrated per-sample metric for detecting memorization and duplication of training data. Our metric uses image features extracted using an MRI foundation model, aggregates multi-layer whitened nearest-neighbor similarities, and maps them to a bounded Overfit/Novelty Index (ONI) and Memorization Index (MI) scores. Across three MRI datasets with controlled duplication percentages and typical image augmentations, our metric robustly detects duplication and provides more consistent metric values across datasets. At the sample level, our metric achieves near-perfect detection of duplicates.

title: Follow Your Heart: Landmark-Guided Transducer Pose Scoring for Point-of-Care Echocardiography
authors: Zaiyang Guo, Jessie N. Dong, Filippos Bellos, Jilei Hao, Emily J. MacKay, Trevor Chan, Shir Goldfinger, Sethu Reddy, Steven Vance, Jason J. Corso, Alison M. Pouch
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Point-of-care transthoracic echocardiography (TTE) makes it possible to assess a patient's cardiac function in almost any setting. A critical step in the TTE exam is acquisition of the apical 4-chamber (A4CH) view, which is used to evaluate clinically impactful measurements such as left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF). However, optimizing transducer pose for high-quality image acquisition and subsequent measurement is a challenging task, particularly for novice users. In this work, we present a multi-task network that provides feedback cues for A4CH view acquisition and automatically estimates LVEF in high-quality A4CH images. The network cascades a transducer pose scoring module and an uncertainty-aware LV landmark detector with automated LVEF estimation. A strength is that network training and inference do not require cumbersome or costly setups for transducer position tracking. We evaluate performance on point-of-care TTE data acquired with a spatially dense “sweep” protocol around the optimal A4CH view. The results demonstrate the network's ability to determine when the transducer pose is on target, close to target, or far from target based on the images alone, while generating visual landmark cues that guide anatomical interpretation and orientation. In conclusion, we demonstrate a promising strategy to provide guidance for A 4 CH view acquisition, which may be useful when deploying point-of-care TTE in limited resource settings.

title: Design and Simulation of Regenerative Braking Strategy for BLDC Motor with Buck Converter
authors: Veeraiah. Nagulla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: Regenerative braking act as a pivotal role in enhancing the efficiency of electric vehicles (EVs) by recovering the kinetic energy otherwise lost during conventional braking and redirect it back to the battery. This paper presents a detailed simulation study of regenerative braking in a Brushless DC (BLDC) motor-driven electric vehicle using MATLAB/Simulink. The model integrates a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500 ~\mathrm{W}, 48 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> BLDC motor, a six-cell battery pack rated at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 0 ~ A h}$</tex>, and a voltage source inverter who is governed by hall sensor-based commutation logic. A control approach combining PI speed regulation and braking logic ensure seamless transition between motoring and regenerative modes. During normal operation, the motor speed sustained at 3000 RPM under a reference command. At braking onset, the motor operates as a generator, with the back electromotive force (EMF) exceeding the terminal voltage, thereby channel current back into the battery. The simulation results shows that battery current become negative during braking, confirming the recharging process, while the state of charge (SOC) and terminal voltage increases correspondingly. Torque reversal in the BLDC motor further validate energy recovery. This study confirms the feasibility of integrating regenerative braking in BLDC motor-driven EVs, providing more energy efficiency, extended driving range, and reduced dependency on external charging. The proposed framework also highlights the significance of efficient control logic for practical EV application.

title: Enhancing Mammogram-Based Breast Cancer Prediction From Pretrained Vision-Language Models: the Role of Soft Prompts and Bidirectional Fusion
authors: Fareeha Sarwar, Nuno Miguel De Figueiredo Garrido, Margarida Silveira
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) such as CLIP and BLIP have demonstrated strong generalization in visual reasoning tasks. However, their potential for medical image analysis, especially breast cancer prediction from mammograms, remains underexplored. This study investigates how a pretrained VLM can be adapted for full mammographic classification. Unlike prior approaches that rely on costly region-of-interest (ROI) annotations, we process entire mammograms and adapt a general-purpose VLM (EVACLIP) using soft prompts, selective fine-tuning, and bidirectional fusion strategies. We compare different fusion methods, including Concatenation, Gated-Residual, Cross-Modal, Co-Weighted and Bi-Attention. Experiments on the CBISDDSM dataset show that bidirectional fusion methods consistently outperform other fusion approaches, while providing enhanced explainability through improved attention localization. Results also demonstrate that our adapted generalpurpose VLM significantly outperforms a mammographyspecific model (Mammo-CLIP), under domain-shift, in both zero-shot and linear-probe settings. This suggests that largescale general-purpose VLMs, when properly adapted, can outperform domain-specific models, reducing the need for extensive annotation and paired image-text training.

title: A Novel 3L-SNPC Power Module for EV Motor Drives
authors: Siqi Dai, Shuangxi Zhu, Jianwei Lv, Suhang Wei, Xuchen Sun, Cai Chen, Yong Kang, Wei Jiang, Pengyang Wang, Bin Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: SNPC(Simplified Neutral Point Clamped) topology as a promising three-level converter topology, implementing such a topology using existing commercial power module inevitably leads to significant parasitic inductance, which limits its further development. So it is necessary to integrate the entire topology within a single module. In this paper, a power module for the SNPC topology is designed. By independently optimizing the commutation loops of the former stage and latter stage, an overlapped busbar structure is introduced to reduce the difference in parasitic inductance between different phases. The proposed module design is validated through simulation and experiments.

title: IntelliGuard: Policy-Contextual Rumor Detection and Sentiment Analysis for Forum Posts
authors: Yanshu Sun, Chi Xu, Danying Zhou, Aobo Wang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In the digital age, online forums have become essential platforms for public discourse, yet they are increasingly vulnerable to the spread of rumors and misinformation. While existing rumor detection methods perform well in analyzing content and propagation patterns, they often lack the ability to incorporate authoritative policy context, making it difficult to flag posts that contradict or misinterpret official policies.To address this gap, we propose IntelliGuard—an intelligent system that enhances rumor detection by leveraging user-uploaded official policy documents for contextual grounding. Our core contribution lies in a novel two-stage training framework. First, we pre-train a BERT-based model on widely used rumor detection datasets such as PHEME, Twitter15/16, and FakeNews-Net to establish foundational detection capabilities. Next, we fine-tune the model using a domain-specific policy-context dataset, employing the parameter-efficient LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) technique for targeted adaptation.We further conduct an ablation study to quantify the impact of key components, including LoRA and policy-context matching. To promote transparency and interpretability, IntelliGuard incorporates explainable AI techniques using Integrated Gradients, allowing users to understand the rationale behind predictions. Extensive experiments on a large real-world Reddit dataset, combined with a qualitative user usability study, demonstrate IntelliGuard’s superior performance, generalizability, and practical value in policy-aware rumor detection.

title:
Robust T–S Fuzzy H
                    <sub>∞</sub>
                    Control for Maximum Power Point Tracking in Uncertain Photovoltaic Systems
authors: Rabii Azdoud, Abdelmounaim Khallouq, Ali Margal, Soukaina El Daoudi, Fatima Zahra Loudifa, Abderrazzak El Abdallaoui, Asma Karama
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: This work addresses the problem of maximum power point tracking (MPPT) in photovoltaic (PV) systems operating under parametric uncertainties and environmental variations. The MPPT is formulated using Takagi–Sugeno (T– S) fuzzy modeling approach, as a reference tracking problem. A robust H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf> control law is designed to guarantee closed-loop stability while attenuating the effect of modeling uncertainties and external disturbances. Stability and performance conditions are established via Lyapunov-based analysis and expressed as Linear Matrix Inequalities (LMIs). The proposed strategy’s performance is evaluated using MATLAB/Simulink simulations under varying irradiance and temperature profiles, showing reliable convergence to the maximum power point.

title: Research and Practice of the Application of Digital Twin Technology in the Intelligent Inspection System of Hydropower Station
authors: Ligui Wu, Liming Yang, Qiangwen Zong
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Hydropower station inspection is an important part of hydropower station operation. In this paper, the digital twin technology is applied to the intelligent inspection system of the Three Gorges Power Station. Firstly, the realistic three dimension (3D) model scene of the Three Gorges Power Station was realized through digital modeling technology. Secondly, on the basis of the three-dimensional digital model, combined with the image monitoring system of the Three Gorges Power Station, the three-dimensional visualization, model and video fusion, intelligent recognition, virtual reality and other technologies are comprehensively used to realize the functions of intelligent online inspection, intelligent early warning, virtual training and other functions, and meet the requirements of hydropower station inspection.

title: Brain-CLIP: An Individual-Preserving Contrastive Framework for Bidirectional Cross-Modal Brain Connectome Translation
authors: Yaokai Yang, Nan Zhao, Zeyu Zhang, Jing Zhang, Lu Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Understanding the complex relationship between structural and functional brain networks is essential for uncovering the mechanisms that govern brain organization. However, most existing studies model this relationship in a unidirectional manner, either predicting functional connectivity (FC) from structural connectivity (SC) or vice versa, while very few approaches enable true bidirectional translation between the two modalities. To address this gap, we introduce Brain-CLIP, a novel framework inspired by the multimodal contrastive learning paradigm exemplified by CLIP, in which heterogeneous modalities are aligned in a shared latent space through semantic correspondence. Similarly, Brain-CLIP aligns SC and FC in a common embedding space to achieve bidirectional translation. Furthermore, we propose an Individual-Preserving CLIP Loss that maintains precise subject-level alignment between SC and FC representations, enabling the model to capture individual-specific connectomic patterns rather than merely population-level correspondences. Comprehensive evaluations on two large-scale datasets, HCP and ADNI, demonstrate that Brain-CLIP substantially outperforms existing methods. (Code is available here: https://github.com/BrainX-Lab/Brain-CLIP)

title: AgesAI: An Intelligent Cybersecurity Framework for Advanced Threat Detection and Mitigation
authors: Ianjot Singh, Manpreet Singh, Vikas Kamra, Sweta Srivastava, Ishpreet Singh
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: A sudden increase in cyber threats such as a zero-day exploit and advanced persistent threat (APT), has demonstrated some serious shortcomings in conventional signature-based cybersecurity solutions especially the failure to keep up with the changes in attack forms. The paper introduces AgesAI, which is an intelligent cybersecurity system conceptualized as an unsupervised machine learning, Large Language Models (LLMs), and reinforcement learning agent that allows the system to detect and mitigate threats dynamically and based on situational factors. The framework uses Isolation Forest-based anomaly detection to detect the deviations of the usual network behavior, and the purpose of the LLMs is to reason semantically of the contextual threat description as well as high-level security decision support. To promote a dynamic role of security policy composition based on perceived states of the system and response to threats, reinforcement learning is included. The paper provides an elaborate architectural design, theoretical statements and algorithmic workflow that shows the interface between the detection, reasoning and adaptive control layers. Analytical analysis and reference comparison shows that an integrated method like the one can provide a great deal of accuracy in detection, a low false-positive rate, and responsiveness relative to systems based on unchanging rules and regulations. The suggested framework illustrates the possibility of integrating learning, reasoning, and adaptation as a single cyber security framework that will provide a scalable and privacy-aware base of intelligent security solutions deployed by enterprises and clouds in critical infrastructure scenarios in the future.

title: Wearable Flexible Triangular Microstrip Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G Applications
authors: Asma Oirgari, Ibtissam Bahlaouane, Saida Ibnyaich, Samira Chabaa, Abdelouhab Zeroual
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: In this paper, a flexible triangular patch antenna is designed, simulated, and fabricated. A textile substrate with a presumed relative permittivity of 1.6 was considered. The antenna was fabricated using denim (jean) textile as the substrate with dimensions of 30 × 34 × 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and copper tape as the conductive material, while it was designed and simulated using ANSYS HFSS, and the results were validated using CST Microwave Studio software. The textenna (textile + antenna) attained a resonance frequency of 4.30 GHz and a bandwidth from 2.74 GHz to 5.00 GHz, exhibiting superior resonance characteristics in the Sub-6 GHz range. These findings verify that the proposed antenna is highly competitive as an inexpensive, compact, and easily integrable solution for Sub-6 GHz 5G, WiMAX, and wearable applications.

title: An Enhanced Multi Complex Coefficient Filter based Control for a Grid Connected Solar Energy Conversion System
authors: Nirmal Mukundan C M, Ahmed Al Durra, Mohamed El Moursi, Tarek El Fouly
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Industrial prosumers are increasingly adopting solar energy conversion systems and integrating them with the grid to enhance reliability. However, the quality of power exchange with the grid is often compromised due to poor grid voltage conditions and distortions introduced by local nonlinear loads. This paper presents a grid integration control strategy capable of operating reliably under weak grid conditions and in the presence of nonlinear, imbalanced, and reactive local loads. The ability of a multi-complex coefficient filter (MCCF) to extract the positive-sequence voltage component is typically affected by high-order harmonics, DC offsets, frequency deviations, and amplitude or phase imbalances. To address these challenges, an enhanced MCCF structure is proposed by incorporating a second-order generalized integrator (SOGI)-based prefilter. The bandwidth of the SOGI controller is carefully tuned for optimal filtering, and the corner frequencies derived from the MCCF are fed back to the SOGI to maintain accurate grid synchronization. The use of SOGI supports the fast dynamic response of the enhanced MCCF, owing to its computational simplicity. The improved SOGI used for active load current component extraction enables the SECS to effectively meet both the reactive power and harmonic compensation requirements of local loads. The proposed single-stage system eliminates the need for a separate DC-DC converter by integrating maximum power point tracking (MPPT) and grid synchronization control within a single voltage source converter. The system performances are validated through a MATLAB/Simulink model under varying source, load, and PV-side conditions.

title: EMI Reduction and Performance Assessment in a Multiphase Buck Converter using Digital Current Control Techniques under Periodic Frequency Modulation
authors: Teja Golla, Santanu Kapat, Philip T. Krein
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A bulky EMI filter is needed in a multiphase buck converter for higher current applications, even with phase interleaving, to attenuate conducted EMI due to discontinuous input current. In this paper, periodic frequency modulation techniques are explored in a multiphase buck converter, and their impacts on steady-state parameters and transient performance are investigated under digital current mode control. A frequency-modulated digital current control architecture is proposed, and a relevant sampling and controller update process is demonstrated. A framework is developed for analyzing power spectral density (PSD) under various periodic frequency modulation techniques. Design aspects of these techniques are discussed in context. A two-phase buck converter prototype and experimental case studies are presented for nominal 1 MHz switching frequency. Periodic bifrequency modulation and quantized triangular modulation techniques are considered. Both techniques are shown to have insignificant impacts on closed-loop stability, steady-state parameters, and transient performance. However, quantized triangular modulation results in substantial EMI reduction compared to bifrequency modulation and to unmodulated cases.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Enhanced Black-winged Kite Algorithm for Cloud Workflow Scheduling
authors: Mohammad Qasim, Md. Saquib Jawed, Raza Abbas Haidri
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The growing complexity of dynamic and heterogeneous cloud environments has significantly increased the need for efficient scheduling algorithms. Metaheuristic (MH) algorithms have attracted significant attention for solving scheduling problems over the past decade. This study presents a recently developed Black-winged Kite Algorithm (BKA) along with three reinforcement learning (RL)-enhanced BKA variants: Q-learning-based BKA (BKA-Q), SARSA-based BKA (BKA-SARSA), and deep Q-network (DQN)-based BKA (BKA-DQN) to tackle the workflow scheduling problem. The performance of RL-enhanced BKA variants compares to standard BKA across the LIGO, Montage, and Epigenomics workflows in cloud environments. Experimental findings demonstrate that the BKA-DQN variant improves the makespan results compared to the standard BKA and the other two RL-enhanced BKA variants. The BKA-DQN variant achieves a superior makespan and faster convergence across all workflows. The DQN-enhanced BKA improves the makespan performance by $\mathbf{1. 5 \%}$ to $\mathbf{5. 4 \%}$ across different scientific workflows compared to the other algorithms. Statistical analysis further validates BKA-DQN’s strong potential for solving workflow scheduling problems.

title: Optimized TPS with New Symmetric Mode Boundaries for Bidirectional DAB Converters in Ultra-Wide Range SiC EV Charging Applications
authors: Mahmoodreza Eskandarpour Azizkandi, Wensong Yu, Md Tanvir Ahammed, Liming Liu, Hongrae Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel optimized triple-phase-shift (TPS) with symmetric mode boundaries for bidirectional dual-active-bridge (DAB) converters, enabling practical and efficient ultra-wide voltage range (150 V - 900 V) and high frequency (150 kHz) operation for SiC-based EV chargers. Unlike state-of-the-art TPS methods, which suffer from asymmetric mode plots, complex multi-case equations, lack of reverse power flow coverage, and impractical real-time implementation, the proposed approach defines a normalized voltage ratio and absolute normalized power to unify and cover both forward and reverse power flow under a single, super-simplified, and computationally efficient control law and reveal a symmetrical relationship between buck and boost regions, eliminating the need for separate equations. Experimental validation on a 12 kW, 150 kHz prototype demonstrates the proposed TPS strategy enables full ZVS even at very low power, and fast real-time execution—confirming its practicality for SiC EV charging applications.

title: Morphology-Embedded Graph Optimization for EM Neuron Tracing
authors: Qihua Chen, Xuejin Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstructing neurons from large-scale electron microscopy (EM) 3D images for connectomics is a significant challenge, particularly for neurons with complex morphologies. Current automatic reconstruction pipelines first generate oversegmented fragments, followed by tracing algorithms that merge them into more complete neuronal structures. However, these merging approaches rely on local features, failing to model long-range dependencies and global structural constraints, which often leads to biologically implausible morphology. To address this, we propose the MorphologyEmbedded Graph Optimization Network for global neuron tracing. The model employs a path-tracing-based message passing mechanism, analogous to human experts, to model long-range connectivity, overcoming the local limitations of traditional graph-cut methods. Additionally, it fuses multiscale morphological representations into the graph to model fine-grained and global biological plausibility. Experiments demonstrate this method significantly reduces reconstruction errors compared to the Multicut algorithm and also outperforms state-of-the-art graph neural networks, enhancing the biological plausibility of the results.

title: Harmonic-Fused Dual-Domain Network for Low-Concentration Magnetic Particle Imaging Reconstruction
authors: Haicheng Du, Guanghui Li, Ziwei Chen, Penghua Zhai, Yu An, Jie Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstruction of low-concentration magnetic particle imaging (MPI) is a critical challenge for clinical translation, due to low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), severe artifacts, and blurred details in the reconstructed harmonic images. The ISBI-2026 LCR-MPI Challenge is focused on achieving high-fidelity fifth-harmonic reconstruction from low-concentration data. The network takes normalized third- and fifth-harmonic images as dual-channel input and integrates a spatially adaptive denormalization-based fusion module to leverage the structural prior of the third harmonic for guiding the restoration of noisy fifth-harmonic images. To further improve the signal-to-noise ratio, a spatial-frequency dual-domain fusion scheme based on a symmetric encoder-decoder architecture with fast Fourier convolution is adopted. Additionally, a learnable gated skip connection is incorporated to adaptively regulate network information flow and enhance feature representation capability. Experiments on the challenge dataset demonstrate that DuLCR-Net outperforms the baselines, providing an effective solution for low-concentration MPI reconstruction and advancing its clinical applicability.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble Framework Using Dimensionality Reduction and XAI for Financial Fraud Detection in Manufacturing
authors: Hui Xia, Qin Wang
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to investigate the application of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) in financial fraud detection (FFD) in manufacturing industry. Financial fraud has become increasingly sophisticated and concealed, particularly prevalent in the manufacturing sector. Such corporate misconduct severely infringes the interests of stakeholders and poses a significant threat to the stability of the entire market system. In recent years, machine learning models have been increasingly applied to FFD. However, traditional models primarily emphasize predictive accuracy while overlooking interpretability, and they are becoming increasingly inadequate in identifying more complex fraudulent activities. To address these challenges, this study proposes an interpretable ensemble learning framework for FFD. The framework employs a stacked integration of multiple well-established models, including XGBoost, LightGBM, decision trees, and random forests. At the top level of the stacking architecture, a logistic regression model is utilized as the meta-learner to integrate the predictions of the base learners. Regarding dimensionality reduction, this study employs LightGBM-based feature selection to reduce information redundancy in high-dimensional data, enhancing FFD model efficiency. Furthermore, this study incorporates SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) into the model framework, endowing the predictive outcomes with transparent and interpretable characteristics.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> We compare several baseline models with the proposed method, and the results show that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance.

title: Automated Sarcopenia Risk Stratification from POCUS Using Vision Transformers and Radiomics
authors: Ricardo Teresa Ribeiro, Théo Coutaudier, Patrizia D'Amelio
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Sarcopenia is an age-related condition characterized by progressive loss of skeletal muscle mass, quality and function, leading to increased morbidity and mortality. Early detection remains challenging due to lack of scalable assessment tools. POCUS offers a portable, non-ionizing modality for bedside muscle evaluation, but currently analysis is operator dependent and time-consuming. This study proposes, an automated framework for sarcopenia risk stratification from standardized POCUS acquisitions. A Vision transformer-based segmentation model (UltraSAM) was fine-tuned to delineate three lower-limb muscles in Bmode ultrasound images acquired from 70 elderly inpatients. The model achieved 88.0% Dice and 79.6%. IoU. Segmentation masks were used to automatically estimate muscle thickness and extract reproducible radiomic features, which were combined with clinical variables. This feature set was integrated within a nested crossvalidation to predict <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S A R C-F \geq 2, S A R C-F \geq 5$</tex> and low handgrip strength. These classifiers were combined using a rule-based ensemble strategy to derive a unified Sarcopenia Risk Ultrasound (SaRUS). The ensemble achieved 75.6% balanced accuracy and 84.6% sensitivity, demonstrating strong screening capability. The proposed approach highlights the potential of integrating AI-driven segmentation, radiomics, and clinical indicators for scalable sarcopenia assessment using POCUS.

title: A 22-kW Three-Phase Wireless Charging System Capable of Independent Phase Control
authors: Hyeon-Young Lee, Yeon-Jun Park, Min-Seok Chae, Jun-Sik Hwang, Seojin Kim, Hyeon-Gyu Choi, Euihoon Chung
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper proposes a new three-phase wireless power transfer (WPT) system designed to address the efficiency degradation caused by coil misalignment. The proposed system independently controls the dc-link voltage and duty cycle of each primary-side inverter phase, this enables each phase to deliver power at an independently optimized level that reflects the phase-specific coupling variations under misalignment. Since the optimal dc-link voltage varies significantly across phases under misalignment, a systematic optimization process is essential. To this end, this study presents a coil modeling technique, a mathematical analysis method, and a numerical iteration–based optimization procedure. Simulation results verify the validity of the proposed mathematical model, and a 22-kW hardware prototype experimentally demonstrates the effectiveness of the method, achieving 96.74 % efficiency under aligned conditions and 95.17 % efficiency under misaligned conditions. Furthermore, the strong agreement between the simulated and measured waveforms confirms that the proposed design procedure provides a systematic and highly reproducible methodology for high-performance WPT system development.

title: Distilling Expert Surgical Knowledge: How to Train Local Surgical VLMs for Anatomy Explanation in Complete Mesocolic Excision
authors: Lennart Maack, Julia-Kristin Graß, Lisa-Marie Toscha, Nathaniel Melling, Alexander Schlaefer
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Recently, Vision Large Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated high potential in computer-aided diagnosis and decision-support. However, current VLMs show deficits in domain specific surgical scene understanding, such as identifying and explaining anatomical landmarks during Complete Mesocolic Excision. Additionally, there is a need for locally deployable models to avoid patient data leakage to large VLMs, hosted outside the clinic. We propose a privacy-preserving framework to distill knowledge from large, general-purpose LLMs into an efficient, local VLM. We generate an expert-supervised dataset by prompting a teacher LLM without sensitive images, using only textual context and binary segmentation masks for spatial information. This dataset is used for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and subsequent Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) of the locally deployable VLM. Our evaluation confirms that finetuning VLMs with our generated datasets increases surgical domain knowledge compared to its base VLM by a large margin. Overall, this work validates a data-efficient and privacy-conforming way to train a surgical domain optimized, locally deployable VLM for surgical scene understanding.

title: Self-Supervised Vision Transformer for Surgical Phase Recognition in Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection
authors: Gyanateet Dutta, Aya Hammad, Thomas Archer, Qi Dou, Noor Mohammed, Sharib Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Surgical phase recognition is critical for context-aware interventions, but deep learning models require large, costly annotated datasets. This work presents DINO-Endo, an efficient framework pairing a self-supervised DINOv2 Vision Transformer (ViT-S/14) backbone with lightweight temporal models. Through sequential domain adaptation—pretraining on unannotated porcine videos followed by human endoscopic videos—our approach produces highly discriminative features that enable simple temporal decoders to achieve state-of-the-art performance. We demonstrate 89.5% accuracy on patient dataset and 90.0% on complex porcine dataset, outperforming existing methods while using 14-times fewer parameters than foundation models like V-JEPA2. Our comprehensive evaluation reveals that powerful SSL features reduce temporal modeling complexity, with dataset characteristics determining optimal decoder architecture. This work establishes that domain-specific self-supervised pretraining combined with efficient temporal heads achieves state-of-the-art results while reducing annotation dependency and computational requirements for clinical deployment.

title: BrainTAP: Brain Disorder Prediction with Adaptive Distill and Selective Prior Integration
authors: Zhenyu Lei, Aiying Zhang, Song Wang, Han Fan, Jundong Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Predicting clinical outcomes from brain networks in largescale neuroimaging cohorts such as the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study requires effectively integrating functional connectivity (FC) and structural connectivity (SC) while incorporating expert neurobiological knowledge. However, existing multimodal fusion approaches are shallow or over-homogenize the inherently heterogeneous characteristics of FC and SC, while expert-defined anatomical priors are underutilized with static integration. To address these limitations, we propose Brain Transformer with Adaptive Mutual-Distill and Selective Prior Fusion (BrainTAP). We introduce Adaptive Mutual Distill (AMD), which enables layer-wise information exchange between modalities through learnable distill-intact ratios, preserving modalityspecific signals while capturing cross-modal synergies. We further develop Selective Prior Fusion (SPF), which integrates expert-defined anatomical priors in an adaptive way. Evaluated on the ABCD dataset for predicting attentionrelated disorders, BrainTAP achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art baselines, demonstrating its effectiveness for brain disorder prediction.

title: A Multi-Scale Linear-Time Encoder for Whole-Slide Image Analysis
authors: Jagan Mohan Reddy Dwarampudi, Joshua Wong, Hien Van Nguyen, Tania Banerjee
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We introduce Multi-scale Adaptive Recurrent Biomedical Linear-time Encoder (MARBLE), the first purely Mamba-based multi-state multiple instance learning (MIL) framework for whole-slide image (WSI) analysis. MARBLE processes multiple magnification levels in parallel and integrates coarse-to-fine reasoning within a linear-time state-space model, efficiently capturing cross-scale dependencies with minimal parameter overhead. WSI analysis remains challenging due to gigapixel resolutions and hierarchical magnifications, while existing MIL methods typically operate at a single scale and transformer-based approaches suffer from quadratic attention costs. By coupling parallel multi-scale processing with linear-time sequence modeling, MARBLE provides a scalable and modular alternative to attention-based architectures. Experiments on five public datasets show improvements of up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6. 9 \%}$</tex> in AUC, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0. 3 \%}$</tex> in accuracy, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 \%}$</tex> in C-index, establishing MARBLE as an efficient and generalizable framework for multi-scale WSI analysis.

title: Modified Mantis Shrimp Optimization-Based Global MPPT for Photovoltaic Systems under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Nisha G Poothullil, Abdul Saleem
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) techniques are essential for enhancing the efficiency of photovoltaic (PV) systems under dynamically changing environmental conditions. Under partial shading conditions (PSC), PV arrays exhibit multiple peaks in their power-voltage (P-V) characteristics, posing a significant challenge for conventional MPPT techniques to accurately locate the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP). Although traditional techniques perform well under uniform irradiance, they often fail under PSC due to their inability to distinguish between local and global peaks. To address this, a Modified Mantis Shrimp Optimization (MSO) algorithm is proposed in this paper. Inspired by the predatory and sensory behavior of mantis shrimps, the algorithm effectively balances exploration and exploitation to ensure rapid and accurate tracking of the GMPP. The proposed MSO algorithm demonstrates improved tracking performance with faster convergence, reduced power oscillations, and high efficiency. MATLAB-based simulation results under various partial shading scenarios validate the superiority of the proposed approach when compared with conventional P&O, PSO, and GWO techniques. The MSO algorithm exhibits excellent dynamic response and robustness, making it a promising solution for real-time MPPT in PV systems under non-uniform irradiance.

title: Analysis of Hard and Soft Switching Loss Imbalance Induced by Parameter Dispersion in Parallel Connected SiC MOSFETs
authors: Dimitrios Deldimos, Andrea Piccioni, Fausto Stella, Gianmario Pellegrino
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Parallel operation of SiC MOSFETs is essential in high-current converters since individual switches are limited in current handling capabilities by maximum die size and packaging constraints. However, device-to-device parametric variations lead to unevenly distributed switching currents, thus resulting in unbalanced switching losses and additional thermal stress. This paper presents an experimental investigation of how variations in the intrinsic parameters of the devices can influence loss sharing during hard and soft switching. A population of device samples was tested at datasheet conditions, and switching loss imbalance was investigated on paralleled pairs. Results reveal that even modest parameter mismatch can lead to significant imbalance in both soft and hard-switching transitions. These findings highlight the importance of dispersion-aware design practices for reliable parallel operation in high-current converters.

title: Enhancing ECG Signal Compression Robustness Using Noise-Aware Deep Convolutional Autoencoders in IoT-Assisted Remote Healthcare
authors: Alyaa Ali Neamah, Mustafa Ismael
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: High-efficiency compression of the ECG signal is crucial for minimizing transmission bandwidth and power consumption in healthcare and Internet of Things (IoT) systems. In this work, the proposed Deep Convolution Auto encoder (Deep CNN AE) model consists of three stages: encoder, noise channel, and decoder. This allows for efficient signal compression and reconstruction, enabling the model to learn concise representations while maintaining its resistance to noise, under varying noise channel conditions, experiments were conducted using real ECG signals from the MIT-BIH database. The proposed model achieved minimal noise during signal reconstruction and high accuracy, thus outperforming other models (Baseline, Quantized, and Denoise). This model achieved PRD values as low as 1.7%, which is considered very low, along with high PSNR values of up to 41.81 dB and correlation coefficients exceeding 0.99 and compression ratio of 4, this demonstrates the model’s ability to retain the ECG signal shape during retrieval. The experimental findings confirm that incorporating deep convolutional feature extraction with noise-aware latent representations substantially enhances compression robustness and reconstruction quality. Consequently, the proposed Deep CNNAE represents a promising solution for low-power, bandwidthconstrained ECG monitoring in next-generation remote healthcare and wearable biomedical systems Keywords-ECG signal compression, Deep convolutional auto encoder, Noise-aware learning, Biomedical signal processing, IoT-Based remote healthcare.

title: Large Language Models in Cybersecurity AI-Driven Threat Detection and Prevention
authors: Deepika Sharma, Anurag Shrivastava, Bharti Sahu, Ronak Agrawal, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Ankam Sreejyothsna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The Large Language Models (LLMs) have been made considerably more efficient, and the AI helps to identify and eliminate the possible threats to the safety of the cyberspace. The intrusion identification, the automation of vulnerability tests, and the use of complex attack patterns are helpful in identifying a vulnerability and assist in bettering the assault intelligence, which is used in the localization of complex patterns of attacks. However, the significant threats of the implementation of ML-based applications, such as adversarial attacks, data privacy, and model bias, are to be addressed in the relevant manner. LLMs in Cybersecurity: Overview of recent advances The article concerns the future of using LLMs in cybersecurity in terms of how the powerful models can be used to prevent threats before they occur, detect anomalies and facilitate the process of automated response. It also brings out some of the problems and ethical issues of introducing LLM to security systems. By enlisting the strength of the progress of AI, organizations will be able to improve their cyber defense and minimize exposure to the ever-changing character of cyber threats. The authors of the study believe that LLAMs have potential solutions to cybersecurity but more research is needed to reduce vulnerabilities and maximize their use in practice.

title: Obscuration to Clarity: Bone Suppression for Enhanced Localization in Pneumothorax Segmentation of Chest Radiographs
authors: Ananya Shukla, Amog Rao, Siddharth S., Rina Bao
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Chest radiography (CXR) is a primary modality for assessing cardiopulmonary conditions, but its effectiveness is limited by anatomical obstructions (e.g., ribs, clavicles) that hinder accurate pneumothorax segmentation, boundary delineation, and severity estimation. While deep learning-based bone suppression improves soft-tissue visibility, its utility for precise pixel-wise localization remains underexplored. This study investigates the downstream application of bone suppression for pneumothorax segmentation, integrating it as a preprocessing step to mitigate bony obscuration. We evaluate its impact across CNN and Vision Transformer models on two public datasets, where models trained on bone-suppressed CXRs significantly outperform (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.05$</tex>) non-suppressed counterparts, achieving up to 17 % improvement in Mean Average Surface Distance (MASD), 4.9% in Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC), and 5.9 % in Normalized Surface Dice (NSD), alongside a 9.5% gain in Matthew's Correlation Coefficient (MCC). These results demonstrate bone suppression as an architecture-independent enhancement for pneumothorax localization, improving the reliability of automated CXR interpretation.

title: Predicting Longitudinal Brain Change from Baseline 3D MRI and Detecting Abnormal Atrophy Using Deep Learning
authors: Linh N. N. Le, Evan Fletcher, Duygu Tosun, Jinyi Qi, Audrey P. Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is highly heterogeneous, with characteristic regional changes in patterns of cortical volume. This study aims to predict future longitudinal brain volume changes in older individuals with various stages of clinical diagnoses, from healthy to dementia, using predictions from baseline MRI. We test two deep learning (DL) approaches predicting longitudinal atrophy: (i) a unimodal model using baseline MRI scans from a single time point as predictor and log-Jacobian atrophy map as target, and (ii) a multimodal model including imaging and non-imaging data. We then developed a normative model using mixed linear regression, based on the longitudinal brain changes predicted by the DL model, to detect deviations in the clinical patient population. This preliminary result using the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset demonstrated the efficacy of the proposed framework to detect abnormal brain regions with high atrophy rate related to dementia.

title: 800 V On-Board Charger with Reduced DC-Link Capacitance via Embedded Active Power Decoupling
authors: Qishan Pan, Guanjiang Liu, Dejun Zhang, Haoyu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: On-board chargers (OBCs) face challenges from bulky DC-link electrolytic capacitors, oversized isolation transformers, and efficiency loss in the isolated DC/DC stage over wide battery voltage ranges. Conventional active power decoupling (APD) circuits can reduce DC-link capacitance requirements. However, they typically rely on additional external switches and energy storage components dedicated to a single function. To improve, this work introduces a two-stage 800V OBC architecture with reduced DC-link capacitance via embedded active power decoupling. The proposed design employs an integrated APD unit, thereby lowering capacitance demand, minimizing transformer volume, and enhancing the performance of the isolated DC/DC stage under wide gain conditions. A 3.3kW prototype, supporting an output voltage range of 600–900V, achieves a peak full-load efficiency of 95.96%, while consistently maintaining high performance across the entire operating range.

title: Indirect MPC with Adaptive DC-Link Voltage Control for a CHB-based Shunt Active Power Filter
authors: Christopher Curtis, Joseph Fourcaudot, Saige Niemi, Darian Smith, Apparao Dekka, Deepak Ronanki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The fixed dc-link voltage operation of a shunt active power filter (SAPF) produces high current ripple and affects its ability to compensate the grid harmonic currents under light loading conditions (i.e., operating at low modulation indices). In this paper, an indirect model predictive control (MPC) is proposed for a cascaded H-bridge (CHB) based SAPF to compensate the grid harmonic currents. In addition, an adaptive dc-link voltage control philosophy is proposed to adjust the net dc-link voltage depending on the harmonic current magnitude. Through the proposed method, CHB-based SAPF always operates at higher modulation indices, resulting in an improved performance and produces less current ripple, irrespective of the harmonic current magnitude. Also, the redundancy switching state selection algorithm is developed to equally distribute the net dc-link voltage between each H-bridge module in a CHB. Simulation studies are presented to study the performance of the proposed indirect MPC with adaptive dc-link voltage control philosophy for a CHB-based SAPF with current and voltage source type harmonic loads.

title: Identifying Optimal nnU-Net Configuration for Cerebral Microbleed Segmentation
authors: Junmo Kwon, Sang Won Seo, Hwan-ho Cho, Hyunjin Park
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The nnU-Net has become the de facto standard in medical image segmentation. While the generalizability of nnUNet has widely been studied across various domains, we question the optimality of the automated nnU-Net configuration specifically for cerebral microbleed (CMB) segmentation. We identified that the standard nnU-Net setup, particularly the recommended input patch size, is primarily optimized for the heterogeneous nature of multi-organ segmentation, resulting in suboptimal performance for CMB segmentation. To address this limitation, we propose a patch selection algorithm based on a local minima search that fully leverages the imaging characteristics of CMB lesions. Experiments on two independent cohorts demonstrate that our approach improves the overall performance without altering the segmentation backbone, highlighting the important role of preprocessing. Our code is available at https://github.com/junmokwon/CMBPreprocessing

title: High-Performance FCS-MPC for a New Five-Level Voltage Source Inverter
authors: Ali Azimi Bizaki, Apparao Dekka, Deepak Ronanki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The efficacy of finite control-set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) for multilevel inverters (MLIs) is contingent upon the precision of the system’s mathematical models. Particularly, the prediction accuracy is interlinked with the accuracy of mathematical models, which further influence the harmonic performance and switching frequency of MLIs with FCS-MPC methods. This research proposes an enhanced FCS-MPC approach for a novel five-level voltage source inverter (5L-VSI). The new 5L-VSI has lesser flying capacitors (FCs) and possesses sufficient redundant switching states to provide flexible regulation of FC voltages and inverter currents. Additionally, the discrete-time (DT) models of the 5L-VSI’s currents and FC voltages are formulated using Heun’s discretization method to enhance the performance of the proposed FCS-MPC. The proposed Heun’s-based DT models minimize the discretization error in the predicted control variables, which further results in the reduction of switching frequency and total harmonic distortion in the output currents of MLIs. Through simulation studies, the performance of the proposed enhanced FCS-MPC is exhibited under steady state and transient scenarios. It is further analyzed in conjunction with the existing FCS-MPC method to demonstrate its benefits.

title: Prediction of Crop Yield Using AI Approach
authors: Megavath Aravind Kumar, Swarup Nandi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: Agriculture remains vital to both the economy and food security of India, but accurate crop yield forecasting remains hampered by climatic variability, soil heterogeneity, and limitations in traditional estimation methodologies. Traditional methods are mainly based on historical records, which hardly consider real-time environmental conditions and thus cannot produce reliable predictions. This paper, therefore, proposes a hybrid machine learning-based framework for crop yield prediction using multi-source agricultural data. The given framework combines weather parameters, soil nutrient data, and historical data of crop yield, collected from Medak district. Individual models such as Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) are trained and evaluated to assess their predictive capabilities. A hybrid ensemble model has been further developed in order to address the limitations of single model approaches by synergistically integrating the advantages of these techniques. To validate the generalizability of our findings, we utilized the standard metrics of $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ score, Mean Absolute Error(MAE), Root Mean Square Error(RMSE), and accuracy besides $\mathbf{k}$-fold cross-validation to ensure robustness. The experimental results indicate that the proposed hybrid ensemble model achieves a maximum prediction accuracy of 95.82%, outperforming individual machine learning models in terms of stability and error reduction. The key contributions of this research are three-fold: (i) the integration of multi-source district-level data such as weather and soil nutrients for crop yield prediction (ii) the design of a hybrid ensemble model that combines DT, RF, SVM, and ANN to address the limitations of individual models when data is limited, and (iii) the evaluation of the model using accuracy, $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$, MAE, and RMSE, which outperforms the individual models. The dataset is for a period of ten years, from 2015 to 2025, covering important nutrients in the soil.

title: Variance-Penalized MC-Dropout as a Learned Smoothing Prior for Brain Tumour Segmentation
authors: Satyaki Roy Chowdhury, Golrokh Mirzaei
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation is essential for diagnosis and treatment planning, yet many deep learning approaches, particularly convolutional neural networks, such as UNet and its variations produce noisy boundaries in regions of tumor infiltration. We introduce UAMSA-UNet, an Uncertainty-Aware Multi-Scale Attention-based Bayesian UNet that leverages Monte Carlo Dropout to impose a data-driven smoothing prior while fusing multi-scale features and attention maps to capture both fine details and global context. The smoothingregularized loss augments binary cross-entropy with a variance penalty across stochastic forward passes, suppressing spurious fluctuations and yielding spatially coherent masks. On BraTS2023, UAMSA-UNet improves Dice by up to 3.3% and mean IoU by up to 2.7% over UNet; on BraTS2024, it delivers up to 4.5% Dice and 4.0% IoU gains over the best baseline. It also reduces FLOPs by 42.5% relative to UNet++ while maintaining higher accuracy. By coupling multi-scale attention with a learned smoothing prior, UAMSA-UNet achieves higher-quality, more efficient segmentation and provides a flexible foundation for future integration with transformer modules.

title: Using Google Authenticator and HashiCorp Vault to Build a Security System to Boost Digital Wallet Security and Recovery Secrets
authors: Samah Mahmood Ahmed, Raad A. Muhajjar, Mishall Al-Zubaidie
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: This paper presents a system designed to enhance and extend the security architecture of digital wallets by integrating HashiCorp Vault (HV) and Google Authenticator (GA) technologies with Amazon Web Services (AWS). GA is used as a mechanism to strengthen digital wallet security by protecting encryption keys and passwords from attackers. Access rights management is implemented in HV using AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) capabilities, in addition to HV’s own encryption and secret management capabilities. By synchronizing these technologies, we create a comprehensive security system capable of mitigating the risk of unauthorized access to digital wallets and recovering encrypted and stored statements, while simultaneously restricting access to them. The results, based on the performance of the designed system, demonstrate the benefits of rapid response. When testing a database of 5,000 entries, we found that the recovery and storage processes were 100% successful. Running the Python code using Python3 entropy_test.py resulted in a quality mark of 97.02% with a Shannon entropy of 3.2914 bits, plus an achievable maximum mark of 3.3926 bits, indicating that the data generation process is random and reliable within the limits of a robust and reliable system.

title: Swarm Intelligence Based Approach Towards Construction of an Optimal Portfolio of Stocks
authors: Karthikeyan P, Manjula K A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Nature-inspired metaheuristic algorithms are popular for their capability to solve complex issues. Among the Nature-inspired computing techniques, Swarm intelligence techniques, especially Ant Colony Optimisation (ACO) algorithm are a widely used technique. ACO is inspired by how ants find paths. This study presents a novel approach to portfolio optimization based on the principles of Ant Colony Optimisation (ACO) algorithm. We applied this method to historical stock data of eight selected companies in India. The basic principles of search in ACO is used along with the principles of Modern Portfolio Theory to create the best possible portfolio. Our aim is to achieve the highest Sharpe Ratio, even when the market is highly unpredictable. In this study, the portfolio is created using data from eight major Indian stocks collected from the NSE, for the years from 2015 to 2024. A Sharpe Ratio of 1.45 is achieved, with an expected annual return of 18.2% and a risk level (volatility) of 10.5%. These results indicate that the new method based on ACO is an effective tool for developing an optimized portfolio.

title: Deep-GyralNet: Enabling Machine Learning in Gyral Folding Pattern Extraction on Cortical Surface
authors: Chao Cao, Jiale Cheng, Minheng Chen, Jing Zhang, Tianming Liu, Lu Zhang, Dajiang Zhu, Gang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: The 3-hinge gyrus (3HG), where three gyri converge, acts as a structural and functional hub in the brain. Connecting these hubs forms the GyralNet, but traditionally extraction requires complex geometric steps, limiting scalability. To address this challenge, we propose Deep-GyralNet, a novel deep learning framework for efficient 3HG and GyralNet extraction. Deep-GyralNet leverages a Spherical U-Net to learn gyral crest representations from cortical morphological features, coupled with a Connectivity-Aware Path Enforcement loss that enforces topological continuity of predicted ridges. A minimum spanning tree-based refinement is then applied to ensure structural connectivity and yield a topologically valid GyralNet. Experiments on the Human Connectome Project dataset demonstrate that Deep-GyralNet achieves an average completeness of 98%, while reducing processing time by 94% compared to the conventional pipeline. These establish DeepGyralNet the first deep learning solution for fast and accurate 3HG identification, enabling large-scale studies of gyral folding networks in brain development, aging and disorders.

title: Enhanced Classification Using Dynamically Weighted Attribute Valued Averaged One-Dependence Estimators (DAVA-AODE)
authors: M. Vigilson Prem, D. Paul Raj, P.S. Priyanka, V. Chrissy Panfila
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: In a variety of classification situations, the Averaged One-Dependence Estimator (AODE) classifiers have proven to be useful due to their interpretability and their ability to perform classification. Unfortunately, these classifiers usually struggle when trying to handle classification datasets with characteristics of different relevance but same weight, leading to inferior performance.
