This paper presents the implementation of discontinuous SVPWM (DSVPWM) strategies within the FOC framework for FPIMs to reduce switching losses without disturbing the dynamic performance of the drive. The proposed DSVPWM techniques reduce switching transitions in each modulation cycle. A comparative analysis between conventional SVPWM and DSVPWM is conducted using MATLAB/Simulink, evaluating switching losses and current ripple. In addition, the proposed technique is verified in hardware-in-the-loop mode with the Opal-RT real-time simulator (OP5700). The results demonstrate that DSVPWM significantly reduces switching losses while maintaining waveform quality and dynamic response, making it suitable for high-efficiency drive applications.

title: Source Code Vulnerability Detection and Personalized Password Recovery Tool
authors: T Shyam Ganesh, T Vignesh, R Vasanthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The paper presents an intelligent machine learning-based security tool, which is used to process uploaded source code and detect possible vulnerabilities with the help of a feedforward neural network model. The system uses the method of token extraction, feature selection, model training and classification as the techniques of the system that are used to efficiently identify risky blocks of code. The application is trained on labelled datasets using a simple user interface and it helps developers and organisations to improve the security of their software by detecting flaws in real-time. Also, a password-recovery module is incorporated that provides possible passwords according to the keywords that were used in the past and typed in by the user. This project enhances cybersecurity, minimizes manual auditing, and allows identifying and addressing security vulnerabilities in time due to automatic password suggestions and vulnerability detection.

title: Eosinophil Counting with Green Learning
authors: Qixin Hu, Yixing Wu, Mate Levente Nagy, Hwan Dong, C.-C. Jay Kuo
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Quantifying eosinophils in gastrointestinal (GI) biopsies is critical for diagnosing eosinophilic esophagitis. The gold standard, manual counting by pathologists, is laborious, timeconsuming, and suffers from significant variability between physicians. Although deep learning (DL) models have automated this process with high accuracy, they often operate as black boxes, lacking transparency and interpretability. Furthermore, these neural networks require substantial computational resources for training and inference. This paper introduces a novel Green Learning (GL) framework for eosinophil counting, prioritizing transparency and computational efficiency. Our pipeline diverges from backpropagation-based DL. It uses unsupervised sparse-coding-based feature extractors. A dictionary selection module then identifies the most representative dictionaries, which are sent to the decisionlearning model for final classification and counting. We prove that this GL framework can achieve an accuracy competitive with the state-of-the-art DL models while drastically reducing the model size and complexity. This work represents a step towards green transparent AI in digital pathology, offering a computationally frugal and interpretable alternative for efficient clinical deployment.

title: Bytecode-Level Ensemble Framework for V8 Vulnerability Detection in Regression Tests
authors: Deepa Thilak K, Muhammed Sahal M N, Abdur Rahman
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: JavaScript engine vulnerabilities pose critical security risks to billions of web users, yet automated detection in regression test suites remains challenging due to high false-positive rates and limited semantic understanding of execution behavior. This paper presents a bytecode-level machine learning framework for vulnerability detection in V8 engine regression tests, extracting 41 interpretable features from Ignition bytecode including instruction diversity metrics, constant pool characteristics, and proximity-based semantic patterns. A soft-voting ensemble combining Random Forest and Gradient Boosting achieves 78.8% accuracy in balanced mode and–critically–94.1% precision at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 4 3 \%}$</tex> FPR in low-noise triage mode, the operating point most relevant for CI deployment. The ensemble outperforms LSTM-based deep learning by 10.2 percentage points, while SHAP analysis confirms instruction diversity as the primary discriminative feature. The framework supports threshold-based deployment enabling automated prioritization of regression tests requiring manual security review.

title: Joint Multiscale Image Learning for Segmentation in Macroscopic and Microscopic Pathology Images
authors: Yijie Zhu, Ayesha Azam, Ibrahim Alsanie, Ricardo Marques, Syed Ali Khurram, Shan E Ahmed Raza
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Image segmentation at both macroscopic and microscopic level is important in digital pathology. In these images, visually similar regions within the same image often share semantic characteristics, offering an opportunity for intra-image reference learning. We propose a novel binary segmentation framework using a joint multiscale image learning strategy. Our approach builds upon a whole image segmentation model and introduces a complementary part level module that enables mutual referencing among image regions. By computing the feature similarity, each target part image identifies its corresponding reference part images. Cross-attention is then applied between the target and reference part images to exchange information. This strategy not only enhances segmentation consistency but also increases the effective training sample size, improving generalization in small datasets. The proposed model significantly outperforms competitive methods in both macroscopic and microscopic datasets.

title:
Method for Determining Switching Timing of Gate Drive Current of Active Gate Driver to Maximize Turn-off Loss Reduction Under Fixed V
                    <sub>DS</sub>
                    Overshoot Condition in SiC MOSFETs
authors: Yohei Sukita, Toshiaki Inuma, Michihiro Ide, Kenichi Morokuma, Yukihiko Wada, Yuta Yamaoka, Yasushige Mukunoki, Makoto Takamiya
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In the active gate drive, which switches the gate current setting (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> setting) three times when turning off an SiC MOSFET, a method for determining the switching timing of I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> setting is proposed that maximizes the reduction rate of switching loss under V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf> overshoot voltage aligned conditions, compared to conventional gate drive that does not change the gate drive current. Double pulse test measurements of SiC MOSFET are performed at 600 V and under four combinations of conditions including load current of 300 A and 500 A, and temperature of 25 °C and 125 °C, using proposed method. The proposed switching loss reduction rates under V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">DS</inf> overshoot voltage-aligned conditions are −33% to −38%. These values are close to the true maximum reduction rates of −48% to −60%, thus confirming the validity of the proposed method.

title: Digital Outcome Measures for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy: Machine Learning on Wearable Gait Signals Compared with Classical Classifiers and Deep Sequence Models for Good Health
authors: Saravanan. M. S, Siva Rama Krishna Prasad
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Digital outcome measures for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) were derived by applying machine learning to gait signals collected from waist-mounted smartphone accelerometers during walking and running in children with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) Digital outcome measures were obtained by using machine learning on gait signals filtered by waist mounted smartphone accelerometers during walking and running in patients with DMD and typical developing controls, and obtained temporospatial features (step length, step time, cadence, step velocity, and mediolateral power) to characterize DMD unique locomotor patterns. An example SVM classifier using the above example metrics gave a 94.3% accuracy, serving sensitivity of 93.8 and specificity of 94.7; a random forest model with 95.8 accuracy, 95.1 sensitivity and 96.2 specificity; a k nearest neighbors classifier with 92.5 accuracy, 91.9 sensitivity and 93.0 specificity; while deep sequence models, such as an LSTM network, obtained 99.6 accuracy, 99.2 sensitivity and 99.8 specific These accurate performance metrics ensure that both classical and deep learning models are valid to measure DMD specific signatures, which include reduced step length, and increased mediolateral power that may be likened to a Trendelenburg like gait and that deep sequence models are specifically useful in prescreening, monitoring and tracking progression of DMD in a real world environment with high accuracy and reliability, and outperform standard laboratory gait measurements and clinical scales, like the North Star Ambulatory Assessment (NSAA) when assessing DMD.

title: Multimodal Post-Disaster Building Damage Assessment with Weakly Supervised Learning
authors: P Vivekanandhan, Mithuna R, Tharun Kumar V, Swetha D, Denesh Kumar S
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Rapid post-disaster building damage assessment is critical for effective humanitarian response [1]. We present an edge-optimized framework that fuses optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellite imagery with crowd-sourced point annotations to produce real-time damage maps. An adaptive fusion module dynamically adjusts optical-to-SAR weighting based on estimated cloud cover, preserving all-weather capability [11]. A tiered Independent Bayesian Classifier Combination (IBCC) strategy scales computational load to available hardware [7], while progressive neural architectures span devices from smartphones <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{4 7 ~ M B})$</tex> to workstations <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{8 9 0 ~ M B})$</tex> [14]. Experiments on the xBD and BRIGHT datasets show 78% accuracy retention under total cloud occlusion, 64% annotation throughput improvement over expert-only labeling, and robust cross-disaster generalization-establishing practical viability for time-critical disaster scenarios.

title: Transformer-Based Foundation Model for Universal Ultrasound Image Analysis
authors: Zhikai Yang, Junjie Hu, Félix Nieto-del-Amor, Guangyuan Li, Rodrigo Moreno
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We propose a transformer-based foundation model for unified multi-task, multi-organ, multi-disease ultrasound image analysis, targeting 27 subtasks across segmentation, classification, detection, and landmark regression. The model follows hard parameter sharing: the encoder of a Swin Transformer pretrained on ImageNet is shared by all tasks, while each task uses its own prediction head. For segmentation and detection tasks, we use feature pyramid network decoders with separate segmentation and detection branches; for classification and regression tasks, we use encoder-level features with specific heads. Compared to other existing methods in offline experiments, the proposed method has the best performance. At the Foundation Model Challenge for Ultrasound Image Analysis (FMC UIA) challenge, our method achieved 8th place among 24th participating teams. The code is public at https://github.com/HJJ-D/Foundation-Model-Challenge-for-Ultrasound-Image-Analysis.

title: Edge-Based LSTM for Hyper-Local Precision Weather Forecasting
authors: Simhadri Vadrevu, Deepak Kumar, K. Harika Sri Lakshmi, P. Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This paper presents an edge-based hyper-local weather forecasting system that integrates multi-sensor Internet of Things (IoT) data with recurrent deep learning models to achieve higher prediction accuracy than regional weather services. The proposed system employs temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure, air quality, ultraviolet (UV), wind speed, and precipitation sensors interfaced with an Arduino Due and Rasp- berry Pi edge platform to collect real-time meteorological data at a fixed geographic location (Latitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$17.016300^{\circ} \mathrm{N}$</tex>, Longitude: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81.773435^{\circ}$</tex> E). Historical and live sensor data are preprocessed using standard scaling and label encoding and modeled using RNN-based LSTM architectures to forecast weather parameters over a 10-day horizon with low latency edge execution. The Edge- LSTM model has achieved prediction accuracy of 97.53% for temperature, 93.07% for humidity, 98.92% for pressure, and 99.43% for precipitation according to experimental evaluation. In comparing with Google Weather, there is a significant decrease in errors, with the temperature MAE decreasing from 10.88% to 2.47% (77.29% reduction) and the precipitation MAE decreasing from 17.51% to 0.57% (96.74% reduction). Further next, the proposed system achieves an average 10-day forecast coverage of 89%, compared to Google weather is 33%, demonstrating superior reliability in capturing micro-climatic variations. These results are validate that localized sensing combined with edge-based LSTM modeling provides substantial precision advantages over regional.

title: Forward Recovery Aware of Antiparallel Diodes for GaN-Based Synchronous Rectifier Turn-On Delay in MHz-Class LLC
authors: Dong Jiao, Zhengming Hou, Jih-Sheng Lai
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Synchronous-rectifier (SR) turn-on delay becomes a non-negligible loss mechanism in MHz-class GaN LLC converters because reverse current must initially flow through the GaN HEMT’s third-quadrant conduction path, which exhibits a relatively large dynamic drop. A widely used mitigation is to place an antiparallel diode across the SR device so that the reverse current can commutate to the diode before the SR channel turns on. However, experimental results often show inconsistent outcomes, and the underlying reason for the diode’s success or failure has not been clearly identified. This work demonstrates that forward recovery, the transient voltage overshoot that occurs during diode turn-on, is the dominant factor determining whether current commutation can occur within the limited SR turn-on interval. The SR delay is decomposed into two distinct intervals. Mode-1 is governed by loop parasitics and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oss</inf> discharge, during which no diode can conduct regardless of its characteristics. Mode-2 begins when V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf> reaches zero; only in this interval can the antiparallel diode take over current. A diode is effective only if its forward-recovery peak is lower than the GaN body-diode effective drop under the operating current and di/dt. A forward-recovery measurement method is presented to isolate intrinsic diode behavior from inductive overshoot. Hardware tests on an 800-kHz, 400-W LLC prototype confirm approximately 1 W loss reduction using a low-inductance SiC SBD, while ultrafast PN diodes provide no benefit due to large forward-recovery peaks. The study establishes a practical, physics-based selection criterion for antiparallel diodes in GaN SR applications.

title: Prompt-Free Lightweight Sam Adaptation for Histopathology Nuclei Segmentation with Strong Cross-Dataset Generalization
authors: Muhammad Hassan Maqsood, Yanming Zhu, Alfred Lam, Getamesay Dagnaw, Xuefei Yin, Alan Liew
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Histopathology nuclei segmentation is crucial for quantitative tissue analysis and cancer diagnosis. Although existing segmentation methods have achieved strong performance, they are often computationally heavy and show limited generalization across datasets, which constrains their practical deployment. Recent SAM-based approaches have shown great potential in general and medical imaging, but typically rely on prompt guidance or complex decoders, making them less suitable for histopathology images with dense nuclei and heterogeneous appearances. We propose a prompt-free and lightweight SAM adaptation that leverages multi-level encoder features and residual decoding for accurate and efficient nuclei segmentation. The framework fine-tunes only LoRA modules within the frozen SAM encoder, requiring just 4.1M trainable parameters. Experiments on three benchmark datasets TNBC, MoNuSeg, and PanNuke demonstrate state-of-the-art performance and strong cross-dataset generalization, highlighting the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed framework for histopathology applications.

title: Physics-Accurate Modeling of Quantum Effects in Photonic Integrated Circuits
authors: Richard Chung, Mahdi Nikdast, Kaveh Rahbardar Mojaver
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: We present a physics-accurate modeling framework for analyzing quantum effects in photonic integrated circuits. The framework is validated through comparisons with Hong-Ou-Mandel interference experimental data, and used to analyze the fidelity and bi-photon transmittance of Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI)-based meshes implementing Haarrandom matrices.

title: Mitigating the Reconstruction-Detection Trade-Off in Vae-Based Unsupervised Anomaly Detection
authors: Agathe Senellart, Maëlys Solal, Stéphanie Allassonnière, Ninon Burgos
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Variational autoencoders are widely used for unsupervised anomaly detection. Model selection however remains an open-question: to remain fully unsupervised, hyperparameters are often chosen to minimize the reconstruction error on normal samples. In this paper, we reveal a trade-off between reconstruction quality and anomaly detection among <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\beta$</tex>-VAE models. Models with constrained latent space reach higher detection metrics but lower reconstruction quality. We also assess the performance variability across random seeds and show it is linked to the distance between normal and abnormal latent distributions. From this analysis, we justify and investigate two methods to mitigate the reconstructiondetection tradeoff: beta-scheduling and the Sparse VAE. The latter especially shows an improvement in detection while maintaining high reconstruction quality.

title: LMPT: Conditional Point Transformer for Anatomical Landmark Detection on 3D Point Clouds
authors: Matteo Bastico, Pierre Onghena, David Ryckelynck, Beatriz Marcotegui, Santiago Velasco-Forero, Laurent Corté, Caroline Robine-Decourcelle, Etienne Decencière
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate identification of anatomical landmarks is crucial for various medical applications. Traditional manual landmarking is time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variability, while rule-based methods are often tailored to specific geometries or limited sets of landmarks. In recent years, anatomical surfaces have been effectively represented as point clouds, which are lightweight structures composed of spatial coordinates. Following this strategy and to overcome the limitations of existing landmarking techniques, we propose Landmark Point Transformer (LmPT), a method for automatic anatomical landmark detection on point clouds that can leverage homologous bones from different species for translational research. The LmPT model incorporates a conditioning mechanism that enables adaptability to different input types to conduct crossspecies learning. We focus the evaluation of our approach on femoral landmarking using both human and newly annotated dog femurs, demonstrating its generalization and effectiveness across species. The code and dog femur dataset will be publicly available at: https://github.com/Pierreoo/LandmarkPointTransformer.

title: Grid Reactive Power Control in Three-phase Unfolding-based Architectures
authors: Sanat Poddar, Regan Zane
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Three-phase unfolding-based power converter architectures provide a highly efficient and power-dense alternative to conventional multi-stage grid-tied converters. However, the absence of bulk energy storage inherently limits their functionality. This work proposes an auxiliary converter that interfaces with the soft dc-link voltages at the output of the unfolder and circulates only a fraction of the total load power to regulate grid reactive power. A key novelty is that the auxiliary converter is designed to operate with the characteristic time-varying dc voltages produced by a three-phase unfolding stage. The associated challenges of accommodating this voltage variation, as well as identifying a suitable converter topology, design, and control strategy, are addressed. The proposed system is evaluated through simulation and experimentally validated using a 1 kW prototype. The results demonstrate that integrating a partial-power auxiliary converter extends the functionality of unfolding-based architectures and allows for reactive power control.

title: 100kW 3-phase ARCP soft switched SiC (S2SiC) inverter - design and in-lab test verification
authors: Yushi Yang, Nan Lin, Yuheng Wu, Dongyu Jin, Zach Wehri, Muhammad Fasih Uddin, Brij Singh, Long Wu, Yue Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The auxiliary resonant commuted pole (ARCP) inverter is one of the most promising soft switching inverter topologies. In the ARCP inverter, resonant current supplied by the auxiliary circuit ensures soft switching operation. Successful soft switching of inverter results in lower level of electrical and thermal stresses on semiconductor power devices, and leads to lower temperature rise, and higher inverter efficiency. In this paper, 100kW ARCP 3-phase SiC inverter system was designed, fabricated, and double pulse test (DPT) test-verified to prove out feasibility for continuous operation. An excessive voltage overshoot was found under hard start, which poses a potential risk for premature failure of SiC power devices. To mitigate excessive voltage overshoot, authors have developed inverter start-up technique that was test-verified by DPT method. As expected with the developed soft-starting technique, power devices in the soft switched SiC (S2SiC) inverter don’t experience excessive voltage overshoot. The developed hardware for the S2SiC inverter and its control method is ready for interior permanent magnet (IPM) motor control. In this final paper, results from S2SiC inverter feeding a three-phase R-L load are included. Results to be obtained from a back-to-back motor dynamometer-based set-up will be reported in a future paper, where ARCP-based S2SiC inverter will drive an interior permanent magnet (IPM) motor with objective to prove out vitality and a compelling application of the 100kW S2SiC inverter.

title: A Pipeline for DTI Indices and Connectivity Analysis Applied to a Longitudinal Multiple Sclerosis Cohort
authors: Liwei Zhou, Zhangxing Bian, Jinwei Zhang, Blake E. Dewey, Shiv Saidha, Peter A. Calabresi, Aaron Carass, Jerry L. Prince
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multiple sclerosis (MS) causes progressive white matter damage, which can be sensitively assessed using diffusion MRI. However, existing analyses often rely on manual delineation, limiting scalability. We present an automated diffusion-MRI pipeline that computes global lesion-averaged DTI indices, tract-specific measures within tract-lesion intersections, and whole-brain structural connectivity metrics without manual intervention. The framework integrates lesion segmentation and filling, DWI preprocessing and DTI index computation, tractography reconstruction, bundle extraction, brain network analysis, and statistical analysis. Applied to a longitudinal MS cohort, the pipeline produced diffusion and connectivity patterns consistent with previously reported trends, supporting its validity. These results demonstrate the feasibility and practical utility of the proposed framework for scalable, longitudinal MS analysis.

title: Automated Midbrain-To-Pons Ratio Analysis for Differential Parkinsonism Diagnosis: Comparing U-net and Densenet Approaches
authors: Vipul Gohil, Manoj Sankhe, Mitusha Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This paper tackles the problem of differential diagnosis in parkinsonian disorders by creating an optimized deep learning framework for automated midbrain to pons ratio measurement from MRI scans. While this ratio is a clinically validated biomarker, current manual measurement methods have inter-rater variability and are resource intensive. We test U-Net for segmentation and DenseNet for feature extraction on 43 PD and 40 controls from 2 datasets. Our results show U-Net is 96.77 % accurate and DenseNet is 95 % precise, better than previous methods and reducing measurement variability. This work solves the problems of small dataset, anatomical subtlety and clinical workflow integration.

title: Comparison of DC biased Inductances with Small- and Large-Signal AC-Excitation
authors: Pierre Küster, Lennart Hoffmann, Jens Friebe
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Numerous permeabilities are defined in literature under various DC- and AC-excitation levels. In most cases, these are linearized values, which leaves the question which effective inductance is actually present during operation. The paper shows hysteresis measurements of DC biased inductances with different alternating field excitations (large-signal measurements) and the differential as well as the effective inductance derived from this. The results are compared with impedance measurements (small-signal measurements) and discussed. The measurements are performed on an MnZn toroidal core, an MnZn PM-core with air gap, and also on a powder core. In addition to investigating inductance as a function of the excitation, flux density as a function of DC current is also discussed. The article concludes with a comparison of the inductances investigated in a buck converter.

title: Toward Agentic Software Engineering Beyond Code: Framing Vision, Values, and Vocabulary
authors: Rashina Hoda
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Workshop on Agentic Engineering
abstract: None

title: An Agentic System for LLM-Driven Public Transportation Analytics: A Practical Application and Case Study in Salvador-Brazil
authors: Lucas T. Borges, Fei T. Liu, Tatiane Rios, Marcos V. Ferreira, Clovis Carmo, Danilo B. Coimbra, Jorge Nery, Matheus Souza, Noe O. Garcia, Albert Bifet, Ricardo Rios
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Workshop on Agentic Engineering
abstract: None

title: Effects of Heat Treatment on Microstructure, Thermal Transport and Stability in SRF Niobium
authors: Marissa Salisbury, Santosh Chetri, Pashupati Dhakal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Efficient heat transfer and thermal stability are critical for preventing thermal quench and ensuring the high performance and reliability of superconducting radiofrequency (SRF) cavities, especially under high rf fields. In this work, we investigate thermal conductivity and heat capacity of high-purity cold-worked niobium used in SRF applications, with a particular focus on the effects of high-temperature heat treatments. Our measurements reveal a pronounced sensitivity of thermal transport and thermodynamic properties to the underlying microstructure such as grain size and dislocation density. The results show the critical role of controlled heat treatment processes in optimizing the thermal performance of SRF niobium, providing valuable insights for improving cavity fabrication and processing.

title: Enhancing Information Security using Implicit Transmission
authors: Dhanushki Hewawaduge, John P. Fonseka
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In recent studies, it has been shown that Generalized implicit transmission (GIT) can transmit a significantly higher amount of information implicitly over any N number of implicit sequences while transmitting a small fraction of information explicitly over a channel. Such GIT coding schemes can reach data rates higher than the Shannon limit. In this study, it is demonstrated that the same GIT coding technique can be used to transmit information with a level of security much higher than that offered by currently known encryption methods. Specifically, this study proposes adjustments to GIT coding schemes by employing independent interleavers on each sequence, and independently randomizing message bits that are forced to zero during encoding on every sequence, to enhance security.

title:
Engineering of L-channel Tunnel Field Effect Transistor (L-Channel TFET) with high k material to reduce the I
                    <sub>off</sub>
                    current
authors: Sudha Rani, Ashish Raman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Proposed paper represent a novel approach modified L-channel tunnel field Effect Transistor (L-channel TFET) structure doped silicon source, which improve the subthreshold swing (SS), reduced I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</inf>, and the combination of dielectric material (Sio2) and the Molybdenum (Mo) create excellent effect of leakage current reduction. Using the low k-material at the drain to reduce the capacitance of gate–drain. It also improving in terms of downscaling Tunnel Field Effect Transistor.

title: Optimal Temporal SNR Window for Multi-Sensor Fusion: Theory and Jamming-Aware GNN Architecture for Electronic Warfare
authors: Simiao Fei, Xueer Hao, Yinfeng Wu, Lin Huo, Huicun Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multi-object tracking in electronic warfare (EW) relies heavily on temporal fusion—accumulating observations across frames through filtering or recurrent models. Yet this strategy relies on the premise that inter-frame data association is sufficiently accurate to benefit from accumulation. We challenge this assumption by analyzing the temporal signal-to-noise ratio SNRt = Δp/σ (inter-frame displacement over measurement error) and deriving an Optimal SNR Window Theorem. Under nearest-neighbor association, temporal modeling is harmful when SNRt ≲ 5%, beneficial between ≈5% and ≈45%, and saturated beyond; these boundaries are operating-region markers under the assumed association model. In EW scenarios with heavy jamming and false-target injection, SNRt can fall to ∼2.3%—well inside the harmful region.We therefore design a single-frame graph attention network (GAT) with jamming-aware gating that bypasses temporal association entirely. On a multi-sensor benchmark (900 frames, 37.5% jammer ratio, 1 200 injected false targets), the proposed method achieves OSPA of 2717±449 and MOTA of 14.0%±9.3% across seven seeds, outperforming both classical filters (JPDA, LMB/GLMB) and all temporal GNN variants, which produce negative MOTA. A sweep across six noise levels confirms the predicted non-monotonic benefit curve, peaking at SNRt = 11.5% with negative gain at the 2.3% operating point.

title: Broad and Spectrally-Efficient Beamforming for Uni-polarized Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
authors: Mohammad Javad-Kalbasi, Mohammed Saif, Vasileios G. Ataloglou, Kypros M. Kossifos, Marco A. Antoniades, George V. Eleftheriades, Shahrokh Valaee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is composed of low-cost elements that manipulate the propagation environment from a transmitter by applying phase shifts to incoming signals before they are reflected. This paper explores a unipolarized RIS with linear shape aimed at transmitting a common signal to multiple user equipments (UEs) spread across a wide angular region. To achieve uniform coverage, the uni-polarized RIS is designed to emit a broad and spectrally-efficient beam featuring a spatially flat-like array factor, diverging from the conventional narrow beam approach. To achieve this objective, we start by deriving probabilistic lower and upper bounds for the average spectral efficiency (SE) delivered to the UEs. Leveraging the insights from the lower bound, we focus on optimizing the minimum value of the power domain array factor (PDAF) across a range of azimuth angles from −π/2 to π/2 . We employ the continuous genetic algorithm (CGA) for this optimization task, aiming to improve the SE delivered to the UEs while also creating a wide beam. Extensive simulation experiments are carried out to assess the performance of the proposed code, focusing on key metrics such as the minimum and average values of the PDAF and the SE delivered to the UEs. Our findings demonstrate that the proposed code enhances the minimum SE delivered to the UEs while maintaining the desired attribute of a broad beam. This performance is notably superior to that of established codes, including the Barker, Frank, Golay, and Chu codes. Moreover, the effectiveness of the proposed code in generating a broad beam is experimentally validated using a RIS prototype.

title: On Taipei MRT Passenger Flow Prediction by Considering Multiple External Factors
authors: Yu-Wen Hung, Yu-Siou Siao, Kuei-Ping Shih
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper aims to address the key challenge in metro passenger flow prediction: accurately capturing the influence of multiple external factors while overcoming the high computational cost of deep learning models. To balance the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, this paper incorporates model compression techniques and proposes a Factor-Specialized Multi-Teacher Knowledge Distillation (FS-MTKD) framework, in which multiple expert teachers are specialized based on different external factors. Unlike traditional single-model approaches that attempt to learn all factor relationships simultaneously, this paper designs multiple expert teacher models, each dedicated to capturing the impact of a single external factor on passenger flow. Through Knowledge Distillation (KD), a lightweight student model is guided to absorb the heterogeneous knowledge distilled from all expert teachers and to achieve effective balancing among multiple influencing factors. The experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. After applying FS-MTKD, the student model achieves prediction accuracy comparable to that of more complex teacher models, while significantly reducing both model parameters and computational cost.

title: Exploration of Optimal Conditions for a Simple Anisotropy Control on Thin-film Element Using Joule Heating
authors: Hiroaki Kikuchi, Shuto Kawasaki, Haruhi Isomura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: We have proposed a simple method for controlling magnetic anisotropy in thin-film magnetic devices by combining Joule heating with permanent magnets. For thin-film magnetoimpedance elements, we confirmed the possibility of controlling anisotropy using Joule heating alone, and analyzed—based on FEM simulations—the differences in properties between Joule heating alone and Joule heating combined with permanent magnets. Furthermore, we experimentally demonstrated that by alternately applying Joule heating alone and Joule heating combined with permanent magnets, it is possible to achieve magnetoimpedance properties close to those obtained by conventional annealing under a static magnetic field. This method also has the potential to provide improved controllability.

title: Effectivity of Exposure Therapy Simulations in Video Games on Mitigating Social Anxiety
authors: Jasmine L. Gallaron, Marvi G. Fetiluna, Lieudwig A. Abella, Vicente F. Patalita
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study evaluates a 3D game-based simulation designed as a controlled aid for exposure therapy-aligned interventions for social anxiety. The platform integrates a Python-powered facial-emotion recognition (FER) module and IBM Watson-driven dialogue to monitor user mood and dynamically adjust session flow. Grounded in the APA Severity Measure for Social Anxiety Disorder, the research analyzed emotional patterns across varying social scenarios. Results demonstrate that the simulation effectively evokes scenarioappropriate responses: casual interactions yielded higher positive affect, while performance-based tasks-such as public speaking-successfully elicited neutral and negative emotions associated with evaluative stress. Notably, the 6% margin of positive over negative affect demonstrates the system's ability to maintain user engagement without exceeding manageable emotional thresholds. High usability ratings and successful emotional induction indicate the simulation is a viable tool for early-stage exposure-based support.

title: Wavelet Neural Network-based Human Activity Recognition: A Gravity-Aware Hybrid Architecture
authors: Faizal Shanavas Puthiyaveettil, Ramanujam E, Ashutosh Sahoo, Ponnekanty Y Raj Kamal Tutu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using wearable sensors is often restricted by noisy, high-dimensional data. Standard deep learning models frequently fail to capture fine-grained time-frequency details, leading to specific confusion between static postures such as "Sitting" and "Standing". This paper introduce a hybrid model featuring an Auto-Grouped Wavelet Layer that generates 511 features via a Power Set approach, and a dedicated Static Branch using Global Average Pooling. By isolating the gravity vector, the proposed architecture effectively resolves static ambiguity and ensures robust classification accuracy. This study evaluates the proposed architecture on the widely-used UCI Human Activity Recognition (UCI-HAR) dataset,where the proposed WNN-CNN and WNN-LSTM models achieved accuracies of 95% and 93% respectively, demonstrating significant improvements over baseline models.

title: Building a Framework Towards Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Capabilities
authors: Doha Rahma, Mohamed Aborizka, Menna Kamel
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: To the best of our knowledge, this work introduces a framework that combines YOLOv7-based deep learning and real-time object detection with robotic process automation (RPA) to enhance decision-making. The goal is to enable bots to “see,” interpret visual elements, and choose actions dynamically instead of following static workflows. Traditional RPA systems often fail when dealing with unstructured data, unexpected layouts, or multilingual interfaces and usually depend on predefined rules, which leads to increased errors and human intervention. Our approach integrates deep learning for perception, transfer learning for adaptability across domains, and AI-driven decision modules to make automation more flexible and context-aware. Experimental results demonstrate notable improvements in accuracy, efficiency, and recovery from unpredictable scenarios. By linking real-time detection with autonomous decisions, the framework pushes RPA toward scalable, intelligent, and adaptive business process automation.

title: Scenario Equivalent Decoupling-Based Optimal Cost Control Strategy for Power Systems Considering N–1 Security and Uncertainties
authors: Dapeng Wang, Cong Zhang, Huaizhi Yang, Lili He, Long Zeng, Jiayong Li, Lipeng Zhu, Ke Zhou, Lei Xie, Julong Chen, Zhikang Shuai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article proposes a novel N–1 interval optimal power flow (NIOPF) scheme that considers N–1 contingencies and interval uncertainties in renewable generation. Based on the scenario-equivalent decoupling method (SEDM), the NIOPF addresses the problems associated with existing methods, including constraint violations and low solution efficiency. First, the NIOPF model is formulated by integrating security constraints involving interval variables under both N–0 normal and N–1 contingency scenarios, along with scenario transition constraints. Second, a credible domain reduction method (CDRM) based on the Minkowski difference is proposed to handle interval constraints, converting them into tractable deterministic constraints that guarantee the system operates within safe limits across all uncertain scenarios. Then, the SEDM represents numerous N–1 scenarios equivalently as two positive and negative deviation scenarios, effectively reducing the dimensionality of N–1 scenarios. Next, a two-stage optimization model that integrates the N–0 normal operating scenario with the two equivalent deviation scenarios is constructed and solved iteratively to improve computational efficiency. Finally, case studies on the modified IEEE 30-bus system verify that the proposed SEDM ensures constraint satisfaction under uncertainties while improving computational efficiency. Further tests on the modified IEEE 118-bus and PEGASE 1354-bus systems confirm its engineering applicability.

title: Efficient RTL to GDSII Layout Implementation and Verification of UART Protocol Using SystemVerilog and OpenLane
authors: Dhanavath Rajesh, Arvind Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This paper outlines the entire process of designing an ASIC for a Universal Asynchronous Receiver and Transmitter (UART), from RTL to GDSII layout. Yosys begins by creating a gate-level netlist using Verilog's RTL design. Functional verification is thoroughly examined using SystemVerilog testbenches to ensure that it is accurate at the RTL level. The OpenLane toolchain, which includes the OpenROAD application, is used to automate the process of floorplanning, placement, routing, and timing closure for the physical design. The paper goes into more detail about the most important design problems that were faced and talks about possible ways to make things even better. We show simulation results with waveform outputs from different testing scenarios, as well as backend results that show the physical layout and Quality of Results metrics, to prove that the process works effectively, validating the capabilities of the open-source EDA toolchain for producing production-ready ASIC layouts. The importance of each phase evaluated in the ASIC development life cycle is discussed in the concluding sections, along with potential terminology for relating the measurement of design performance and potential solutions for future work that aims to achieve an order of magnitude higher operating frequencies.

title: Adaptive Optimal Output Regulation Based on Adaptive Dynamic Programming and Output-Feedback Hybrid Iteration: Theory and Applications Across Diverse Time Scales From PMDC Motor Drives to IBR-Based Microgrids
authors: Sitong Liu, Masoud Davari, Weinan Gao, Omar Qasem, Tianyou Chai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article investigates the adaptive optimal output-feedback control problem for discrete-time multiagent linear systems. An adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) algorithm, hybrid iteration (HI), is proposed to solve the adaptive optimal output-feedback control problem. The primary contribution of this work is the advancement of an approach to addressing adaptive optimal output regulation problems for multiagent systems via output-feedback control. Unlike existing ADP-based strategies, such as policy iteration (PI) and value iteration (VI), the proposed output-feedback HI strategy learns the optimal output-feedback control policy over two phases. In the first phase, a data-driven scheme is deployed to learn an admissible output-feedback control policy based on online data. In the second phase, given the admissible control policy, the optimal output-feedback control policy is learned via PI with an accelerated convergence rate. Illustrative examples demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed output-feedback HI approach to controlling systems with slow and fast dynamics, including long and short time scales (or time constants), respectively, are provided and studied. In this regard, experimental results from rapid control prototyping of a permanent-magnet DC motor drive utilizing a DC–DC buck converter (as an example of a system with slow dynamics, or equivalently, with long time scales) are presented. Additionally, testing results from power hardware-in-the-loop simulations of a modern microgrid utilizing inverter-based resources (as an example of a system with fast dynamics, or equivalently, with short time scales) reveal the efficacy and practicality of the proposed ADP-based adaptive optimal output regulation employing HI.

title: Period-Doubling Bifurcation in Spatiotemporally Mode-Locked Mid-Infrared Fiber Laser
authors: Hang Ren, Ying Yang, He Cao, Zhanhong Lip, Jun Guo, Jiachen Wang, Jinzhang Wang, Fanlong Dong, Shuangchen Ruan, Chunyu Guo
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: In this paper, to the best of our knowledge, we report the period-doubling bifurcation phenomenon observed in a spatiotemporally mode-locked mid-infrared fiber laser for the first time. The laser is established with a Er3+:ZBLAN fiber. Conventional soliton is generated using the laser under a pump power of 3.66 W, of which the center wavelength, pulse duration, and repetition frequency are 2.8 μm, 300 fs, and 45.02 MHz, respectively. The conventional soliton is switched to period-doubling bifurcation by simply increasing the pump power. We theoretically investigate the mechanism of period-doubling bifurcation. It is found that besides pump power, nonlinear phase shift is also a critical factor for the generation of period-doubling bifurcation in the spatiotemporally mode-locked mid-infrared fiber laser. The work can be informative for understanding the dynamics of spatiotemporally mode-locked fiber lasers.

title: SkyLLM: Enabling Trustworthy UAV RF Surveillance With Foundation Models for Open-World Signal Recognition
authors: Weijie Zhang, Guan Gui, Xianbin Wang, Hikmet Sari, Fumiyuki Adachi
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications
abstract: UAV RF surveillance is becoming a critical layer of low-altitude airspace security, yet most fingerprinting pipelines still assume that every signal belongs to a known class. In open-world deployments, where new platforms, protocol updates, and changing propagation conditions are routine, that assumption makes conventional classifiers brittle and often overconfident on unseen emitters. Foundation models offer a compelling alternative because their pretrained priors can support richer sequence understanding beyond task-specific training. We present SkyLLM, a foundation-model-driven framework that aligns RF time-series with the token space of a frozen LLM through patch-based embedding, cross-channel fusion, and prompt conditioning, so that waveform fragments can be interpreted in a representation space better suited to novelty-aware decision making. Experiments on real UAV RF data show that SkyLLM preserves strong discrimination while delivering markedly more reliable unknown rejection than lightweight baselines across increasingly open settings. More broadly, the study suggests that future trusted spectrum intelligence will depend not only on better classifiers, but on representation layers that remain stable as the airspace evolves. SkyLLM therefore offers a practical path toward 6G-era UAV monitoring systems that must combine recognition, uncertainty awareness, and operational trust.

title: Functional Semantic Inference Method for Industrial Control Proprietary Protocol
authors: Yahui Yang, Yangyang Geng, Qiang Wei, Man Zhou, Zhuo Lv, Liupeng He
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Industrial control protocols (ICPs), especially proprietary protocols, lack public specifications due to security and commercial considerations, posing profound challenges for security audits and vulnerability detection. Although the function code fields in ICPs define critical operational semantics (e.g., PLC start/stop), existing protocol reverse engineering (PRE) methods struggle to accurately infer the functional implications of their value dependencies. To address this critical gap, we propose FuncSeinfer, an operation-traffic correlation-driven method for inferring functional semantics in proprietary ICPs by associating graphical user interface (GUI) operations of programming software with network traffic. FuncSeinfer first employs an entropy-based dynamic field extraction algorithm to generate candidate fields. Subsequently, it applies heuristic rules and clustering scoring rules to accurately infer function code fields. Finally, it identifies semantics through variance analysis of traffic marked with operational labels. We rigorously evaluated FuncSeinfer on 7 widely used ICPs (S7comm, UMAS-A, UMAS-B, Modbus, PCCC, Melsoft, and Fins) in 9 real-world PLCs from 5 leading manufacturers, achieving 100% accuracy in function code field inference, outperforming state-of-the-art PRE tools such as Netzob, FieldHunter, Netplier, and FSIBP. Furthermore, FuncSeinfer can accurately identify the semantic meaning of 78 function codes, which is an average improvement of 20.2% compared to Wireshark. This study fills the gap in functional semantics within ICP reverse engineering, enabling in-depth security analysis without requiring protocol specifications.

title: Real-Time Seat-Based Sitting Action Recognition Using Soft Pressure Sensors and Embedded Machine Learning
authors: Chi Cuong Vu, Viet Hoang Nguyen, Jooyong Kim
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Seat-based sitting behavior monitoring is an important research area due to its impact on spinal health, work performance, and long-term comfort. However, challenges remain in accuracy, stability, cost, deployment flexibility, and data privacy. This paper proposes an integrated system combining soft pressure sensors, embedded machine learning, and a flexible communication architecture. The pressure sensor, fabricated from conductive textile and Au film, achieves a sensitivity of up to 0.46 kPa⁻¹ with response/recovery times of approximately 60/50 ms. The compact machine learning model (4.34 KB) achieves 99.7% accuracy in recognizing six sitting actions, including trunk tilting and sitting transitions. All processing and inference are performed locally on the embedded board, with only classification results transmitted to the cloud to enhance data security. The system focuses on trunk tilt and sitting transition recognition rather than full-body ergonomic posture assessment. These results demonstrate the feasibility of real-time, resource-efficient monitoring of sitting actions on edge IoT devices. This pilot study used data from one participant, with a fixed chair and sensor setup in a single recording session. Therefore, the results show feasibility under controlled conditions, not generalizability across subjects.

title: SpeakBright: A Mobile Application for Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) for Nonverbal ASD Learners
authors: Jessa Donnalyn Reroma, Jovelyn C. Cuizon, Justin Antonio Bolivar, Ethan Ortiz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and speech impairments face significant communication challenges, impacting their development and quality of life. Existing solutions like the Picture Exchange Communication System (PECS) and Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) apps have limitations, such as a lack of emotional expression support and progress tracking. This study introduces SpeakBright, a mobile application which combines traditional methods like PECS and Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) and modern technologies to provide child friendly learning tool with adaptive recommendations, progress tracking, and completion estimation. The application adapts to a child's developmental stage, which allows children to expand their vocabulary, improve sentence formation, and support positive communication habits, enhancing both verbal and non-verbal abilities. Testing results indicate a high level of acceptability by care providers and ASD teachers of SpeakBright's data-driven functionalities in support of educational intervention.

title: Analysis of Historical Speech Based on the Decision-Making Process of Political Leaders Using Natural Language Processing
authors: Varsha Mittal, Narendra Bisht, Kamola Oltiboyeva, Bakhromjan Djalalov, Madirimova Saodatjon Madirimovna, Nafisa Khudayberganova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Historical speeches of political leaders have served as among the most powerful parts in influencing the globe. Speeches of the textual form were imprinted into history. These kinds of conversations have a significant impact on the general population and their activities in the days ahead. Additionally, if allowed unsupervised, political persons or groups may generate severe issues. In numerous situations, there may be an indicator that the governing body has to adjust its regulations and simultaneously attend to the populace. Recognizing the sentimental analysis of a political statement is vital, as they can be preliminary signs or alerts for future worldwide emergencies, alliances, battles, and future wars. This study emphasized the prime ministers of the United States and India, categorizing their speeches according to their emotional content and context. In this study, optimism is a supplementary emotion, while happiness and upset are the main ones. Beyond categorizing the speeches depending on context and emotion, among the primary tasks of this study present a database of political speeches containing 2010 speeches annotated with context-based and emotion-based historical speeches. The speeches that are being focused on are quite lengthy. This study introduces EMPOLITICIAN-Context, an adaptive voting classification technique for context-based categorization, and EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion, an adaptive voting algorithm for emotion categorization of political speeches. The suggested EMPOLITICIAN-Context approach has obtained 73.2% accuracy in the area of context-based categorizing, and the EMPOLITICIAN-Emotion approach accomplished 53.1% accuracy in categorizing the emotion of the historical speeches of political leaders.

title: Towards a Superconducting Magnetic Thermal Modulator for Bolometric Detectors
authors: Enrico Petriella, Matías Hampel, Juan J. Bonaparte, Jesús Bonilla Neira, Gabriel Bosch, Juan M. Geria, Nahuel Müller, Manuel García Redondo, Lucía Sucunza, Luciano Ferreyro, Alan Fuster, Alejandro Almela, Sebastian Kempf, Manuel Platino
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: We present the preliminary development and characterization of a superconducting thermal switch designed to modulate the thermal response of cryogenic bolometers. The device uses an aluminum thermal link whose conductance is modulated by a niobium control coil. Initial measurements confirm the feasibility of the switching principle and demonstrate dynamic operation at least up to 50  kHz. These early results indicate that this approach can shift the signal spectrum away from 1/f noise, with ongoing efforts focused on integration with bolometric sensors and optimizing device geometry.

title: Accurate and Efficient Equivalent Circuit Modeling for Single-Port SAW Resonators
authors: Tinglun Ao, Chen Chen, Guopeng Hui, Haotian Liu, Minglei Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: An optimized equivalent circuit modeling framework for single-port surface acoustic wave (SAW) resonators is proposed in this article, addressing key limitations of conventional Butterworth–Van Dyke (BVD) and modified BVD (MBVD) models in representing spurious resonances and packaging parasitics. It incorporates: 1) simplified parasitic circuits to quantify packaging effects and 2) explicit resonance loops for spurious modes. The extraction of equivalent circuit parameters within this framework is guided by an iterative strategy that combines analytical derivation and least-squares optimization. Model validation across 15 commercial SAW resonators (303–868 MHz) shows significantly improved precision: the fit MBVD model reduces root mean squared error (RMSE) by roughly one order of magnitude, while the extended MBVD (EMBVD) further lowers RMSE by 30%–50%. The parasitic-integrated BVD (PBVD) model achieves high-accuracy packaging characterization with a relative RMSE (RRMSE) of 15.1% and root-mean-squared relative error (RMSRE) of 0.51%. In practice, EMBVD prevents oscillation mode hopping by capturing spurious resonances, while PBVD guides the optimization of devices’ packaging and PCB layout. Furthermore, the extracted equivalent circuit parameters provide clear criteria for sensor-specific resonator selection: balancing sensitivity against resolution for mass-loading sensing, or prioritizing low motional resistance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${R}_{\text {m}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and static capacitance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${C}_{{0}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> to ensure oscillation stability for viscoelastic sensing. The proposed framework is also extendable to two-port SAW resonators, further broadening its applicability. This work thus facilitates the transition from precise resonator characterization to robust system design.

title: Deep Mixture of Experts Network for Resource Optimization in Aerial–Terrestrial CF-mMIMO Systems Under URLLC
authors: Donggen Li, Chong Huang, Jingfu Li, Pei Xiao, Wenjiang Feng, Dusit Niyato, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: As a critical component of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks, ultra-reliable and low-latency communication (URLLC) is expected to support real-time and reliable information exchange in low-altitude environments. However, achieving URLLC often incurs significant resource overhead, including increased bandwidth consumption, higher transmit power, and denser access point (AP) deployment, which pose significant challenges to both spectral efficiency (SE) and energy efficiency (EE). Besides, existing iterative optimization algorithms are computationally intensive and struggle to meet the latency requirements of URLLC. To address these challenges, we propose a hybrid aerial-terrestrial cell-free massive MIMO (CF-mMIMO) network to support diverse services, along with a channel prediction network and a deep mixture of experts (MoE) network for uplink optimization. First, we design a channel prediction network (CP-Net) to mitigate channel aging caused by high-mobility user equipment (UE). CP-Net employs three Transformer-based sub-networks for aged channel state information (CSI) prediction, while a channel quality-aware loss function is introduced to improve the prediction accuracy of weak links. Based on the predicted CSI, we develop a deep MoE network (MoE-Net) for power allocation comprising three expert models targeting different objectives. Then, we introduce a weighted gating network (WT-Net) to learn an efficient adaptive combination of expert outputs. The proposed framework better captures heterogeneous UE requirements and improves communication performance under URLLC constraints. Numerical results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title:
Impact of Oblique Heavy Ion Irradiation on the Transient Response of Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    MODFETs
authors: Gaurav Kumar, Khushwant Sehra, Anupama Anand, Manoj Saxena
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: This manuscript investigates the influence of Single Event Transients triggered by heavy ion irradiation on β - (AlGa)<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MODFETs. A systematic reliability study of Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> MODFETs is carried out by deliberately probing vulnerable device region using particle strikes and by localizing the effects across the Gate – Drain access regions near the quantum well through angled ion strikes. Comparative analysis of the resulting drain current transients reveals an increased sensitivity to angled heavy ion strike towards the drain electrode, leading to a pronounced increase in peak transient current due to enhanced carrier collection at the drain electrode.

title: Self-training Based Source-free Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Shuo Han, Xu Tang, Jingjing Ma, Xiangrong Zhang, Fang Liu, Licheng Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Remote sensing scene classification (RSSC) is crucial for interpreting remote sensing (RS) images across various applications. Existing deep learning methods have promoted RSSC results to a new level. However, most of them rely on an assumption that training and test data are independently and identically distributed. While this requirement can be readily met under laboratory conditions, it proves to be exceptionally stringent in practical settings, as the collected new RS images may differ significantly in distribution from the RS images used to train the classification model. In this cross-domain case, many successful models would be disabled. To address the above challenges and enhance the practicability of the RSSC model, this paper proposes a self-training based source-free domain adaptation (SFDA) method named STSFDA. First, a multi-task source domain pre-training strategy is developed to provide a strong initialization for the SFDA process. Next, a teacher-student network is adopted to drive the SFDA process. In the teacher network, target RS scenes are divided into two groups based on confidence and then passed to the student network. The student network applies supervised learning to reliable samples and unsupervised learning to uncertain samples. A neighbor-guided pseudo-labeling strategy is also introduced to generate more reliable pseudo-labels with the consideration of neighborhood information. In addition, mixup learning and contrastive learning are applied to all target domain samples to obtain more discriminative feature representations. Experimental results demonstrate that STSFDA consistently outperforms many existing baseline models, highlighting its effectiveness and potential for future research in the RS community. Code is available at https://github.com/TangXu-Group/STSFDA.

title: Experimental Study of Timing Synchronization Techniques for Outdoor Mobile MIMO Transmission
authors: Haowen Bai, Hidekazu Murata, Satoshi Suyama, Huiling Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In outdoor mobile environments, our multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) transmission experimental system experiences continuously varying timing offsets between transmitted and received samples. These variations arise from leading and delayed multipath components, as well as changes in radio propagation distance. As a result, timing synchronization fails, resulting in degradation of the bit error rate (BER). In this paper, we consider two synchronization algorithms that estimate and compensate for timing offsets between transmission and reception samples using correlation functions, and evaluate their performance experimentally. The experimental results show that both algorithms can compensate timing offsets in real time and reduce the BER, while one of them achieves a significantly greater improvement.

title: Scalable Energy-Aware Task Scheduling on Heterogeneous Voltage Frequency Islands: From Optimal ILP to Practical Heuristics with Real-World Validation
authors: Sanchit, Sakshi, Maanas Singal, Dheeraj Kumar Dhaked, V.L. Narayanan, Jagpreet Singh
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive approach to energy-efficient DAG scheduling on Voltage Frequency Islands (VFI) architectures, combining optimal Integer Linear Programming (ILP) formulation with a novel Energy-Aware List Scheduling with Dynamic Slot Reuse (EALS-DSR) heuristic. The work addresses the critical challenge of minimizing makespan and overall energy consumption through intelligent task allocation, voltage/frequency assignments, and scheduling optimization. Our contributions include: (1) a detailed ILP formulation with theoretical guarantees for optimal solutions, (2) the EALS-DSR heuristic providing near-optimal solutions with polynomial complexity O(n²p), (3) comprehensive experimental validation on LUD, FFT, and Random DAGs, and (4) real-world molecular dynamics simulation case study. To minimize makespan and overall energy consumption, a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) can be scheduled on voltage frequency islands by first splitting the available processing units into numerous islands with different voltage and frequency levels. This paper presents a comprehensive approach combining Integer Linear Programming (ILP) with a novel Energy-Aware List Scheduling with Dynamic Slot Reuse (EALS-DSR) heuristic. The proposed methodology includes four main components: (a) DAG modeling, (b) job allocation based on slots, (c) voltage/frequency assignments to each slot, and (d) scheduling optimization. We extend the original ILP formulation with theoretical analysis, comprehensive experimental validation, and real-world case studies including molecular dynamics simulation. Results demonstrate that the ILP approach improves performance by 10-24% over existing methods, while EALS-DSR achieves 15-25% energy savings with excellent scalability for large-scale problems. The molecular dynamics case study shows 37.4% energy reduction and 3.86× efficiency improvement, validating practical applicability.

title: Service-based RAN Control Plane Decoupling Based on the Fusion of Protocol Semantics and Topological Features
authors: Shangyuan Du, Xin Sun, Chunjing Yuan, Zecheng Fang, Lin Tian
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: To address the diversified scenario requirements and stringent real-time challenges of Beyond 5G/6th Generation (B5G/6G) networks, this paper focuses on the problem of insufficient scalability caused by the tight coupling of control plane (CP) functions in traditional radio access network (RAN), and proposes a service-based RAN CP decoupling method based on the fusion of protocol semantics and topological features. First, this study establishes a Context-Operation-State component model to define atomic service units, and introduces a Text Structure Fusion Architecture (TSFA) to extract the fused representation of component semantics and structural interactions. To overcome the limitations of general pre-trained models in understanding vertical domain terminology, this study constructs a communication domain specific dataset named 3rd Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) Protocol Semantic Matching (3GPP-PSM), and fine-tunes the Qwen3-Embedding model via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). Finally, the hierarchical clustering algorithm is applied to generate service units with different granularities. Comprehensive experiments conducted using metrics including the elbow method, silhouette coefficient, and latency increment demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms both the single-feature baselines and existing decoupling schemes significantly. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method not only achieves the goal of “high cohesion and low coupling” but also effectively mitigates the latency overhead induced by CP function splitting. This study provides theoretical and technical support for the softwarization and service-based architecture (SBA) of 6G networks as well as the “white-box” transformation of base stations, thereby promoting the evolution of wireless networks toward flexibility, efficiency, and intelligence.

title: National Innovation Policy: Transforming to Knowledge Economy
authors: Hossam Osman
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)

title: A Comparative Performance Analysis of Fog-Assisted vs. Cloud-Only Architectures for a Real-Time Mood-Aware IoT System
authors: Prashanth Ragam, Harathi Chavala, Ashish Nerusu, Sai Ganesh Reddy Venna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Real-time internet of Things (IoT) applications based on continuous multimodal inputs, such as EEG and camera streams intended for mood detection, put demanding requirements in terms of latency, efficient bandwidth usage, and controlled energy consumption. Traditional Cloud-Only architectures are not suitable to meet these requirements due to the long network path and high transmission load involved. This paper presents a performance comparison between Fog-Assisted and Cloud-Only deployment models for a real-time mood-aware IoT system using iFogSim. Two scenarios are simulated by varying module placement: (1) Fog-Assisted, where computation happens on a local fog node, and (2) Cloud-Only, where processing will be offloaded completely onto the cloud. Key performance indicators-measured end-to-end latency, network usage, and total energy consumption under identical conditions-demonstrate that the Fog-Assisted model significantly reduces latency and WAN bandwidth usage and presents more efficient system-level energy consumption for continuous real-time workloads. These results highlight the suitability of fog architectures for intelligent, delay-sensitive IoT applications, and the presented work will lay the ground for future enhancements, including adaptive module placement, mobility support, and AI-driven edge inference. In comparison to the Cloud-Only model, the Fog-Assisted model demonstrated a 98% reduction in latency (4 ms vs 212 ms), an 80% reduction in network usage (10,000 units vs 50,000 units), and a 99.6% reduction in energy consumption (1.67 × 10 Ws vs 4.45 × 10 Ws). These results confirm fog computing’s suitability for intelligent and delay-sensitive applications and show its obvious superiority for real-time mood-aware IoT workloads. This work also lays the groundwork for future studies on AI-driven edge intelligence, mobility-aware processing, and adaptive module placement.

title: iNoodle: An Artificial Intelligence Seasoning-Packet Recognition System Applied to Packaged Dried Noodle Production Line
authors: Wan-Jung Chang, Ping-Ying She, Chia-Yu Wu, Jian-Ping Su
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This paper proposes a seasoning-packet recognition system based on an AI-based deep learning model, called iNoodle. By designing an inspection chamber that integrates a camera module and an AI-edge platform with an inverted U-shaped base, iNoodle can be easily and cost-effectively deployed on a packaging line to detect abnormal quantities and types of seasoning packets. Experimental results demonstrate that iNoodle effectively and accurately identifies types and quantities of seasoning packets, enabling dried noodle manufacturers to eliminate the need for manual inspection and decrease inspection errors, thereby reducing production costs and improving product quality.

title: A Photovoltaic and Battery-Driven Full-range Buck and Boost DC-DC Converters for the Drone Wireless Power Transmission System
authors: Mahmoud F. Elmorshedy, Seshagiri Rao Vemparala, M. S. Bhaskar, Dhafer Almakhles, Md Rabiul Islam, Kashem M Muttaqi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Drones are increasingly employed for a wide range of applications, including military surveillance along national borders and the maintenance of telecommunications infrastructure in remote locations and inaccessible regions, where ensuring uninterrupted operation remains a major challenge due to the lack of reliable charging infrastructure. To address this limitation, this article proposes and experimentally validates an integrated three-port photovoltaic (PV)-battery-wireless power transfer (WPT) charging station for autonomous drone recharging in off-grid environments. PV panels are cost effective and sustainable power source for remote regions with WPT technology. The architecture combines solar PV generation with battery energy storage through coordinated DC-DC power conversion to provide a regulated DC-link for a high-frequency WPT interface, enabling contactless and maintenance-free drone charging. To maximize system efficiency and ensure optimal PV energy harvesting under variable irradiance conditions, the maximum power point tracking (MPPT) technique is adopted for the PV panels, while a closed-loop proportional-integral controller ensures the DC voltage remains stable at the desired level under dynamic load and source variations. The battery energy management system enables bidirectional power flow by managing charging and discharging cycles, to buffer solar intermittency and ensure uninterrupted power delivery to the WPT subsystem. The performance of the complete system, including system design, control architecture, and power management strategy, is validated through detailed simulations and experimental results obtained from a laboratory-scale prototype. The results demonstrate robust dynamic performance, fast MPPT convergence, stable DC-link regulation, and reliable wireless power delivery, thereby confirming the feasibility and effectiveness of the hybrid PV-battery-powered WPT charging station for sustained drone operation in remote and challenging environments.

title: A Novel Quality Model for Education Management Information Systems: Insights From the Openemis Case Study in Jordan
authors: Ahmad Althunibat, Addelrahim Emad Mehyar, Seif hashem Alazzam, Naif Alzriqat, Bassam M. Al-mahadeen
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: Education Management Information Systems (EMIS) in modern educational administration, highlighting challenges such as rapid data processing, user accessibility, and advanced analysis tools that limit user satisfaction and data-driven decisionmaking. A mixed-methods approach was employed, involving a questionnaire completed by 519 EMIS users and semistructured interviews with 15 experts from organizations like UNESCO and the Jordanian Ministry of Education. Results indicated a need for improvements in real-time data processing and user accessibility, with statistical analysis showing these factors account for 74.7 % of the variance in user satisfaction. The study demonstrated strong reliability with a Cronbach's Alpha of 0.928, and user satisfaction ratings improved from 2.95 to 3.7 after implementing a proposed quality framework. Despite positive findings, experts emphasized resource constraints and the necessity for ongoing user education. The research aims to enhance EMIS, making it more user-friendly and responsive in educational management.

title: Blood Procurement with Prescriptive Analytics and Deep Learning Method
authors: Xiaoke Liang, Yusen Xia, Xiaojun Wang, Zheng Luo, Xu Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Blood is a scarce and valuable resource in healthcare, making the effective management of blood inventory a persistent challenge for blood centers and hospitals due to irregular supply and heterogeneous demand. To address severe blood shortages, this study focuses on platelets as a representative case. It employs data-driven prescriptive analytics and deep learning to manage inventory under uncertain supply and prioritized demand, where emergency demand takes precedence over regular demand. First, the prescriptive analytics approach combines a local learning predictive method with optimization to directly predict order quantities based on historical demand and observed features, utilizing a distribution-free, one-step machine learning algorithm. Second, the deep neural network approach utilizes historical demand and feature data to build a neural network that produces optimal ordering decisions. Both approaches are evaluated against conventional methods through numerical experiments using real data from a regional blood center. Extensive multi-year out-of-sample evaluations demonstrate that prescriptive analytics and deep learning consistently outperform traditional data-driven methods across multiple performance metrics.
