Their relative performance evolves with dataset size: prescriptive analytics approaches exhibit strong robustness under limited data, whereas deep learning achieves superior cost efficiency as historical data accumulates. The results remain stable under varying cost parameters and feature configurations, confirming the robustness and scalability of the proposed framework. Feature selection is also crucial for improving algorithm performance. The findings provide both theoretical and practical contributions by offering insights into demand behavior and supporting cost-efficient, patient-centered blood inventory management strategies.

title: Stretchable AgNP/AgNW Co-Deposited Conductive Fiber with PDMS Encapsulation for Patellar Tendon Reflex Strain Sensing
authors: Ying-Nien Chou, Po-Chun Huang, Cheng-You Chi, Kai-Sheng Hsieh, Wan-Jung Chang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This study presents a stretchable conductive fiber designed for dynamic patellar tendon reflex sensing, fabricated through AgNP/AgNW co-deposition on alkali-treated polyurethane (PU) fibers. The alkali pretreatment promotes silver ion absorption and enhances insitu nucleation of AgNPs, while subsequent AgNW deposition forms a continuous conductive network with improved mechanical compliance. To further enhance durability, a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) protective layer is applied, providing resistance against moisture, oxidation, and repeated bending during knee-tapping tests. When the patellar tendon is stimulated, the involuntary muscle contraction induces strain on the fiber, resulting in an immediate resistance change that can be used to quantify reflex onset, peak response, and response latency. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed conductive fiber exhibits stable electromechanical performance and reliable signal output during repetitive knee-tapping events, indicating its potential for wearable biomedical sensing applications and neuromuscular function evaluation.

title: Character MBTI for Quantifying Narrative Atmosphere in Novel Recommendation
authors: Jikang Zhang, Yusong Zhou, Yuanyuan Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: Current novel recommendation systems often fail to capture the subtle narrative atmosphere that emerges from a story's characters and interactions. To address this limitation, we introduce a computational framework that quantifies narrative atmosphere using character Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) profiling. The framework uses a character influence model that considers the quantity of dialogue and the semantic importance of speech, as measured by Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF), to weigh character profiles derived from multi-model fusion. Our experiments demonstrate that conventional evaluation metrics are not well-suited to subjective tasks such as this one. Therefore, we propose an alternative evaluation approach based on Kendall's coefficient and paired win counts. It identifies the most effective models and reveals the limitations of simple averaging fusion strategies. Overall, this study offers a fresh perspective on deep text comprehension and provides a more reliable method for evaluating subjective ranking tasks.

title: Next-Generation Biosensing: A Review on TFET-Based Sensors with Machine Learning (ML) Integration
authors: V.Karutharaja, N.B.Balamurugan, G.Vijaya Lakshmi, K.Kokulavani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this review, summarizes the physics and device topologies of Tunnel FET-based biosensors, dielectric-modulation techniques, Two dimensional material platforms, manufacturing challenges and performance measures. Next, we discuss the growing integration of Artificial Intelligent (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) with biosensor data streams in order to improve the processing signal, false positive, false negative rejection and to achieve rapid classification and quantification of analytes. TFETs have emerged as a strong platform for ultra-low-power, highly sensitive label-free biosensing owing to their steep subthreshold swing and band-to-band tunneling characteristics. Finally, we assess outstanding challenges and future approaches for combining sophisticated machine learning pipelines with advancements in TFET technology to accelerate translational biosensing applications.

title: APEX-IDS: Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System Using Deep Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Mostafa Mohamed, Saad M. Ibrahim, Amr A. Abohany, Muhammad Mahmoud
publish: 2025 35th International Conference on Computer Theory and Applications (ICCTA)
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has created unprecedented cybersecurity challenges, necessitating advanced intrusion detection systems capable of identifying sophisticated attack patterns in real-time. This paper presents APEX-IDS (Advanced Performance Excellence IoT Detection System), a novel deep learning framework that integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, and explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques for comprehensive IoT intrusion detection. Our multi-layered architecture combines spatial and temporal feature extraction with attention mechanisms to achieve superior detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency for edge deployment. Extensive evaluation on multiple benchmark datasets including CICIoT2023, UNSW-NB15, ToN_IoT, NSLKDD, and IoT-23 demonstrates exceptional performance with 99.23 % accuracy, 98.87 % precision, 99.15 % recall, 99.01 % F1score, and 0.996 AUC. The integration of SHAP and LIME explainability techniques provides transparent decision-making capabilities essential for operational deployment. Statistical significance testing confirms APEX-IDS superiority over existing approaches (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.001$</tex>), while maintaining practical deployment feasibility with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 3 m s}$</tex> detection time and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5. 2 M B}$</tex> memory footprint.

title: SUROY TA!: A Virtual City Simulator Exploring Historical Landmarks in Cebu City
authors: MA. Angelica Elimanco, Vicente Patalita, Temothy Homecillo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: This project introduces an immersive virtual tourism platform that addresses the limitations of current services like Google Street View and YouTube 360 by offering real-time 3D exploration, spatial audio, and virtual tour narration. Focused on key landmarks in Cebu City, the platform enables users to freely navigate interactive environments enhanced with cultural and historical context. Designed for both VR and desktop computers, it prioritizes accessibility, educational value, and user engagement. By combining advanced graphics, virtual guides, and optimized asset use, the system provides a scalable and inclusive alternative to traditional tourism experiences.

title: Sparse Matrix Accelerator Based on Dynamic Pruning
authors: Mei-Yun Chen, Yu-Cheng Fan, Ching-Min Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: To accelerate model computation, this paper presents a dynamic token selector based on Vision Transformer, focusing primarily on pruning operations using a self-attention mechanism. To enable the model to adaptively retain key tokens and discard unimportant ones based on the importance of the input data, accordingly decreasing computational load and increasing efficiency, a module is added after the Softmax operation to dynamically identify and retain important tokens, directly using pre-calculated attention as the pruning basis, resulting in simpler hardware.

title: Service Orchestration Strategy in STINs: A Multi-Task SFC Mapping Approach
authors: Xiaojing Fan, Huachun Zhou, Tao Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: 3GPP NTN enables deploying network functions in space. However, existing service orchestration frameworks and service function chain (SFC) mapping methods struggle to meet the flexible orchestration of diverse services in satellite-terrestrial integrated networks (STINs). To address this challenge, we propose SOFTIN, a three-plane service orchestration framework based on software-defined networking (SDN) and network function virtualization (NVF). The framework abstracts the mapping from user requirements to service functions into an orchestration workflow. Within SOFTIN, the SFC orchestration problem is formulated as an integer nonlinear programming (INLP) model, and a single SFC mapping algorithm based on double deep Q-network (DDQN) is designed to minimize deployment overhead. To enhance scalability and convergence stability while supporting service diversity and concurrency, we further propose a multi-task parallel mapping algorithm, termed multi-task graph neural network-based actor–critic with proximal policy optimization (MT-GACP). This algorithm integrates a graph neural network (GNN)-based representation learning network with a proximal policy optimization (PPO) module to mitigate convergence oscillations caused by topology changes. Moreover, multiple reinforcement learning agents manage SFC requests for multiple tasks. Finally, experiments on a Linux prototype system validate the SOFTIN framework's feasibility and orchestration capabilities. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithms achieve near-optimal performance across multiple metrics, such as placement success rate and resource utilization, and demonstrate excellent scalability in both multi-layer satellite networks (MLSN) and the Iridium network.

title: Hybrid Ensemble Learning for High-Precision DDoS Attack Detection in IoT Networks Using the INDDOS24 Dataset
authors: Usman Mustafa, Minahil Kamran, Muhammad Yousaf Rafique, Alina Akhtar, Ashas Mushtaq, Umer Majeed
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Machine Learning, and Intelligence (DataSciMI)
abstract: The rapid growth of the Internet of Things (IoT) resulted in several connected gadgets in the world never seen before, expected to continue to tens of billions by 2025. Although the development of IoT is efficient in smart homes, industrial automation, and healthcare, there is also an increase in the attack surface that cyber threats can attack. IoT devices are also being used more as a large-scale botnet in Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. In recent times, there have been reports of multi-terabit DDoS attacks, novel IoT botnets (e.g., Mirai-based) with the ability to generate record-breaking volumes of traffic. Conventional signature-based systems (e.g., Snort, Cisco Firepower) have a hard time resisting such advanced attacks. In response to this, we will elaborate a hybrid machine-learning ensemble model based on new INDDOS24 data (an IoT DDoS benchmark). Our voting ensemble is soft (e.g., tuned classifiers, such as XGBoost, Random Forest, and a neural network) to obtain high detection precision. We also conduct a lot of data preprocessing and feature engineering on INDDOS24, use SHAP to have interpretability, and compare our results with the state of the art. Our model achieves more than 98% accuracy and F1-score, which is better than single-model baselines. Explanation analysis displays the important flow characteristics (e.g., the rate of packets, the duration of flows) upon which decisions are made. We comment on deployment efficiency (latency of the inference less than a second) and robustness, in comparison to the legacy IDS benchmarks, and describe their practical implication. Federated and transfer learning methods to real-time IoT security are also to be worked on in the future.

title: When Discourse Meets Behavior: Cross-Platform and Multilingual Fandom Dynamics during High-Impact Events
authors: Rian Wagner Costa, Lívia Dâmaso, Carlos Henrique Ferreira, Carolina Xavier, Vinícius Vieira

title: Statistical Delay Performance of STAR-RIS-Aided Short-Packet NOMA Systems for xURLLC Services
authors: Zixuan Xu, Xiong Li, Jin Xie, Qimei Cui, Xiaofeng Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The integration of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and simultaneously transmitting and reflecting re configurable intelligent surfaces (STAR-RIS) in short-packet communications offers a promising solution to meet the strin gent requirements of next-generation ultra-reliable low-latency communications (xURLLC). However, existing studies primarily focus on average performance metrics, which fail to capture the statistical characteristics of network performance. To address this gap, this paper proposes a novel STAR-RIS-assisted NOMA xURLLC network architecture that incorporates a stochastic network calculus (SNC)-based theoretical framework for statis tical delay performance analysis. Moreover, we adopt a novel correlated phase shift model for STAR-RIS, considering two distinct phase shift configuration strategies. Then, we derive the upper-bounded delay violation probability (DVP) for both phase shift configuration strategies. Extensive simulations validate the effectiveness of our theoretical analysis and comprehensively evaluate system performance under various network parameters, offering some insights for the STAR-RIS-assisted NOMA xURLLC system.

title: SilentNoise: Non-interactive Noise Generation for Differential Privacy with Malicious Security_supp1-3694826.pdf
authors: Reo Eriguchi
publish: N/A
abstract: Differential privacy (DP) is a standard notion of privacy-preserving data analysis. Traditionally, DP has been studied in the central model, where a trusted curator collects all private inputs and releases aggregation results preserving DP. However, this model creates a single point of failure, as all private data are held by a single party. We aim to decentralize additive noise DP mechanisms by leveraging secure multiparty computation (MPC) techniques. The main technical challenge is how to obliviously sample secret noise from specific probability distributions in the presence of malicious parties. Most existing maliciously secure protocols require multiple rounds of interaction, resulting in high communication and computation overheads. Moreover, the only known maliciously secure, non-interactive protocol suffers from a utility loss that grows exponentially with the number of computing parties. In this work, we propose <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SilentNoise</small>, a general framework for maliciously secure, non-interactive protocols for sampling DP noise, and present several instantiations that improve utility and efficiency both theoretically and empirically. In our protocols, any incorrect noise contributed by malicious parties is rejected and meaningful utility is guaranteed whenever the protocols succeed. After a one-time interactive setup that can be amortized over multiple uses, parties can non-interactively generate an unbounded number of shares of noise. This leads to a significant improvement in efficiency compared to the previous interactive protocols. Furthermore, our protocols improve the utility loss of the prior maliciously secure, non-interactive protocol by an exponential factor in the number of computing parties. They can even achieve a constant utility loss that is comparable to central-model DP mechanisms, under a strong honest-majority setting.

title: LIDSS: A Latent Information-Driven Dynamic Framework for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Yuan Yuan, Deping Zhang, Bingfeng Luo, Yaozhong Luo, Shaopeng Liu, Xu Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Semi-supervised learning has emerged as a promising approach to address the challenge of scarce high-quality annotations in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) segmentation. However, existing methods often suffer from limited segmentation accuracy due to difficulties in effectively leveraging labeled data, which stems from low tissue contrast, ambiguous anatomical boundaries, and a lack of reliable supervision in unlabeled regions. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel latent information-driven dynamic semi-supervised segmentation (LIDSS) framework. LIDSS incorporates three key components: an enhanced boundary refinement module (EBRM) to improve discriminative feature extraction along tissue borders, a confidence-guided adaptive replacement module (CGAR) that enhances feature learning robustness through an adaptive fusion mechanism, and a self-contrasting auxiliary training strategy to minimize discrepancies between dual decoders. Extensive experiments on the PROMISE12 and ACDC benchmark datasets demonstrate that LIDSS achieves superior segmentation accuracy and robustness compared to state-of-the-art semi-supervised segmentation methods.

title: paraDOX™: A New Photonic Switching Architecture
authors: Jose Garcia Echeverria, Matthew Posner, David Rolston
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: ParaDOX<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TM</sup> <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{\text{TM}}$</tex>—parallel Digital Optical Cross (X) Connect is an entirely new packet switching architecture that scales Radix to thousands of ports by using distributed photonic technology. paraDOX <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{\text{TM}}$</tex> still targets <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 / 100 \mathrm{G}+$</tex> Ethernet protocols while also maintaining the data center network interconnects, servers and GPU nodes.

title: DFA-FPN: Deformable Feature Aggregation with Dual Attention for Polyp Detection
authors: Minghui Lv, Yu Hu, Jingjing Xiao, Xin Tao, Yongjian Nian, Hao Fan, Jianyun Zhou, Xinghui Dong, Xia Xie
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The detection of colorectal cancer (CRC) relies heavily on the accurate detection of polyps during colonoscopy. However, conventional Feature Pyramid Networks (FPNs) face critical limitations in handling the diverse morphology, spatial misalignment, and low-contrast boundaries of polyps. To address these challenges, we propose DFA-FPN, a novel deformable feature aggregation framework with dual attention mechanisms. Our approach integrates three key innovations: (1) deformable convolutions that dynamically adapt sampling positions to match polyp contours, (2) a lightweight dynamic alignment module to resolve spatial misalignment during multi-scale feature fusion, and (3) a dual attention mechanism combining local boundary refinement and global context modeling to enhance lesion saliency. The proposed modular design offers a plug-and-play solution for enhancing existing computer-aided diagnosis pipelines with minimal computational overhead. Extensive experiments on the PolypSets benchmark demonstrate that replacing original FPN modules in four mainstream detectors (RetinaNet, ATSS, FCOS, ATSS DyHead) with DFA-FPN yields a consistent 5.95% average improvement in AP@0.5, validating its effectiveness across different detection frameworks.

title: Cross-Domain Vision-Language Transfer Learning for Surgical Drain Detection in Real-World Postoperative Images
authors: Ashok Choudhary, Frank G. Lee, Ellen L. Larson, Cornelius A. Thiels, Hojjat Salehinejad
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate detection of surgical drains in postoperative patientsubmitted images is vital for monitoring recovery and preventing complications. We present a transfer learning approach using vision models pretrained with natural language supervision to identify drains in real-world surgical images. Multiple architectures are fine-tuned on a diverse dataset of patient-submitted photos. Results highlight model performance trade-offs and provide insights toward developing automated systems for postoperative surgical site monitoring.

title: Pathology-Centric Region Exchange: Joint Carotid Plaque Segmentation and Vulnerability Assessment in Ultrasound
authors: Xuanqi Huang, Bodong Du, Xinrui Zhou, Xiaomeng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Automated plaque segmentation and vulnerability assessment of carotid ultrasound images plays a critical role in stroke prevention and clinical risk stratification. However, developing robust models for this joint task is hindered by limited annotated dataset and sparse supervisory signals of classification task. In this paper, we propose our Pathology-Centric Region Exchange framework to address the task in CSV 2026 Challenge: Carotid Plaque Segmentation and Vulnerability Assessment in Ultrasound in ISBI 2026. Specifically, we utilize unlabeled data through a pathology-centric region exchange strategy, swapping plaque-centric patches between labeled and unlabeled samples across longitudinal and transverse views to preserve discriminative morphological cues. Subsequently, we employ a region-aligned loss to mitigate pseudo-label uncertainty in critical pathological regions and optimize joint supervision. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves segmentation accuracy and classification performance compared with existing semi-supervised baselines. As a technical report for the challenge, this paper elaborates on our methodology and experimental findings in detail. The code will be made publicly available upon acceptance.

title: Data Driven Framework for Anomaly Detection and Inter Subsystem Correlation in CubeSat Telemetry
authors: Mustapha Ahricha, Slimane Bah, Zouhair Guennoun
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: CubeSats have become an important platform for low-cost space missions by integrating multiple tightly coupled subsystems whose interactions strongly affect mission performance and reliability. These nanosatellites continuously generate massive volumes of telemetry data that are used to monitor subsystems health. Most existing studies analyze telemetry data at the level of individual subsystems, which limits the understanding of system-level interactions and dependencies. This paper proposes a data-driven framework to investigate inter-subsystem relationships in CubeSat platforms using telemetry data. The approach first applies a threshold-based Out-Of-Limits anomaly detection method to identify abnormal operational periods. Temporal windowing is then performed around detected anomalies, followed by multi-subsystem data fusion. Cross-subsystem dependencies are quantified using the Pearson correlation coefficient, enabling the analysis of statistical relationships between telemetry parameters from different subsystems. The framework is evaluated using telemetry data from five CubeSat subsystems: Electrical Power System, Attitude Determination and Control System, Communications, Flight Computer, and Payload Controller. The results show that meaningful correlation patterns emerge during abnormal operating conditions, particularly between the electrical Power System and the attitude Determination and Control System. The temporal behavior of these correlations highlights dynamic interaction mechanisms within the CubeSat architecture. The proposed framework offers a lightweight and interpretable approach for system-level telemetry analysis and provides a structured basis for building well-prepared datasets for future anomaly detection models without relying on detailed physical models or labeled fault data.

title: Evanescent Germanium-on-Silicon Waveguide Sensors for Therapeutic Monitoring
authors: David J. Rowe, Siyu Chen, Daniel R. Owens, Callum J. Stirling, Pin Dong, Graham T. Reed, Milos Nedeljkovic, Saul N. Faust, Goran Z. Mashanovich
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: Mid-infrared germanium-on-silicon waveguide sensors were designed and fabricated for therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM) using absorption spectroscopy of dried samples. Tunable diode laser absorption spectroscopy was employed to measure varying concentrations of the anti-seizure medication phenytoin. The drug was characterised both from solutions prepared directly in solvent, achieving a limit of detection of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.33 \text{mg} / \mathrm{L}$</tex>, and from samples extracted from spiked human serum using liquid-liquid extraction, where the minimum prepared concentration of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 ~ m g} / \mathbf{L}$</tex> was accurately detected. These results meet or exceed the therapeutic window of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 0 ~ m g} / \mathbf{L}$</tex> for phenytoin and represent an important step toward enabling TDM directly on serum from patients undergoing treatment.

title: Forecasting Future Anatomies: Longitudinal Brain MRI-TO-MRI Prediction
authors: Ali Farki, Elaheh Moradi, Deepika Koundal, Jussi Tohka
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Predicting future brain state from a baseline magnetic resonance image (MRI) is a central challenge in neuroimaging, and has important implications for studying neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease (AD). Most existing approaches predict future cognitive scores or clinical outcomes, such as conversion from mild cognitive impairment to dementia. Instead, here we investigate longitudinal MRI image-to-image prediction that forecasts a participant's entire brain MRI several years into the future, intrinsically modeling complex, spatially distributed neurodegenerative patterns. We implement and evaluate five deep learning architectures (UNet, U<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }^{2}$</tex>-Net, UNETR, Time-Embedding UNet, and ODEUNet) on two longitudinal cohorts (ADNI and AIBL). Predicted follow-up MRIs are directly compared with the actual follow-up scans using metrics that capture global similarity and local differences. The best performing models achieve high-fidelity predictions, and all models generalize well to an independent external dataset, demonstrating robust crosscohort performance. Our results indicate that deep learning can reliably predict participant-specific brain MRI at the voxel level, offering new opportunities for individualized prognosis.

title: A Machine Learning (ML)-Aided Design Automation Framework for Rapid Parameter Optimization in Modular Multilevel Converters
authors: Youngkeun Kim, Sooan Pack, Yeonho Jeong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel machine learning (ML)- aided design automation framework for optimizing arm inductor (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">arm</inf>) and sub-module (SM) capacitor (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sm</inf>) parameters in modular multilevel converter (MMC). The proposed framework formulates the parameter selection problem as a constrained sequential decision-making task and employs a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) agent to directly search the admissible design space. A hierarchical space-partitioning strategy is first applied to construct a feasible set by enforcing resonance and voltage regulation constraints. A modified nodal analysis (MNA)-based time-domain circuit simulation environment is integrated with the DRL agent to evaluate key performance metrics, including output voltage regulation, circulating current magnitude, and arm current ripple characteristics. A multi-objective cost function is designed to balance these coupled performance indices, enabling autonomous convergence toward an optimized parameter region. The derived parameters from the developed design framework are validated through MATLAB simulations by comparison with conventional expert-designed values.

title: Renewable Energy Forecasting for Green Hydrogen Production and Photovoltaic Power Using Hybrid Models and Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Falong Cheng, Zhimin Mao, Jie Xu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The global energy landscape is swiftly transitioning to cleaner, lower-carbon power generation, driving the need for alternative energy sources. Green hydrogen (produced via water electrolysis powered by solar energy, wind energy, biomass energy, and other renewable sources) has emerged as one of the key factors in replacing the traditional energy structure, which is characterized by instability and high costs. This article presents a regional integrated energy system that employs a hybrid electric-hydrogen storage setup, combining battery and hydrogen storage. This system addresses the instability of solar power consumption and enables a combined electricity and hydrogen energy supply on the user side.The abundant solar energy resources in spring and summer are stored through this hybrid energy storage system and released when the autumn and winter light is insufficient, achieving seasonal regulation of clean energy consumption. The model focuses on economic and environmental protection research.The goal is to obtain the optimal solution through intelligent algorithms, and to demonstrate the application value and prospects of hybrid energy storage through forms such as energy cascade utilization, seasonal analysis, and key influencing factor analysis.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Energy-Efficient Traversability Prediction in Natural Terrain Using Proprioception
authors: Afonso E. Carvalho, David Portugal, Paulo Peixoto
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: A key step for successful navigation in outdoor environments is traversability analysis - the ability to compute the feasibility and/or effort associated with the traversal of a certain portion of terrain. Traversability analysis is often hindered by multiple factors, such as the unstructured nature of the environment and sensor occlusion, rendering the estimates inaccurate. We propose P-STEER, a method to analyze the long-term traversability of a ground robot considering its proprioceptive data. Using the robot’s instantaneous current draw while traveling through previously observed terrain, the system is able to reliably learn and accurately predict future traversability estimates, enabling autonomous robots to operate in large-scale environments with increased effectiveness. Our method is evaluated experimentally using a quadruped robot, in real-world natural settings, including a dense forest. We compare it to a state-of-the-art approach and show that it outperforms it in both known and unseen rough terrain conditions. The open-source software is available at https://github.com/Forestry-Robotics-UC/P-STEER.

title: GD-NeRF: Grid-Based Deblurring for Neural Radiance Fields
authors: Tianhao Deng, Tingting Zhou, Zhiying Yang, Yanjun Ren, Mi Zhu, Hanguang Xiao
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) have demonstrated a remarkable ability to recover geometric structures and material details from multi-view images. However, NeRF typically relies on sharp images for reconstruction, and the rendering quality significantly degrades when handling blurred images. Existing NeRF reconstruction methods based on blurred images require lengthy training times and have limited rendering quality. To address these issues, we propose a grid-based deblurring NeRF method that not only improves reconstruction quality but also significantly shortens the training time. To enhance sampling efficiency, we design an adaptive spatial sampling strategy, which, during the early stages of training, samples over a large range to capture geometric information, while dynamically shrinking the sampling range in later stages to focus on key areas. Furthermore, we employ an exponentially weighted color smoothing strategy to suppress color fluctuations caused by random disturbances, thereby enhancing the visual consistency and stability of the rendered images. Experimental results demonstrate that our method can reconstruct NeRF from blurred images within 50 minutes, and surpasses some advanced methods in terms of rendering quality.

title: Two-Stage Cortical Surface Reconstruction from MRI with Arbitrary Slice Thickness
authors: Zhongliang Liu, Wenxuan Wu, Sijin Yu, Zhuoyan Dai, Xin Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Reconstructing the cortical surface from brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scans is crucial for cortical surface analysis. However, existing cortical surface reconstruction (CSR) methods are mostly tailored for high-resolution MRI with specific parameters, whereas clinical scans often suffer from thicker slices and inconsistent resolutions. To address this, we propose a two-stage deep learning approach for accurate and robust cortical surface reconstruction from MRI with arbitrary slice thickness. In the first stage, we employ an implicit neural representation (INR) super-resolution method with cycle-consistency loss to unify MRI with varying slice thicknesses to a consistent thickness while preserving underlying statistical properties. In the second stage, we progressively guide the model to learn resolution-agnostic features through Progressive Gaussian Resolution Annealing (PGRA) and a dual-branch attention self-distillation mechanism. The proposed method demonstrates robust and generalizable cortical surface reconstruction across varying slice thicknesses on the Developing Human Connectome Project (dHCP) dataset.

title: A Collision-Aware Kinematic Model of the qb SoftHand 2 in CoppeliaSim
authors: Inês Costa, Daniel Rodrigues, Hugo Remelgado, Luís Louro, Eliana Costa e Silva, Estela Bicho
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: Soft anthropomorphic robotic hands offer intrinsic compliance, adaptability, and passive safety, making them well suited for manipulation tasks in unstructured human environments. Their integration into robotic systems is essential to enhance the capabilities of modern anthropomorphic robots and promote acceptance in human–robot collaboration. This paper presents a kinematic and collision-aware simulation model of the qb SoftHand 2 to support human-like manipulation planning, despite the lack of direct joint sensing. A forward kinematic model based on a modified Denavit–Hartenberg formulation and a hierarchical sphere-based hand representation are introduced to enable efficient fingertip pose computation and collision avoidance. The model is implemented in CoppeliaSim and validated through simulated manipulation tasks involving objects with diverse physical properties, demonstrating realistic, collision-free grasping behavior.

title: Development of a Compact Raspberry Pi-Based Automated Desalination System for Small-Scale Community Application
authors: Jerry I. Teleron, Jordan Y. Arpilleda, Jayvie Mae T. Venus, Al D. Horteza
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: This study presents the design and implementation of a Raspberry Pi-based automated desalination system for small-scale and community-level freshwater production. The system integrates thermal desalination, sensor-based monitoring, and embedded control to automate the conversion of seawater into potable water. The proposed system consists of a heating chamber, condensation unit, filtration system, and pump, all coordinated through a Raspberry Pi controller using Python-based automation. Experimental evaluation was conducted to assess system performance in terms of heating efficiency, freshwater production rate, energy consumption, and operational reliability. Results show that the system achieves an average freshwater output of $0.85-1.0 \mathrm{~L}$ per cycle, equivalent to approximately $3.5 \mathrm{~L} /$ hour, with an energy consumption of 0.33 kWh per cycle. The heating subsystem effectively reached $98-100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ within 18-22 minutes, enabling efficient evaporation and condensation. Reliability testing across multiple trials demonstrated stable and uninterrupted operation. Compared to manual desalination methods, the system improves production efficiency while reducing labor requirements. The results indicate that the proposed system is a cost-effective, reliable, and scalable solution for freshwater generation, particularly suitable for remote and small-scale applications. Future work will focus on enhancing energy efficiency and integrating renewable energy sources.

title: Dynamic Group Joint ICA (Dyna-gjICA) Uncovers Neural Mechanisms Underlying Depression with and without Anxiety
authors: Souvik Phadikar, Martini P. Paulus, Rayus Kuplicki, Masaya Misaki, Vince D Calhoun
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multimodal fusion of electroencephalography (EEG) and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) remains challenging because EEG and fMRI differ in structure, and most existing methods compress their dynamics into static summaries. Previously, we developed a group joint independent component analysis (gjICA) framework to jointly decompose EEG and fMRI, but it did not capture spatially evolving fMRI networks. Here, we extend gjICA to dynamic gjICA (Dyna-gjICA) to incorporate spatial dynamic features of fMRI, enabling joint analysis of time-resolved fMRI networks and EEG spectral dynamics. Applied to simultaneous EEG-fMRI data from 65 anxious-depression (AD) and 65 non-anxious-depression (NAD) patients, the model identified one significant subcortical, two dorsalattention, and one language component discriminating AD from NAD. Further AD shows a pattern of increased fMRI loadings coupled with reduced EEG spectral power in subcortical and language domain, indicating an inverse EEGfMRI relationship.

title: Study on Effects of Voltage Harmonics of Electric Propulsion based Ship Power System on Long Range Radar Power System
authors: Bhagam Guruvulu, Vijay Hiralal Bhosale, Suchith Rajagopal, M Sheik Althaf
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Long Range Radars require few Mega Watts of Power for detection and tracking of targets at hundreds or sometimes few thousands of kilo meters away. Ship based Long Range Radars require compact and highly reliable Power System of higher capacity. Power Systems of Ship Based Radars are designed to allow minimum leakage current. Low leakage currents are maintained by using circuit topologies which offer lower leakage currents and components having higher Insulation Resistance (IR). Electric Propulsion Systems inject harmonics into the Ship Power System which in turn affects the performance and reliability of Radar Power System. In this paper details of the voltage harmonics study carried out on Radar Power System are presented.

title: Convolutional Neural Network-Based Equivalent Circuit Model Classification from EIS Data for Battery Degradation Prediction
authors: Chandan Chetri, Latha Anekal, Sheldon Williamson
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning–based framework for the automated classification of equivalent circuit models (ECMs) from electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) data for enhanced lithium-ion battery (LIB) diagnostics. EIS captures key internal electrochemical processes, including charge transfer, diffusion, and double-layer behavior, but conventional ECM identification remains labor-intensive and difficult to scale. To address this challenge, a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) is developed to classify EIS spectra into four ECM categories using features derived from both real and synthetic datasets. The proposed network incorporates hierarchical convolutional layers, batch normalization, dropout, and global average pooling for robust feature learning and generalization. Experimental results demonstrate a high classification accuracy of 98.25% on the test dataset. The predicted ECMs show strong agreement with Nyquist plot characteristics, confirming the accuracy, robustness, and practical relevance of the proposed approach for intelligent battery management systems.

title: Synthesized Ultra-High B-Value Diffusion Images for Characterization of Neurite and Soma Organization
authors: Tenglong Wang, Zhonghua Wan, Yu Xie, Jiahao Yu, Yifei He, Ye Wu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Diffusion MRI (dMRI) is an advanced neuroimaging technique that provides insights into the microstructural characteristics of the brain. However, acquiring high-b-value dMRI data presents significant challenges due to substantial hardware costs, specialized protocols, and the time-consuming nature of the process. Consequently, obtaining high-b-value dMRI data remains a significant challenge in neuroimaging. We propose a novel approach that employs a conditional diffusion model to accurately predict high-b-value images from a series of low-b-value diffusion-weighted images. Our method adaptively models signal decay patterns and captures essential microstructural details. Unlike conventional image generation techniques, our approach does not depend on a specific b -value acquisition protocol, enabling it to adapt to a range of low-b-value inputs. This flexibility significantly enhances its generalization capabilities across different datasets. We validate our method on multiple datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness and broad applicability. The code is available at https://github.com/1nlandempire/UHb-DWI-Synth.

title: Insights into JV and EQE performance metrics of solar cells
authors: Pilik Basumatary, Nilanjeeb Das, Ashish Malik, Satyabrata Guruprasad, Dhriti Sundar Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The External Quantum Efficiency (EQE) or Incident Photon to Current Efficiency (IPCE) measurements complement current-voltage (J-V) analysis by identifying spectral mismatch losses, parasite absorption and recombination pathways. This study examines a pin silicon solar cell under varying chopper frequency and bias voltage during EQE/IPCE measurements. The J-V analysis, under illumination, reveals key performance parameters: J<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sc</inf> of 31.05 mAcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup>, a V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">oc</inf> of 0.56 V, a FF of 62.20%, and a PCE of 10.73%. The low efficiency is attributed to an ideality factor n>2, indicating significant recombination losses. High series resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">s</inf>), likely due to poor contact, bulk resistance, and potential manufacturing defects, limits the performance. The voltage losses and reduced fill factor impact efficiency, while the extracted ideality factor offers insights into charge transport and recombination mechanism. Under forward bias, increased carrier injection enhances recombination, reducing quantum efficiency and the resultant integrated J<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sc</inf>. Conversely, at -0.25 V, the best EQE is observed due to minimized recombination losses and enhanced carrier separation. Notably, chopper frequency variation has a negligible impact on quantum efficiency, indicating fast and efficient charge carrier dynamics in the device. However, the influence of bias voltage and chopper frequency may vary according to the type of device architecture and the material used. This study highlights the influence of bias voltage on EQE measurements, providing deeper insights into solar cell performance. Furthermore, this study provides new insight into how applied bias voltage and chopper frequency modulate the EQE response of a p-i-n silicon solar cell. The work establishes a direct correlation between recombination dynamics, quantified via ideality factor and series resistance, and bias-dependent quantum efficiency.

title: Riva Cytology Classification and Detection: A Lesson on How “None of Us is as Smart as All of Us”
authors: Chris Kang, Vibujithan Vigneshwaran, Nils D. Forkert
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Early and accurate computer-aided detection of abnormal cervical cells in Papanicolaou smear images is essential for preventing cervical cancer, in particular for centers with limited access to expert cytotechnologists. The RIVA Cytology Challenge provides a large-scale, expert-annotated collection of high-resolution Papanicolaou smear images from Hospital Rivadavia, offering the unique opportunity to develop a highly accurate classification and detection model for automated cervical cancer screening. In this work, we describe our solution for the challenge in Track A, which involves both the classification and detection tasks. Our proposed solution achieved the mean Average Precision (mAP@0.50:0.95) score of 0.14183, and demonstrate many strategies, which were found to be successful in progressively improving the test scores, as well as those which failed to do so.

title: Enhanced ECC—AESGCM Based StegoCryptographic Model for Secure Medical Data Transmission in IoT-Healthcare Systems
authors: Md Ozaif, Mahfooz Alam, Fayeza Zobair
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The IoT devices in the medical field have grown rapidly, and this has resulted in secure medical data transmission being a primary requirement. The transmission of sensitive patient data between remote sensors, medical systems, and servers is extremely prone to unauthorized access, manipulation, and privacy loss. This paper introduces a better lightweight stego-cryptographic model that can enhance the security and performance of IoT-based healthcare systems. The model proposes Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman (ECDH) for key exchange and AES-GCM for authenticated encryption, with variance-based adaptive LSB embedding to hide encrypted diagnostic text within medical images. ECC offers enhanced security using shorter keys, lowering the computing cost. AESGCM provides both integrity and confidentiality in one step, whereas adaptive block selection provides a way of high imperceptibility. The experimental evidence demonstrates the PSNR values of over $\mathbf{8 0 ~ d B}$, SSIM values of nearly 1, and $\mathbf{0 ~ B E R}$, which proves that the proposed model is an effective and safe method to transmit medical data.

title: DIViT: Data-to-Image Vision Transformer Framework for Multi-Drives System Open-Switch Fault Diagnosis
authors: Hamza Mansour AL-Hallaj, Amirhosein Gohari Nazari, Mohammad B. Shadmand
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Design and Control of Automation and Drive Systems (IDCD)
abstract: The reliability of three-phase variable frequency drives (VFDs) is critical in mission-critical applications such as electric vehicles and industrial drives, where an undetected open-switch fault (OSF) can lead to catastrophic system secondary failures within milliseconds. While traditional diagnostic methods based on Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) or Park’s Vector Approach are robust, they often suffer from significant detection latencies and struggle under variable load transients. This paper proposes a novel, high-speed diagnostic framework utilizing a lightweight tiny Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture specifically optimized for edge-computing deployment. By mapping normalized three-phase current waveforms into two-dimensional (2D) feature-space “images” the proposed DIViT framework captures global dependencies and subtle harmonic distortions that traditional Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) overlook. Simulation demonstrates that the model achieves a classification accuracy for exact fault localization. Most notably, the system achieves a total diagnostic latency of less than 10 milliseconds seconds enabling fault localization within one-fourth of a fundamental power cycle T/4. This approach effectively bridges the gap between deep learning complexity and real-time industrial requirements, providing a scalable solution for self-healing intelligent motor drives.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Model for the Detection and Classification of Brain Tumors
authors: Mahmoud M. Abdelhamied, A. M. M. Madbouly, Shaimaa I. Mostafa, Naglaa M. Reda
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: Early-stage detection of brain tumors is a crucial task in medical image analysis, since the early and accurate detection greatly enhances clinical decision-making and treatment planning. Brain tumor screening is inherently difficult because tumorous versus healthy brain tissues have very high interclassed similarity. In this paper, an automated segmentation and classification model using the FCM (Fuzzy C-Means) clustering algorithm is introduced. The proposed method is quantitatively evaluated with traditional machine learning classifiers, in relation to deep learning models for comparison. Traditional classifiers, such as Support Vector Machine (SVM), Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) and Logistic Regression (LR) are utilized with the best classification accuracy of 87 % achieved by SVM. Further, a CNN is introduced to improve the feature learning and it can achieve accuracy being up to 91 %. For further performance optimization, the advanced lightweight deep learning models, MobileNet, is employed. Several benchmark brain MRI datasets (as BRATS, Jun Cheng's, and Additional Kaggle Dataset) are used for training and evaluation. The performance of the MobileNet-based model is 97.28 % (without overfitting) and 99.21% (with overfitting). Experiments show that the proposed model achieves significant quantitative improvement over the current state-of-the-art methods, indicating a strong robustness and effectiveness on brain tumor segmentation and classification.

title: Manhole Cover Hazard Detection Based on the Improved YOLOv10s
authors: Donghua Yuan, Jun Tao, Wenjun Wu, Shunzhi Hou, Yifan Wei, Siyuan Meng
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: As a critical component in urban underground pipeline network maintenance, the management of manhole cover safety hazards faces operational challenges due to the wide distribution and large quantity of manhole pipelines. Traditional manual inspection methods can no longer meet the demand for efficient identification. In this study, we propose an intelligent detection optimization scheme based on YOLOv10s. The model's generalization ability is enhanced through multi-dimensional data augmentation strategies (e.g., hue perturbation, saturation adjustment, and exposure compensation). We innovatively integrate the lightweight and efficient Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) with the Coordinate Attention (CA) module, constructing a dual-channel feature screening mechanism to suppress interference from complex backgrounds. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model achieves significantly higher mean Average Precision (mAP) on the manhole cover hazard dataset compared to the baseline YOLOv10s model and traditional detection methods. This solution provides effective technical support for safety monitoring in urban underground spaces.

title: Real Time Converter-Based EIS with Output Disturbance Mitigation for Fuel Cell Application
authors: Pedro Andrade, Adérito Neto Alcaso, Antonio J. Marques Cardoso
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) is a fundamental tool in Fuel Cell (FC) diagnostics. It provides information on both electrical and electrochemical behaviors, by measuring the equivalent impedance. Converter-based EIS has gained significant attention due to its practicality and integration potential. However, such methods are limited in real-time applications because the injected perturbations propagate to the system output, causing undesirable AC ripple that have a negative effect on the connected load. To address this challenge, this paper presents a closed-loop converter-based EIS that mitigates the AC ripple using a resonant controller in combination with a frequency customized lead filter. The proposed method enables accurate impedance measurements without compromising the output voltage stability. Both simulation and experimental results are presented, and the main conclusion are drawn.

title: A Deep Learning Framework for Glomeruli Segmentation with Boundary Attention
authors: Behnaz Elhaminia, Catherine King, Jiaqi Lv, Lorraine Harper, Paul Moss, Owen Cain, Dimitrios Chanouzas, Shan E Ahmed Raza
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate detection and segmentation of glomeruli in kidney tissue are essential for diagnostic applications. Traditional deep learning methods primarily rely on semantic segmentation, which often fails to precisely delineate adjacent glomeruli. To address this challenge, we propose a novel glomerulus detection and segmentation model that emphasises boundary separation. Leveraging pathology foundation models, the proposed U-Net–based architecture incorporates a specialised attention decoder designed to highlight critical regions and improve instance-level segmentation. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that our approach surpasses state-of-the-art methods in both Dice score and Intersection over Union (IoU), indicating superior performance in glomerular delineation.

title: Reconstruction of Absorption Coefficients for Quantitative Photoacoustic Tomography with Unsupervised Neural Representation
authors: Youshen Xiao, Yiling Shi, Ruixi Sun, Yuyao Zhang, Fei Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Quantitative photoacoustic tomography (qPAT) combines the benefits of optical and ultrasound imaging. It reconstructs the quantitative distribution of optical parameters in biological tissues by detecting ultrasound signals generated from light absorption. The absorption coefficient (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex>) is crucial as it is linked to physiological and pathological features. However, in qPAT imaging, due to wavelength-depend scattering and absorption, the photoacoustic image reflects the product of unknown light fluence (LF) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex>, rather than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex> directly. Reconstructing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex> from the photoacoustic image without knowing LF in deep tissue is an ill-posed inverse problem. To address this challenge, we propose a neural representation-based method for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex> reconstruction. This method integrates a diffusion equation-based optical transport model, taking into account the optical processes in PAT imaging to enhance the accuracy and robustness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex> estimation. Simulated photoacoustic digital brain and phantom data experiments demonstrate that our proposed method can accurately reconstruct the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu_{a}$</tex> distribution, thereby enhancing the quantitative imaging capabilities of qPAT.

title: Thermal Measurement of Asymmetric Losses in a Submodule of an Isolated Modular Multilevel DC-DC Converter
authors: José Andrés Aguilar Croston, Narges Moeini, Jean-Yves Gauthier, Cyril Buttay, Besar Asllani, Piotr Dworakowski, Maryam Saeedifard
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: In this work, asymmetric loss distribution between the high-side and low-side devices of an Isolated Modular Multilevel DC-DC Converter (IM2DC) submodule (SM) is identified and experimentally quantified, revealing that low-side devices can dissipate up to twice the losses of their high-side counterparts. This imbalance indicates unequal semiconductor utilization, with direct implications for thermal design and long-term reliability in modular DC-DC architectures. To investigate this phenomenon, a steady-state calorimetric method based on thermal imaging is applied directly to a half-bridge SM while the full converter operates under various voltage levels. The approach provides accurate, non-intrusive loss measurements and is validated through analytical estimations with an absolute error below 0.07 W. These findings lay the groundwork for future optimization of IM2DC converter design and control.

title: Hybrid Control Strategy for Dual Active Bridge Converters in UPS Systems
authors: Kuo-Fu Liao, Chieh-Pin Chan, Kuo-Liang Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The dual active bridge (DAB) converter is widely used for bidirectional DC–DC conversion due to its high efficiency and soft-switching capability. While traditionally applied to DC loads, its use in uninterruptible power supply systems faces challenges as the AC inverter introduces double-line-frequency pulsating power, leading to increased circulating current, a narrowed ZVS range, and efficiency degradation under conventional single-phase shift (SPS) control. This paper investigates triple-phase-shift (TPS) control for DAB converters to address these issues. By exploiting three-phase-shift variables, TPS reduces circulating energy, extends ZVS, and improves efficiency under AC load conditions. Finally, a 3 kW prototype with a 44-56 V battery input and a 120 VAC output is built to verify the theoretical analyses. The highest efficiency of 95% is achieved at 60% load.

title: Cross-Modal Image Learning for HER2 Status Detection From H&E Histopathology Images
authors: Shubham Mishra, Debapriya Roy, Sandeep Mathur, Bikash Santra
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) status plays a key role in predicting breast cancer outcomes and guiding targeted treatments. The current clinical standard, Immunohistochemistry (IHC), provides reliable results but depends on specialized reagents, extra processing time, and advanced laboratory facilities, making it less accessible in many settings. This work introduces a cross-modal image learning framework that can automatically determine HER2 status directly from routine Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) stained whole-slide images, reducing the need for IHC images. Our model learns from paired H&E-IHC samples to capture subtle tissue structures and patterns in H&E images that reflect HER2 expression. We evaluate our approach on the BCI dataset and compare it qualitatively with IHC-based scoring. Our method performs inference solely on H&Estained slides yet achieves superior performance compared to models trained on both H&E and IHC images, as well as those trained exclusively on H&E data. The improvement is statistically significant with a p-value of ~ 0.02(<0.05). By enabling accurate HER2 prediction from standard H&E images, the proposed framework offers a faster, more affordable, and widely accessible solution to support timely clinical decisions and follow-up testing.

title: A Unified Mixed Reality Interface for Dynamic Swarm Mission Planning and Control
authors: Miguel Caeiro, Akshat Singh, Rodrigo Ventura, Meysam Basiri
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: As drone technology scales from single-agent operations to coordinated teams, traditional two-dimensional interfaces often fail to provide the spatial context and intuitive control necessary for effective supervision. To address these challenges, this paper introduces a novel unified Mixed Reality (MR) framework designed to enhance operator situational awareness and streamline the dynamic control of multiple Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). The system utilizes a Meta Quest 3 headset to provide an immersive planning interface, enabling operators to define waypoint-based missions and generate automated volumetric area mapping paths using intuitive spatial inputsThe proposed architecture integrates a custom ROS–Unity bridge (Middleman) deployed on the drone’s companion computer, which facilitates reliable, bidirectional telemetry streaming and supports dynamic mission management, including real-time preemption and automated safety failsafes. The framework was validated through Software-In-The-Loop (SITL) simulations and hardware testing, demonstrating a mean command latency of approximately 408 ms and linear network scalability. Furthermore, a user study (N=15) confirmed that the spatial registration of mission data significantly reduces cognitive load, with participants reporting high confidence in the interaction mechanics.

title: CephRes-MHNet: A Multi-Head Residual Network for Accurate and Robust Cephalometric Landmark Detection
authors: Ahmed Jaheen, Islam Hassan, Mohanad Abouserie, Abdelaty Rehab, Adham Elasfar, Knzy Elmasry, Mostafa El-Dawlatly, Seif Eldawlatly
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate localization of cephalometric landmarks from 2D lateral skull X-rays is vital for orthodontic diagnosis and treatment. Manual annotation is time-consuming and error-prone, whereas automated approaches often struggle with low contrast and anatomical complexity. This paper introduces CephRes-MHNet, a multi-head residual convolutional network for robust and efficient cephalometric landmark detection. The architecture integrates residual encoding, dual-attention mechanisms, and multi-head decoders to enhance contextual reasoning and anatomical precision. Trained on the Aariz Cephalometric dataset of 1,000 radiographs, CephResMHNet achieved a mean radial error (MRE) of 1.23 mm and a success detection rate (SDR) @ 2.0 mm of 85.5 %, outperforming all evaluated models. In particular, it exceeded the strongest baseline, the attention-driven AFPF-Net (MRE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=1.25 ~\text{mm}$</tex>, SDR @ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0 ~\text{mm}= 84.1 \%$</tex>), while using less than 25 % of its parameters. These results demonstrate that CephRes-MHNet attains state-of-the-art accuracy through architectural efficiency, providing a practical solution for real-world orthodontic analysis.

title: A Temperature-Tolerant, Ultrafast Fault Protection Scheme for GaN HEMTs with PCB-Integrated Rogowski Coil Current Sensors
authors: P. T. Nandh Kishore, Soumya Shubhra Nag, Sumit Kumar Pramanick
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: A key challenge in the reliable design of power converters with GaN HEMTs is their low short circuit withstand time (SCWT) compared to SiC MOSFETs. Conventional desaturation protection schemes exhibit longer response times and are based on device output characteristics, whose variation with junction temperature shifts the fault-triggering instant. Fault protection schemes based on high-bandwidth current sensors can facilitate protection times much lower than the SCWT of the device. This work presents the design of a temperature-tolerant, ultrafast fault protection scheme for GaN HEMTs based on device current sensing with PCB-integrated Rogowski coils. The fault threshold level is set online, upon evaluating the mutual coupling between the coil and the DC power trace. Experimental results are presented at a DC bus voltage of 400 V in a half-bridge test setup. Reliable protection of the GaN HEMT subjected to hard switched fault is demonstrated for various device junction temperatures. Owing to an online scheme of high-bandwidth current sensing, the protection response time is reported to be under 40 ns across all operating conditions, independent of elevated junction temperatures.

title: Enhancing Robustness and Generalizability Through Self-Distillation Driven Deep Learning Model for Improving Diagnostic Reliability in Healthcare Systems
authors: Kavitha. B, D. Paulraj, M.R. Kousalya
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Deep learning models have shown very impressive performance in every field of AI, yet their working capabilities tend to drop after being presented with unfamiliar data distributions or domain changes. The proposed study presented a self-distillation-based deep learning model that can enhance the resilience of AI. The proposed method uses conventional knowledge distillation that relies on a hierarchical learning framework and allows a model to positively self-learn in order to refine internal representations and minimize overfitting. Experiments using heterogeneous datasets show better results in all metrics such as accuracy, stability, and transferability on the baseline deep networks. This work found that self-distillation is a valuable regularization mechanism in testing and performing reliable and adaptable AI systems that could maintain high performance even in a dynamic, real-world setting.

title: Osteo-Net: Mask R-CNN Based Osteoclasts Detector
authors: Yeonsoo Chung, Seung-Hyun Hong, Frank C. Nichols, David W. Rowe, Dong-Guk Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of osteoclasts in whole-slide images (WSIs) is essential for quantifying cell counts and sizes, a prerequisite for studying bone diseases such as osteoporosis and osteopetrosis. We propose a WSI-to-instance pipeline, Osteo-Net, that integrates patch-level augmentation with seam-robust stitching (feathering, edge trimming, and mask enforcement). Osteo-Net uses Mask R-CNN (ResNet-50 FPN) with SE attention and a composite loss (focal loss for classification and BCE+Dice for masks). On validation patches, Osteo-Net achieves a Dice score of 0.95141 and F1 of 0.93636, and on held-out WSIs it attains Dice 0.9031, substantially outperforming a fine-tuned Cellpose-sam baseline (Dice 0.6623). Ablation analysis shows that focal loss and SE attention drive these gains, whereas CBAM and removal of augmentation degrade performance. These results indicate that tailored loss design with lightweight attention and WSI-aware stitching offers a practical solution for automated osteoclast quantification.

title: Overhang-Constrained Bezier Strut Optimization for Body-Centered Cubic Lattice Structures in Additive Manufacturing
authors: Ali El Mechhouri, Othmane Noureddine, Younes Moubachir, Zouhair Guennoun
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Support structures in laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) increase build time, material waste, and post-processing burden, and are particularly problematic for internal lattice geometries where access for removal is limited. We present a manufacturability-driven optimization of body-centered cubic (BCC) lattice unit cells that reduces support-critical downward-facing surfaces by minimizing a differentiable, normalized overhang-violation metric subject to relative density control. Strut radii are parameterized with cubic Bézier curves using four control radii, enabling asymmetric, variable-thickness profiles while preserving periodicity. Effective elastic properties are computed via computational homogenization with periodic boundary conditions. Across target relative densities (0.20 to 0.45), cell sizes (2 and 3 mm), and overhang thresholds (40, 45 and 50 degrees), the method achieves up to a 9.5% reduction in the normalized overhang-violation metric for cell sizes of 2 mm and above while maintaining or improving homogenized stiffness.

title: Operation of Wide-Range Universal V2G Charger in DC Microgrids
authors: Hans Anniste, Andrei Blinov, Dmitri Vinnikov
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper presents a universal wide operating range bidirectional DC-DC charger for electric vehicles (EVs), along with its features that enable seamless integration into DC grids. The proposed topology provides an output voltage range of 150 V to 1000 V while maintaining a low component count in the power stage. Its dual-input stage design enables compatibility with three DC grid configurations, including unipolar and bipolar types. Charging power and V2G operation are controlled according to a charger-specific droop curve. Additionally, the charger supports voltage balancing both during EV charging and when the EV is not connected.

title: Large Language Model Assisted Neural Architecture Search Through Concept Evolution in TinyNAS Environments
authors: Jasmine Selvakumari Jeya I, Lavanya Arora, Adhita Sinha, Tanya Jain, Divya Tiwari, Om Sharma
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Neural Architecture Search has become an important method for automating the design of neural networks, yet many existing approaches remain costly because they rely on repeated training and evaluation cycles. Trainingfree proxy metrics offer faster screening but often suffer from unstable rankings, limited exploration capability, and weak generalization across different search spaces. This paper proposes a Large Language Model guided Neural Architecture Search framework that integrates language-driven mutation generation with training-free proxy evaluation to enable context-aware, efficient, and interpretable architecture discovery. The framework includes an LLM-based mutation generator, a concept evolution mechanism that adapts exploration based on objective drift, a diversity controller to maintain population variety, a multi-objective evaluator that incorporates hardware-related metrics such as latency, memory use, and energy behavior, and a safety validation stage that ensures architectural correctness. The system is implemented on a lightweight search setting and produces complete audit trails and exportable model definitions while achieving rapid search times. Experiments show that LLM-guided mutations improve convergence, increase the rate of valid architectural transformations, and enhance interpretability when compared with random mutation baselines.

title: Improving Fairness in Vision Language Models for Medical Applications via Max Deviation Penalty
authors: K. Imbulgoda, R. Tennakoon, S. Korevaar, W. Chuah, A. Bab-Hadiashar
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Fairness in medical artificial intelligence (AI) tools is essential, as biased model behaviour can lead to unequal diagnostic outcomes and raise serious ethical concerns, ultimately reducing clinical trust. Although Vision Language Models (VLMs) show strong performance in medical imaging, they still exhibit demographic biases, particularly across gender, race, and other sensitive attributes. Addressing these disparities is difficult due to the utility-fairness trade off, where improving equity often reduces diagnostic accuracy. To overcome these challenges, we propose the Max Deviation Penalty, a lightweight regularization objective that integrates seamlessly into parameter efficient finetuning schemes. Experiments across diverse medical and general domain benchmarks demonstrate that our method consistently achieves state of the art performance in balancing fairness and utility, surpassing existing baselines enabling fair, reliable VLM adaptation.

title: A Multistage Soft Start-up Technique for SHB-FB Type DAB Converter Considering Precharge of DC Blocking Capacitors
authors: Yanda Lyu, Ziwei Liang, Fred Wang, Hua Bai, Zhou Dong, Debanjan Chatterjee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Soft start-up in Dual-Active-Bridge (DAB) converters is a significant challenge due to the need to mitigate initial inrush currents. This challenge is even more pronounced in the Stacked Half-Bridge Full-Bridge (SHB-FB) converter incorporating an additional blocking capacitor and is a derivative of the conventional DAB converter. This paper initially assesses the start-up issues introduced by the dc blocking capacitor in the SHB-FB converter, which has received limited attention in existing literature. Then, a multistage soft start-up technique for the SHB-FB converter is proposed, considering the precharging process of the dc blocking capacitor. The technique is first validated through simulations and further experimentally verified with a Gallium Nitride (GaN)-based SHB-FB converter. The start-up challenges arising from the blocking capacitor are addressed, and experimental results show a 74% decrease in the initial inrush current compared with the hard start-up condition.

title: Effectiveness of SAM for Conjunctiva Segmentation
authors: Adapa Sunil Kumar, Kotiya Mohith Goud, Kamalaker Dadi, Raju S. Bapi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: We propose an end-to-end framework for anemia detection from eye conjunctiva images, integrating a LoRA fine-tuned Segment Anything Model (SAM) for segmentation with machine learning models for hemoglobin estimation and classification. The LoRA-based SAM achieved mean IoU scores of 83.4 % on the Eye-Defy dataset and 76.1 % on the Eye Conjunctiva Segmentation Dataset, outperforming zero-shot SAM variants. Handcrafted RGB and CIE-Lab features extracted from the segmented conjunctiva enabled Random Forest regression to achieve an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.52 (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.23 ~\mathrm{g} / \text{dL})$</tex>, closely matching results using ground-truth masks. For binary anemia classification, Random Forest, XGBoost, and LightGBM achieved accuracies up to 90 %, exceeding prior literature. These results highlight the effectiveness of SAM in producing high-quality conjunctiva segmentation without manual annotation, enabling scalable and automated anemia screening. Code is available online.

title: Deep Learning-Based Accelerated Adaptive Beamforming for Enhanced Musculoskeletal Ultrasound Imaging
authors: Midhila Madhusoodanan, Mahesh Raveendranatha Panicker, Abhilash R Hareendranathan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Medical ultrasound (US) imaging typically uses delay and sum beamforming (DASB) for image reconstruction due to its computational simplicity. In DASB, the backscattered signals from the tissue are geometrically compensated for the time-of-flight (TOF) and apodized to reconstruct the final image. However, DASB images are susceptible to interference and are not optimized for any particular medical imaging use case. Various adaptive beamforming algorithms that leverage data covariance (like Minimum Variance Distortionless Response (MVDR)) have been developed to address the limitations of DASB. Despite their effectiveness, these algorithms are computationally intense and not suitable for high frame rate applications or real-time clinical use, especially for point-of-care US systems (POCUS). In this work, we propose a patchGAN-based beamforming accelerator for generating multiple plane wave (MPW) MVDR beamformed images directly from single plane wave (SPW) radio frequency (RF) data, learning both TOF correction and apodization. The model was trained and evaluated on in-vivo musculoskeletal (MSK) US datasets. The proposed model achieved a mean CR of 4.25 dB, CNR of 3.62 dB, and SNR of 6.00 dB, closely approaching MPW-MVDR (CR: 4.80 dB, CNR: 3.47 dB, SNR: 5.54 dB) while substantially improving over SPW-DASB (CR:3.25dB, CNR: 2.54 dB, SNR: 2.63 dB). Our patchGAN beamforming accelerator outperformed simpler encoder-decoder models like the U-Net by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-10 \%$</tex>. This approach generated high-quality MVDR-like US images in 0.004 s vs. 148 s for conventional MVDR, which makes it well suited for real-time applications.

title: Multi-Port Operation of Modular Bridge Rectifier Solid-State Transformer with Integrated Medium-Voltage Energy Storage
authors: Zhenchao Li, Giacomo Andrioli, Drazen Dujic
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: Solid-state transformers (SSTs) demonstrate significant potential for hybrid power conversion systems requiring medium-voltage (MV) and low-voltage (LV) DC interfaces to a MVAC grid. The modular bridge rectifier (mBR) topology represents an emerging single-stage SST implementation, featuring just one diode and isolated DC/DC converter per submodule to achieve superior power density and modularity. This paper presents the first comprehensive exploration of mBR’s multi-port functionality for MVAC / MVDC / LVDC power conversion. The quantitative analysis of each port’s achievable power range, accounting for topological constraints and current stress limitations, is presented.
