A novel branch current distribution method is proposed to reduce operational voltage range of branch DC/DC converters while compensating for MVDC current disturbances. Furthermore, the islanded operation without MVAC grid is discussed, wherein the three-port mBR morphs into three parallel input-series-output-parallel (ISOP) DC/DC converters. A hierarchical control architecture capable of seamless transition between grid-connected and islanded modes is also introduced. Finally, all proposed operational modes of multi-port mBR SST are validated through a thorough simulation of a 250 kW demonstration converter connecting a 3.3 kV MVAC and a 4.67 kV MVDC grids to an 800 V LVDC load.

title: From Client-Side to Server-Side: Rethinking Web Security in the Age of Multimodal AI
authors: Abdelrahman Elsayd, Saeed Hamouda, Ayman Mohamed, Mohamed M. Reda Ali, Salameh A. Mjlae
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The rise of multimodal artificial intelligence requires a shift from client-side to server-side data protection. In this paper, we empirically show that recent multimodal AI advances have compromised client-side obfuscation mechanisms to protect sensitive information (e.g., email addresses). We validate our approach based on 200 hybrid text-image obfuscated samples and testing it with state-of-the-art AI systems (GPT-4V and Google Gemini), for which we show that we obtained nearest 100 % extraction success rate by AI, whereas legacy scrapers that use OCR extracted only 1 % of exact emails, and 12 % of emails with spaces. This paper argues that in era of advanced AI, client-side obfuscation is no longer relevant. Hence, a paradigm shift should be implemented toward server-side protection models based on zero-exposure storage, verified proof of intent, and action-oriented disclosure. Additionally, it will serve as empirical validation for the failure of client-side defenses while providing a framework for next-generation web security architectures.

title: Hierarchical Clustering for False Negative Aware Prototypical Contrastive Learning in Histopathology
authors: Laetitia Rebiere, Aïna Venkatasamy, Thomas Lampert
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Digital histopathology generates large images in which annotation is difficult. Contrastive learning is an approach to learn from large amounts of unlabelled data that allows the performance of fully supervised methods to be achieved with fewer labels. However, the success of contrastive learning is largely dependent on the reliable definition of negative sample pairs. Extracting patches from histopathology datasets results in considerable intersample similarity, which increases the presence of false negative samples that can perturb selfsupervised training. In this work, we present HARP, a model for false negative aware contrastive learning. Hierarchical clustering is performed in the latent space to capture general contextual information over the data. This information is then used to properly select negative samples and limit the presence of false negative samples that can degrade the final representation. Using the publicly available Camelyon 16 dataset, we show that HARP's performance is within 1% F1score of the fully supervised baseline while requiring only 0.5% of the labels.

title: Medical Image Analysis Integrating ViT Network and Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Hangyu Cao
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The development of deep learning provides technical support for the analysis of medical image data. In order to solve the problem of data scarcity of medical images in deep learning training, this study proposes a medical image analysis method that integrates VisionTransformer and self-supervised learning. A self-supervised learning strategy that integrates hybrid pre-training is adopted to solve the problem of data scarcity, and the combination of self-supervised learning and downstream tasks is realized through the VisionTransformer training framework to strengthen the deep data mining of medical images. In the analysis of main and auxiliary tasks, the optimal Dice coefficient is determined by mixing data sets of different proportions, and the maximum Dice value of the mixed data set is 0.815. In the classification analysis of lung medical images, the model performs best under the mixed data set, with an area under the curve of 99.13%, which is better than the unfused data. It can be seen that the research technology has good application effect. This study provides technical support for medical image training and analysis.

title: DiGMa: A Dual-Domain Collaborative Framework for Efficient Temporal MPI Reconstruction
authors: Zining Liu, Gen Shi, Ziwei Chen, Jie Tian, Hui Zhang, Jingying Jiang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Magnetic particle imaging (MPI) is a novel imaging technique offering radiation-free and fast imaging with promise for temporal imaging studies (e.g., vascular and functional imaging). However, MPI temporal reconstruction is challenged by per-frame noise and the cost of joint spacetemporal modeling. In this study, we first propose a dualdomain collaborative framework for efficient temporal MPI reconstruction, named DiGMa, to address the two main challenges. DiGMa performs signal-domain denoising via GCN-based inter-frame modeling, and image-domain enhancement via Mamba that efficiently captures long-range dependencies. The proposed method was evaluated on both simulation and measured data, demonstrating superior performance over traditional approaches. Besides, qualitative results indicate fewer artifacts and superior image quality, while the computational load of the model is also reduced. DiGMa thus achieves a favorable balance between reconstruction quality and computational efficiency for temporal MPI.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Network based Modeling of Magnetic Couplers for EV Wireless Charging System
authors: Chang Li, Junjun Deng, Zheng Yi, Mengchen Duang, Shuo Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The design of magnetic couplers is crucial to the efficiency of inductive wireless power transfer (WPT) systems in EV charging application, where coil geometries and material layouts must be engineered under tight electromagnetic and packaging constraints. Conventional finite-element modeling (FEM) provides high fidelity but is computationally expensive for iterative design, while purely data-driven surrogate models are fast but often neglect electromagnetic constraints and material interactions. This paper presents a physics-informed neural network (PINN) framework that embeds the frequency-domain quasi-static A-form of Maxwell’s equations with boundary and interface conditions, incorporates material-aware eddy-current effects, and leverages lightweight FEM-based calibration for absolute scaling. The method parameterizes DD-shaped and rectangular coils together with ferrite and aluminum shielding, trains a physics-constrained network on these inputs, and performs forward inference to directly predict inductive characteristics from geometry parameters. Validation on representative prototypes at 85 kHz shows agreement within a few percent relative to FEM and bench measurements, while reducing evaluation time from tens of minutes to a few minutes per design, thereby enabling rapid and accurate modeling of practical WPT couplers.

title: KOCOBrain: Kuramoto-Guided Graph Network for Uncovering Structure-Function Coupling in Adolescent Prenatal Drug Exposure
authors: Badhan Mazumder, Lei Wu, Sir-Lord Wiafe, Vince D. Calhoun, Dong Hye Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Exposure to psychoactive substances during pregnancy, such as cannabis, can disrupt neurodevelopment and alter largescale brain networks, yet identifying their neural signatures remains challenging. We introduced KOCOBrain: KuramotO COupled Brain Graph Network; a unified graph neural network framework that integrates structural and functional connectomes via Kuramoto-based phase dynamics and cognition-aware attention. The Kuramoto layer models neural synchronization over anatomical connections, generating phase-informed embeddings that capture structure-function coupling, while cognitive scores modulate information routing in a subject-specific manner followed by a joint objective enhancing robustness under class imbalance scenario. Applied to the ABCD cohort, KOCOBrain improved prenatal drug exposure prediction over relevant baselines and revealed interpretable structure-function patterns that reflect disrupted brain network coordination associated with early exposure.

title: A Triple-Path 12V/24V-to-1V GaN-Based Hybrid DC-DC Converter
authors: Saket Jha, Rafaella Gomes, Hanh-Phuc Le
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents a new 12V/24V-to-1V non-isolated hybrid converter topology. This converter employs multiple current paths at the output, resulting in reduced current stress on each passive component. The lower current reduces I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>R conduction losses and thus enables the use of passive components with smaller footprints, improving the power density of the implementation. Experimental results from a 500kHz, 25W hardware prototype verify the converter’s operation and performance, demonstrating efficiencies of 90.25% and 90.96% at 12V-to-1V and 12V-to-1.2V conversions, respectively, and achieving a peak power density of 495 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: A Secure Retrieval-Augmented LLM Framework for Cloud-Based Incident Response
authors: Saravanan Raj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The response to a cloud incident is characterized by the need to make fast decisions and maintain evidentiary integrity, tenant isolation, and adherence to policies. In this paper, the authors introduce SRAG-IR, a secure retrievalaugmented LLM framework governing an incident response on the cloud that interconnects a controlled evidence lake and an attested retrieval and action layer. SRAG-IR builds a timeindexed part of incident memory over logs, alerts, tickets, and certified threat intelligence and determines only those fragments that are policy authorized through attribute-based access control and tenant-bound vector indices. A context firewall removes untrusted instructions in text accessed and enforces schema constrained prompts to minimize prompt injection and data exfiltration. Chunks of evidence are hash-chained and signed and produce verifiable cites and auditable respondent provenance. To have containment and recovery, sandboxed tool broker runs approved cloud actions as per playbook templates, producing change plans and rollback hints. The design enhances trust, privacy, and operational safety and does not need model retraining with multi-tenant production clouds. Dual retrieval integrates semantic search and asset-dependency graphs allowing root-cause hypothesis and prioritized triage suggestions should there be strict latency and cost budget limits.

title: Omnidirectional Wireless Power Transfer at MHz for Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Heng Zhang, Paul D. Mitcheson, Chi-Kwan Lee
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The advancement of capsule endoscopy toward higher-resolution imaging and multifunctional capabilities has significantly increased power consumption, rendering traditional low-frequency (kHz) wireless charging methods and limited battery capacities insufficient. We present a miniaturized 6.78 MHz wireless power transfer (WPT) system that eliminates magnetic cores and leverages compact MHz coils to drastically reduce both transmitter and receiver size. A three-dimensional orthogonal receiving coil design achieves near-zero mutual coupling, enabling independent operation without interference, with outputs combined via full-bridge rectification and filtering. A prototype, validated through modeling, simulation, and experiments under varying positions and orientations, delivers up to 1.681 W with 23.0 % peak efficiency. These results demonstrate efficient high-frequency wireless charging in a compact form factor, offering a viable solution for next-generation capsule endoscopes.

title: Quantitative Research on Safety Distance between Ships and Offshore Floating Aquaculture Platforms
authors: Zhixiu Du, Xiaozhi Liu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: To mitigate navigation safety risks near aquaculture farming platforms and standardize their offshore siting and construction, it is imperative to quantify the safe distance between ships and aquaculture structures. First, this study establishes a boundary determination model for floating aquaculture platforms based on their deployment characteristics. By analyzing the nuclear density of AIS traffic flow around such platforms, a methodology for deriving safe distances is proposed. Taking the "Mintou No.1" aquaculture platform in Lianjiang as a case study, the safe distances are calculated separately using a ship out-of-control model, emergency stopping model and AIS traffic flow model. Comparative analysis of theoretical safe distances derived from different models demonstrates that the AIS traffic flow model is most safety model. It can effectively control the navigation safety risk of ships in the vicinity of the waters of offshore aquaculture platforms.

title: Deep Learning Analysis of Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocyte, High Endothelial Venule, and Tertiary Lymphoid Structures in Breast Cancer Histopathology Whole Slide Imaging
authors: Hongxuan Tan, On Ki Tang, Chi Wai Lou, Conrad H.C. Lee, Wai Ki Wong, Ngou Men Wong, Scotty Kwok, Julia Y.S. Tsang, Ronald C.K. Chan, Gary M. Tse
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Understanding the tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) is crucial for predicting prognosis and guiding immunotherapy in breast cancer. In this work, we present a deep learning pipeline to jointly analyze tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), high endothelial venules (HEVs), and tertiary lymphoid structures (TLSs) from whole slide images (WSIs). Our method integrates multi-modal histopathology data with advanced segmentation and classification models. Specifically, we use HoverNet to segment tumor and immune cells, followed by ResNet-50-based classification to identify immune cell types and generate spatial heatmaps via Gaussian smoothing. For HEV detection, we apply nnU-Netv2 with color normalization using ICC profiles and morphological post-processing to improve accuracy. The resulting masks enable quantitative assessment of immune infiltration patterns and vascular architecture. This automated framework provides a scalable tool for comprehensive TIME analysis in digital pathology.

title: Using OPC UA PubSub for Real-time Control in a Conveyor Tracking Application
authors: David Pereira, Luis Perdigoto, Hugo Costelha, Carlos Neves
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: This paper evaluates the use of the OPC UA publisher/subscriber (PubSub) protocol in a real-time motion control case study, specifically, a Conveyor Tracking application using a GoFa10-CRB15000 collaborative robot and a linear conveyor belt. The robot’s goal is to maintain the end-effector in contact with a touch screen placed in the conveyor, maintaining a predetermined relative pose, independently of the conveyor motion. To achieve this, the conveyor’s encoder signals are sent to the robot, in real-time, using OPC UA PubSub. The communication is established through low cost Raspberry Pi 5 single board computers that serve as gateways between the devices. Tests are performed using standard Ethernet and WiFi 7, using dedicated and shared networks (i.e. with and without additional traffic in the network). The touch screen is used to sense the point of contact and measure the positioning error.Results show that using OPC UA PubSub, over standard Ethernet or WiFi 7, is a feasible solution for real-time motion tracking and control. The measured tracking errors obtained with the OPC UA PubSub communication were comparable to the reference case, where the robot uses encoder signals through direct electrical connections.

title: Carrier Sense Multiple Access Protocols in Smart Multi-domain Integrated Learning Environments: A Comprehensive Analysis
authors: Salsabeel Suhail Theeb, Roaa Mohammad Alabdelal, Batool Mohammad Alrashdan, Obaida M. Al-Hazaimeh, Ashraf A. Abu-Ein
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: The protocols of Carrier Sense Multiple Access (CSMA) underpin contemporary network communication. This study will examine CSMA/CD (Collision Detection) and CSMA/CA (Collision Avoidance) regarding their operational mechanisms, performance characteristics, and application domains. Alongside protocol mechanics, we present a comprehensive study of cybersecurity vulnerabilities inside both protocols and propose innovative defense solutions, including adaptive backoff checking, channel access fairness verification, and multi-layered security systems. Our efforts achieve a balance between enhancing security and optimizing efficiency, providing practical solutions for safeguarding CSMA-based networks against contemporary threats.

title: Radio Signal Detection and Labeling Method Based on Differential Statistical Features of Orthogonal Components of RF I/Q Data
authors: Yuxuan Shao, Yaosen Huang, Zhenjia Chen, Siyuan An
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a radio signal detection method based on the statistical characteristics of the quadrature component difference of radio frequency (RF) I/Q data. It relies on the consistency of the quadrature component difference in two consecutive frames of RF I/Q data and sets a detection threshold based on the characteristic value of background noise to determine the presence of a signal. The RF I/Q data frames containing signals are converted into energy data, and a radio signal annotation method based on a dynamic spectrum energy threshold edge localization method is proposed. Verified by real-world data, the signal detection algorithm proposed in this paper exhibits higher accuracy than traditional energy detection algorithms in low signal-to-noise ratio environments. The signal detection rate reaches 93.6% in low SNR conditions, the signal detection time is shorter than that of the cyclic smoothing detection algorithm, and the signal annotation algorithm can better determine the frequency and quantity of signals.

title: Closed-loop Slew Rate Control of Active Current Source Gate Driver with Analog IC Design for SiC MOSFET
authors: Wen-Xin Wu, Wei-Ru Lin, Wen-Lin Ko, Guan-You Wu, Ching-Jan Chen, Giao Huu Thuc
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: This paper presents an Active Current Source Gate Driver (ACSD) designed for SiC MOSFETs. The driver is applied to HybridPACK Drive modules and controls the drain current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>) slew rate during turn-on and the drain-source voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ds</inf>) slew rate during turn-off. The proposed ACSD employs a start-up current path in the output stage, providing an initial bias for closed-loop regulation. In the control section, the maximum slew rate is sensed using an analog approach. The peak value is captured by a peak detector, stored by double sample-and-hold and fed back into the system, enabling precise slew rate control through cycle-by-cycle iterative method. Circuit simulations are performed using Cadence virtuoso, and the IC is implemented in the TSMC T18 HVG2 process. A Boost Converter is utilized as a multi-pulse test circuit to validate the controllability and performance of the proposed gate driver.

title: IMACT-CXR: An Interactive Multi-Agent Conversational Tutoring System for Chest X-Ray Interpretation
authors: Tuan-Anh Le, Anh Mai Vu, David Yang, Akash Awasthi, Hien Van Nguyen
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: IMACT-CXR is an interactive multi-agent conversational tutor that helps trainees interpret chest X-rays by unifying spatial annotation, gaze analysis, knowledge retrieval, and image-grounded reasoning in a single AutoGen-based workflow [5]. The tutor simultaneously ingests learner bounding boxes, gaze samples, and free-text observations. Specialized agents evaluate localization quality, generate Socratic coaching, retrieve PubMed evidence, suggest similar cases from REFLACX, and trigger NV-Reason-CXR-3B for vision-language reasoning when mastery remains low or the learner explicitly asks. Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) maintains skill-specific mastery estimates that drive both knowledge reinforcement and case similarity retrieval. A lung-lobe segmentation module derived from a TensorFlow U-Net enables anatomically aware gaze feedback, and safety prompts prevent premature disclosure of ground-truth labels. We describe the system architecture, implementation highlights, and integration with the REFLACX dataset for real DICOM cases. IMACT-CXR demonstrates responsive tutoring flows with bounded latency, precise control over answer leakage, and extensibility toward live residency deployment. Preliminary evaluation shows improved localization and diagnostic reasoning compared to baselines.

title: Deep Diffusion Models for Subject-Specific Sar Estimation in 10.5 T Magnetic Resonance Imaging
authors: Javier Hernandez-Rubia, Candela Chinchilla-Sicilia, Blanca Rodriguez-Gonzalez, Alireza Sadeghi-Tarakameh, Yigitcan Eryaman, Sydney N. Williams, Angel Torrado-Carvajal
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultra-high-field magnetic resonance imaging (UHF-MRI) at 10.5 T enables high-resolution imaging but raises safety concerns due to increased radiofrequency (RF) energy deposition and tissue heating. We propose a diffusion-based deep learning model for fast, subject-specific specific absorption rate (SAR) estimation using individual transmit RF field maps. Trained on simulated data from virtual anatomical models, our framework leverages a 3D SwinUNet architecture within the denoising diffusion probabilistic model (DDPM) paradigm. Comparative analysis of 3D and 2.5D patching strategies shows that overlapping patches significantly improve prediction accuracy and hotspot localization. The model achieves high structural fidelity and reduced error metrics, offering a viable alternative to traditional SAR simulations for real-time safety monitoring in UHF-MRI.

title: Imaging-Formation-Guided Implicit Neural Representation for Multi-View Fusion in Light-Sheet Microscopy
authors: Jun Zhu, Mingzhe Wei, Yue Li, Min Guo, Huafeng Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multi-view light-sheet microscopy captures complementary information from different angles to obtain a comprehensive representation of the specimen. However, fusing these views into a single, high-fidelity reconstruction is challenging, particularly under significant noise. Traditional methods like content-weighted fusion and deconvolution struggle with noise, while supervised deep learning requires difficult-to-acquire, high-quality ground-truth data. To circumvent these limitations, we propose Guided Implicit Fusion (GIF), a self-supervised framework that represents the specimen as a continuous function using an Implicit Neural Representation. By leveraging the image formation model as a constraint, GIF directly optimizes the object representation from the low-SNR multiview data itself, obviating the need for external labels. It enables fusion, denoising, and deconvolution within a unified framework. Evaluations on simulated and experimental datasets show GIF provides high-quality, efficient multi-view fusion, outperforming both traditional and supervised methods, especially under noisy conditions.

title: Impact of Colour Temperature Variation on Thermographic Inspection of Mild Steel
authors: Suresh Kumar Bhambhu, Vanita Arora, Ravibabu Mulaveesala
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Pulse thermography (PT) is a widely used active infrared thermographic technique for non-destructive testing of metallic components due to its simplicity and rapid inspection capability. From a signal-processing perspective, PT generates transient thermal heat flux whose quality strongly depends on excitation conditions and material properties. In this work, the influence of excitation correlated colour temperature (CCT) on phase-based defect detectability in pulse thermography is experimentally investigated. Active infrared thermographic inspection is performed on a mild steel specimen containing flat bottom holes of varying depths and diameters using LED based thermal excitation with adjustable CCT. The acquired thermogram sequences are treated as time evolved thermal signals and processed using frequency domain phase (FDP) analysis to enhance robustness against non-uniform heating and emissivity variations. The effect of excitation CCT on the resulting phase contrast is quantitatively evaluated using signal to noise ratio (SNR) as the performance metric. Experimental results demonstrate that excitation CCT significantly influences the quality of the thermal signals and the effectiveness of FDP based detection, highlighting the importance of excitation signal characteristics in conjunction with signal processing techniques for reliable pulse thermographic inspection of mild steel components.

title: FVEN: Fluid Velocity Estimation Network Based on Laser-Induced Plasma Images
authors: Yuhan Liu, Yan Lei, Yangyang Zhao, Nan Li, Zhenhuan Xu
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In aerospace propulsion applications, precisely measuring fluid flow velocity using optical diagnostic methods remains a significant challenge. Plasma-based flow velocity measurement techniques show promise, but their reliability is limited by inherent uncertainties introduced by the plasma’s own evolutionary dynamics. To this end, this study proposes a novel Fluid Velocity Estimation Network (FVEN), using deep learning methods to analyze laser-induced plasma images. The network comprises three stages: plasma image preprocessing, plasma state estimation, and fluid velocity inversion. By tracking the dynamics of centroid position, FVEN enables precise determination of fluid velocity in temporal intervals. In the plasma image preprocessing stage, a local-global denoising module, comprising a local noise segmenter and a multi-head filter, effectively removes both localized pump light noise and global background interference. During the plasma state estimation stage, a multi-layer perceptron leverages its nonlinear modeling capabilities to robustly estimate centroid positions, even in complex fluid environments. Finally, in the fluid velocity inversion stage, a Multi-Image-Aware Attention Module dynamically adjusts the weights of multiple plasma images via an attention mechanism, significantly mitigating the influence of plasma self-interference on velocity estimation accuracy. The experimental results and visualizations demonstrate that the proposed FVEN, combining with deep learning, significantly enhances the accuracy of fluid velocity estimation, proving instrumental in achieving rapid and precise velocity measurements within combustion fields.

title: SatKit: A CubeSat Education Development Kit for Space Education at HCT-UAE
authors: A. M. M. A. Ali, M. K. H. S. Alnuaimi, R. A. I. S. Kalban, A. Alsalmani, M. S. Alias
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: In recent years, miniature and low-cost satellites known as CubeSats have evolved significantly from space education missions to more sophisticated space missions, including scientific missions, Earth observation, and commercial purposes. The SatKit is an educational CubeSat development kit designed to educate students about the assembly, integration, and testing of a 1U CubeSat during a space mission launch. It comprises components and subsystems that replicate an actual CubeSat. Here, we report a comprehensive study that covers the SatKit overview, its assembly and integration, and testing conducted at the subsystem and component levels. The measured voltages and currents for all subsystems and components were within the required tolerances, ensuring CubeSat operability. The image taken from the camera payload demonstrates the required resolution. The study and analysis reported will provide an alternative learning approach for students to explore CubeSat technology in an economical yet realistic manner.

title: A Computational Framework for Intelligent Team Formation Using Semantic Embeddings and Multi-Criteria Optimization
authors: N Kirubakaran, G Senthil Kumar, M Ezhil Srikanth, Balaraman Sundarambal, V Vigneshwaran, Vishali Muralidharan
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The creation of teams is a highly important aspect that determines the success of projects in the current software engineering setting, but it is usually done on a manual basis, subjectively and with poor efficiency. The conventional automated methods are mostly based on the factor of matching of keywords and single-objective optimization and, therefore, they can be used to detect semantic links between skills and to represent managerial trade-offs. It is reported in this paper that STAR (Semantic, Transparent, Adaptive, Reasoning-based) Team Builder is a computational model of intelligent and explainable team formation. The suggested system will utilize semantic embeddings instructed to solve vocabulary differences and a Multi-Mode Scoring Engine that allows flexibly prioritizing the staffing goals based on the cost-effectiveness, skill acquisition, or quick implementation. In addition, the reasoning layer based on LLM can provide natural language explanations to further enhance the transparency of the system’s recommendations. The experimental analysis with a high-fidelity synthetic enterprise dataset has shown that the STAR can obtain better retrieval precision and better coverage of skills than traditional baseline methods and effectively meet the financial and availability limitations under simulated conditions. Such findings show how explainable and multi-criteria AI systems can be useful in managerial decision-making in team formation processes.

title: Low-Voltage Ride-Through Operation of a New Pumped Hydro Storage System with Model Predictive Current Control
authors: Bhuma Naga Satya Sai Vempali, Deepak Ronanki, Apparao Dekka
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: Low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) is an essential criterion for the grid-connected systems to ensure grid stability during voltage disturbances. In this paper, the LVRT operation of a new variable-speed pumped hydro storage system (PHSS), comprising a four-level back-to-back converter-fed wound rotor synchronous motor (WRSM), is presented. A model predictive current control (MPCC) strategy is applied for the proposed configuration to regulate converter operation effectively during fault conditions by maintaining grid-side and machine-side requirements, such as unity power factor operation, flying capacitor voltage control, and grid/machine current control. The discrete-time (DT) models of the proposed PHSS configuration and their coordination control during normal and LVRT operation are formulated to achieve safe operation while meeting the grid code requirements. The LVRT performance of the proposed PHSS configuration with an MPCC is verified through MATLAB simulations under different voltage dip scenarios. Simulation studies demonstrate that the proposed configuration maintains active and reactive current injection in accordance with Indian grid code standards, thereby meeting the LVRT requirement.

title: State of Charge Estimation of a Li-ion Battery with a DP Model based on Open-Circuit Voltage
authors: Ali Margal, Soukaina El Daoudi, Abderrazzak El Abdallaoui, Rabii Azdoud, Fatima Zahra Loudifa, Abdelmounaim Khallouq, Asma Karama
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: This paper present a methodology for determining the state of charge (SoC) of a battery using a 2RC equivalent electrical model, whilst taking into account the non-linear nature of the SoC and the open-circuit voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OC</inf>). The terminal voltage is modeled using a circuit composed of two parallel RC networks and a series resistor, allowing both the slow and fast dynamics of the battery to be captured. SoC estimation is performed by inverting the experimental function V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OC</inf>(SoC) and applying an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) filter to reduce the impact of measurement noise. The method is evaluated on two standardized driving cycles (LA92 and UDDS) simulated in Matlab. The results show good agreement with the reference SoC. In comparaison with approaches reported in the literature such as EKF, the proposed method provides an effective trade-off between implementation simplicity and accuracy.

title: Attention-Based Tracking of Ultrasound Frames for Prostate Biopsy Navigation Using a Zonal Representation
authors: Chloé Soormally, Clément Beitone, Jocelyne Troccaz, Sandrine Voros
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Computer-assistance improves the accuracy of transrectal prostate biopsy procedures through the fusion of real-time 2D ultrasound images with a reference volume. In this study, we propose a method to remove ambiguity in estimating the sign of out-of-plane parameters of the relative transformation between two consecutive ultrasound images based on the efficient integration of a prostate zonal representation into the model. This avoids the need for systematic and costly resampling of slices. Two variants of a ResNet-based model, with and without self-attention, were trained and evaluated on simulated 2D ultrasound streams from the clinical data of 97 patients, each patient being associated with a reference volume acquired in a standardized manner. The results are very encouraging and demonstrate both the computational efficiency of this method and the value of self-attention for realistic simulator-based trajectories.

title: Efficient Wavelength Conversion in Telecom Band using Active Silicon Microring Resonators
authors: Abdou Shetewy, Weizhong Zhang, Menglong He, Kambiz Jamshidi
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: An active silicon microring resonator is designed to suppress nonlinear losses and enable efficient, voltage-controlled nonlinear operation in a silicon-on-insulator platform. Sweeping out the free carriers from a silicon microring resonator enhances wavelength conversion efficiency from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1 8 ~ d B}$</tex> up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 7. 5 ~ d B}$</tex>.

title: SmartSort Bin: AI-Based Solar-Powered System for Automated Waste Segregation Using IoT
authors: Md Yasin Zihad, Khawja Imran Masud, Arobindo Paul, Md Sajedul Islam, Md Obaidur Rahman, Sahara Ali
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: A solar-powered, AI-driven smart waste management system has been proposed and developed in this research to resolve the inefficient waste management in rapidly urbanizing areas of developing countries. The system is a touchless, automated waste-segregation device that incorporates the MobileNetV2 model. The use of solar energy, along with a battery backup, enables continuous grid-independent operation. In addition, real-time email alerts help operators schedule collections efficiently by notifying them when bins are full. The tests conducted show that the system can classify waste with an accuracy of 99.28%, and that the processing is fast. Empirical testing on the Raspberry Pi 4 demonstrated susceptibility to variations in lighting and diminished classification accuracy for remote objects attributable to inferior image quality. The incorporation of a lightweight deep learning model with renewable energy makes the SmartSort Bin not only a device that can clean up urban areas but also a tool that can raise recycling rates and lower the operational costs of cities, thus providing a viable and scalable way to achieve cleaner and more sustainable urban areas in the future.

title: A Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM-Attention Framework for Multi-Class Web Attack Detection in IoT Environments
authors: Zetong Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Vision, Advanced Imaging and Computer Technology (VAICT)
abstract: With the rapid development of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, an increasing number of devices are connected to the Internet, which significantly expands the attack surface of network systems. Traditional rule-based or signature-based intrusion detection methods have difficulty identifying complex and evolving web attacks. To address this problem, this paper proposes a hybrid deep learning model based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), and an Attention Mechanism for multi-class web attack detection. The model employs multiscale Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to extract local features from Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) request sequences, bidirectional long short-term memory networks to capture contextual dependencies, and an attention mechanism to emphasize critical features related to attacks. Experiments conducted on a large-scale IoT web attack dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 97.08% and a weighted F1-score of 0.9707, outperforming several traditional machine learning and deep learning baselines. The results indicate that the proposed architecture effectively improves the detection capability for complex web attack patterns in IoT environments.

title: A Simplified and Low-Cost Voltage Balancing Control Method for Series-Connected SiC MOSFETs
authors: Xianglai Zeng, Hamood-Ur-Rehman, Daixin Chen, Yue Zhao, Xiaoqing Song
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: High-voltage (>10 kV) SiC MOSFETs enable compact and efficient medium-/high-voltage (MV/HV) power conversion, however, their high cost and limited availability restrict widespread adoption. Series connecting low-voltage (LV) SiC MOSFETs provides a practical alternative, but ensuring reliable dynamic voltage balancing remains a key challenge. This paper proposes a simplified active voltage balancing method using a low-voltage bipolar junction transistor (BJT) integrated into the Kelvin source path of each SiC MOSFET. The BJT conduction is dynamically regulated by a closed-loop PI controller, which adjusts the effective gate driving current based on the sampled drain–source voltage. This enables accurate voltage sharing during switching transients without sacrificing high-speed operation. The proposed voltage balancing circuits share a common reference ground between the SiC MOSFET and BJT, thereby eliminating additional isolated power supplies and isolation components typically required in prior active balancing circuits. The method is scalable and validated in both simulations and experiments with three 1.2-kV devices operating at up to a 1.8-kV DC bus, across a wide range of current levels, reaching peak values exceeding 90 A. Results confirm effective voltage balancing with reduced circuit complexity and cost, highlighting the potential of the proposed technique for high-voltage SiC converter applications.

title: From Fear to Features: Deep Learning on EEG for Early Screening of Learning Disabilities in Children
authors: Ghadeer Zidan Suleiman, Mazen Al-Zyoud, Najah Al-Shanableh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Learning disabilities are one of the most common challenges a child will face in their school-age years, and emotional issues like fear and anxiety can rob them of psychological health as well as academic success. In this train of thought, a computational neuroscientific study on LD children being afraid can be successful by using electroencephalogram (EEG) data through an emerging technology including deep learning. Publicly available EEG datasets from four children with learning disorders (recorded under resting-state and fear-evoked conditions) were used. For both frequency bands, several sources were selected according to a combination of statistical and spectral properties, and a pattern of statistical dependency between the emotionally associated neural activities was identified. To manage the lack of informative samples, synthetic samples were generated using advanced over-sampling techniques like SMOTE and ADASYN. We tried and tested a few classification models and selected the best performing deep learning model (ResNet1D) achieved impressive results with an accuracy of 99.33%, Recall of 100% and an ROC AUC score of 0.9997, which again reflects how well it can discriminate fear-related brain patterns. The findings seem to confirm a fear-specific tuning of brain dynamics in children with learning disabilities, mainly on theta and beta frequency bands, which are modernly recognized markers of higher-order cognitive and emotional dysfunctions. These findings also demonstrate the potential of EEG-biased AIs as efficient and noninvasive instruments for early screening. However, the dataset has only four real LD subjects and extensively uses synthetic augmentation so the reported performance should be considered as proof of concept and remains to be validated on larger subject-wise clinical population with real controls. This approach offers new possibilities for integrating affective neuroscience into educational diagnostics, so that it is possible to develop plans of learning specifically adapted to the emotional and cognitive requirements of each child.

title: A Multimodal ResNet-BERT Fusion Model for Accurate Pet Dermatosis Diagnosis from Images and Natural Language Symptoms
authors: Pak-Hei Lam, MingHui Fan
publish: 2026 22nd IEEE International Colloquium on Signal Processing &amp; Its Applications (CSPA)
abstract: Pet skin diseases represent one of the most frequent health complaints in companion animals, yet early and accurate diagnosis remains challenging for pet owners and general practitioners due to visual similarity among conditions and the need to integrate lesion appearance with symptomatic descriptions. This paper presents a novel bimodal diagnostic framework that synergistically combines enhanced ResNet50 (augmented with CBAM attention modules) for dermoscopic lesion analysis and Language-BERT for processing naturallanguage symptom descriptions. Features from both modalities are fused using weighted, concatenation, and attention-based strategies before final disease and species classification. The system is trained on a large-scale dataset exceeding $\mathbf{5 0, 0 0 0}$ annotated pet skin images covering cats and dogs, supplemented by symptom texts. Experimental results demonstrate that the bimodal approach achieves a best validation accuracy of 92.59% and final training accuracy of 97.80%, significantly outperforming unimodal image-only $\boldsymbol{(} \boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{8 8} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{9 0 \%} \boldsymbol{)}$ and text-only baselines. The deployed Python system supports single-modality (image/text) and joint inference, providing top 3 differential diagnoses together with treatment suggestions. This work advances veterinary AI by incorporating multimodal reasoning in a language-sensitive manner, offering a practical, low-cost auxiliary tool for early pet dermatosis screening in Multiple-speaking regions and beyond.

title: A Strategic AI Governance Framework for Cybersecurity: Ensuring Security, Fairness and Accoutability
authors: Sundus Ayyaz, Sultan Mahmood Malik
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into business operations has revolutionized industries, enhancing efficiency, decision-making, and overall competitiveness. However, alongside its significance, AI adoption presents unique challenges, particularly in cybersecurity. AI-driven cybersecurity solutions have been widely deployed to detect threats, automate responses, and enhance overall system security. Yet, despite their advantages, these systems face significant challenges, including adversarial learning and data poisoning, where attackers manipulate inputs or corrupt training data to compromise system integrity. Additionally, ethical concerns, model exploitability, and the complexity of defense mechanisms highlight the need for transparency and continuous adaptation. The opaque nature of many AI models further complicates trust, error mitigation, and the development of effective security strategies. This highlights the need for strong AI governance to ensure responsible, ethical, and regulatory-compliant AI applications in cybersecurity. In this paper, we discuss the principles of AI governance in detail and introduce a structured AI governance framework specifically designed for cybersecurity. Given the growing complexity of AI-driven security measures, our framework ensures AI technologies are implemented securely, ethically, and in alignment with organizational and regulatory requirements. By addressing governance challenges, our proposed framework contributes to the responsible and sustainable development of AI in cybersecurity, mitigating risks while maximizing its potential benefits.

title: Blockchain Based E-Tendering System
authors: Sindhu B, Aswini Gorli, Kishore Bobbili, Pavansankar Bheesetti
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The project investigates the application of blockchain technology, specifically Ethereum-based smart contracts, to develop a distributed e-tendering system aimed at addressing the security implications inherent in traditional procurement methods. Divided into four sections, it focuses on tender creation and publishing, bidding, evaluation, negotiation, and selection of the winning bid. Each phase employs distinct algorithms to ensure efficiency and fairness. By leveraging blockchain's decentralized nature and robust encryption, the system aims to enhance security and transparency, thereby fostering trust in the tendering process. Through a comprehensive evaluation, the project assesses the security and audibility challenges inherent in conventional methods and compares them with the proposed blockchain-based solution. Ultimately, the project seeks to establish a fair, transparent, and open tendering scheme, laying the groundwork for more efficient procurement practices in both governmental and corporate sectors.

title: BASSL-MI: Batch-Agnostic Self-Supervised Learning Uncovers Clinically Relevant Tumor Niches in Multiplexed Imaging
authors: Alexander Lin, Shunxing Bao, Ken Lau, Simon Vandekar, Daniel Moyer, Qi Liu, Siyuan Ma
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Multiplexed imaging enables rich, high-resolution characterization of the tumor microenvironment but relies on labor-intensive and error-prone cell segmentation and phenotyping pipelines. We present BASSL-MI, a batch-agnostic, self-supervised framework for discovering tissue niches directly from multiplexed imaging data. BASSL-MI operates directly on image patches, eliminating the need for explicit cell segmentation while mitigating image-, sample-, or batch-specific artifacts. Built on a modified contrastive block disentanglement architecture, BASSL-MI learns dual latent representations that separate biologically informative features from batch-dependent factors through spatially guided augmentations and batch-invariance objectives. Applied to a 56 -marker colorectal cancer CODEX dataset, BASSL-MI-trained embeddings markedly reduce image-specific variability and recover biologically interpretable spatial niches. Notably, it uncovers CD20-rich follicular regions associated with improved survival, outperforming published findings from cell segmentation-driven clustering. This work demonstrates that self-supervised, patch-based learning can capture clinically relevant spatial organization within tumor microenvironments, advancing toward automated, non-cell-based analysis of multiplexed imaging data.

title: Exploring Deep Learning Approaches for Lung Anomaly Detection: From Current Methods to Future Innovations
authors: Pavai D, G. Bhuvaneswari
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Generally, lung diseases pose significant health risks affecting respiration and require an early diagnosis. The growing availability of medical imaging allows deep learning to enhance the detection and segmentation of anomalies. CNN, Autoencoder, and GAN algorithms are mostly utilized. This review underscores a significant gap in effectively addressing subtle and early stage anomalies when labeled data is scarce. It describes the evolution from conventional supervised models and unsupervised models to sophisticated self-supervised learners and transformers, highlighting their strengths in feature learning, anomaly localization, and interpretability. The objective of this study is to present the remaining problems in relation to recent innovations in lung anomaly detection while illustrating the promise of self-supervised learning (SSL) combined with explainable AI (XAI) for clinical use.

title: Design of an Intelligent SCADA System Using Generative AI
authors: Rengaraj R, G R Venkatakrishnan, M Devesh Raj, Ariharasudhan A, Harish R, Keerthiraj N
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: The conventional approach of using static thresholds and requiring manual interpretation by operators in Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems restricts the ability of these systems to detect faults or provide predictive maintenance opportunities to critical infrastructure as early as possible. In this study, an intelligent SCADA system has been fully developed that incorporates both Machine Learning (ML) and Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) to increase the detection of anomalies, predictive maintenance and operator decision support in sewage water treatment systems. The proposed architecture includes a combination of high frequency data collection, feature extraction through statistics, ML based classification using an ensemble method and GenAI driven explanation of alerts created at run time. Testing was performed on simulations of a multi stage sewage water treatment process and demonstrated a 96.8% sensitivity, a 95.8% specificity and a 34.5 % decrease in Mean Time to Repair (MTTR). Additionally, testing revealed detection times were under 200ms and processing times were minimal. These results confirm that the intelligent SCADA system is operational, reliable and can be used in a production environment.

title: Modeling and Control of a Five-Port DC-DC Converter for Integrating Renewable Energy Sources
authors: Ravi Ranjan, Pragya Nand Singh, Srinivas Bhaskar Karanki
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This article presents a novel five-port converter (5PC) architecture that integrates multiple energy sources using a minimal number of components. A comprehensive mathematical model and control strategy are developed for one of the operating modes in which the biomass source is inactive. The 5PC interfaces solar PV, wind energy, a battery storage system (BSS), and biomass, while delivering a regulated output to the load. Maximum power point tracking (MPPT) is implemented for both the solar and wind sources to maximize energy extraction. In addition, the 5PC maintains a constant output voltage of 380 V. The converter is capable of adapting to varying energy source availability through mode-based operation.

title: A Low Profile, Small Size Asymmetric Parabolic Cylindrical Vortex Antenna Based on Anisotropic Holographic Metasurface
authors: Zhaoneng Jiang, Xinzeng Gu, He Shuang, Hui Liang, Bingzan Sun, Lang Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, an asymmetric parabolic cylindrical antenna based on anisotropic holographic impedance modulation surface is proposed. The anisotropic unit incorporates a slotted octagonal patch with adjustable structural parameters, which enables independent and flexible control of tensor impedance. Based on the proposed anisotropic unit, a holographic impedance surface is designed for loading onto a parabolic cylinder. Using a monopole antenna as the feed, the structure can generate high-purity OAM beams with modes of l = +1, +2, and −3. For verification, the antenna with l = +1 mode is fabricated and measured. Both measurement and simulation results demonstrate that the proposed antenna achieves high-purity OAM beam generation with a compact size of 4.5λ×4.5λ and an extremely low profile of 0.05λ. The proposed structure breaks the geometric limits of conventional planar or symmetric conformal designs, making it suitable for curved conformal platforms such as UAV skins and wearable devices.

title: A Multiple Spatial–Frequency EM Tag for Identification Improvement
authors: Yiwei Wang, Zihao Ning, Mengmeng Li, Yonggeng Zhu, Jihong Gu, Davide Ramaccia, Alessandro Toscano, Filiberto Bilotti, Dazhi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Benefiting from the precise electromagnetic (EM) wave manipulation capability of metasurface (MTS), MTS-based EM tags exhibit significant potential for advancement in the field of radio frequency identification (RFID). This article presents the theoretical analysis, design, and experimental verification of an MTS-based tag employing spatial–frequency coding. Unlike conventional RFID tags and prior MTS-based tags, the proposed tag is an active MTS-based identification platform integrating phase modulation circuits, power amplifiers (PAs), and polarization conversion antennas. By exploiting these functionalities, the tag modulates the interrogation signal to generate tunable harmonics, whose spatial combinations encode the tag information. The backscattered response is further polarization-converted and amplified, rendering it cross-polarized with respect to environmental clutter and extending the read range. An X-band prototype is fabricated, featuring a 700-MHz bandwidth and a 10-dB enhancement in backscattered power compared to a metallic plate of the same aperture. Experiments under strong reflection and long read range conditions verify the feasibility of the proposed tag.

title: Straggler-Aware Cross-DC Distributed Training for Large-Scale Models over OTNs
authors: Meng Lian, Yongli Zhao, Xin Li, Qiaolun Zhang, Memedhe Ibrahimi, Francesco Musumeci, Jie Zhang, Massimo Tornatore
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Large-scale machine learning models (LMs) demand massive computational and communication resources, motivating cross–data center (Cross-DC) distributed training over optical transport networks (OTNs). Even though OTNs provide energy-efficient and fine-grained bandwidth for gradient transmission, conventional federated learning (FL) for distributed training typically relies on a single–parameter-server (SPS) architecture. Such an SPS-based design encounters a compute bottleneck, as the limited bandwidth to access the parameter servers (PS) restricts scalability. Semi-decentralized federated learning (SD-FL) alleviates this issue by partitioning workers into multiple clusters, each communicating only with a local PS, thereby avoiding the SPS bottleneck. However, SD-FL also introduces further straggler effects due to intra- and inter cluster resource competition, which significantly degrade training efficiency (e.g., training throughput). To overcome these limitations, we formulate the SD-FL-over-OTN resource allocation problem as a mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model and propose a scalable heuristic algorithm named straggler-aware parameter server assignment (SAPSA). SAPSA integrates cluster-diameter-based pruning of candidate PSs with an iterative straggler-aware optimization to achieve efficient PS placement and balanced resource allocation, thereby improving on-time task completion (task success rate). Simulations across multiple OTN topologies show that SAPSA achieves near-optimal performance (within 10% of MILP). Compared with competitive heuristic baselines, SAPSA improves task success rate by up to 7%, mitigates straggler waiting time by over 28%, and reduces runtime by 48% via pruning. These results demonstrate that SAPSA enables scalable and straggler-resilient Cross-DC federated training for LMs over OTNs.

title: Design and Modeling of a Human-Powered Pneumatic Supply System: Application as a Pneumatic Source for Peristaltic Pumps
authors: Miku Tsubouchi, Masaki Kawano, Chiaki Yamazaki, Fumio Ito, Taro Nakamura
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study proposes a manually operated pneumatic supply system that operates without an external power source. Soft robots, known for their high compliance, are increasingly used to be utilized in various fields such as manufacturing, healthcare, and agriculture. However, they often rely on bulky pneumatic systems that require external power, limiting their applicability in extreme environments such as outer space and disaster-stricken areas. To overcome this limitation, this study introduces a pneumatic supply system that eliminates the need for conventional compressors and electric vacuum pumps by utilizing a manually operated pump to pressurize an air tank. A theoretical model was developed to determine the required tank specifications based on the air consumption and operating pressure of the actuator. Furthermore, a model was formulated to estimate the number of manual pumping cycles necessary to achieve the target pressure. Experimental validation identified key parameters and confirmed the model’s accuracy. The results demonstrated that the model is particularly effective for tanks with 1 L or larger. To assess its feasibility in extreme environments, the system was tested using a soft peristaltic pump designed to mimic intestinal peristalsis. The drive experiment verified that the pump successfully completed the target of five cycles, thereby validating the predictions of the model.

title: Advanced Ensemble Methods for Air Quality Prediction: A Comparative Study with Hybrid Physics-Guided Neural Networks
authors: Kothandaraman D, J Bhavana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The fast development of nations leads to severe public health problems and disease because of deteriorating air quality which makes India one of the countries with the worst pollution worldwide. The research introduces two new ensemble systems which predict Air Quality Index (AQI) through (1) a Weighted Multi-Model Ensemble that uses learned optimal fusion to combine base models XGBoost 65.7% and Gradient Boosting 30.5% for R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9793 and (2) a Hybrid RF-AirPhyNet framework which uses Random Forest statistical power to achieve R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9829 through physics-based residual correction. The research uses seven baseline models which include machine learning approaches (Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting) and deep learning models (LSTM, GRU, Bi-LSTM) and physics-based neural networks (AirPhyNet) with data from 43 Indian cities across multiple years (2015-2020, 29,531 observations). The experimental data shows that our Weighted Ensemble method delivers 10 times faster training speed than deep learning methods while achieving better results and our Hybrid model produces the highest overall performance through its combination of statistical learning and atmospheric physics knowledge. The feature importance analysis results demonstrate that the engineered temporal features (AQI rolling mean: 86.1%) serve as the primary factors which enable prediction success because ensemble methods with feature engineering outperform deep learning models when analyzing environmental data tables. The research project developed fundamental operational standards which AQI forecasting systems must meet to function properly.

title: Fault Detection for Quadruped Robots Based on Dynamic Decision-Making and Layered Elastic Weight Consolidation
authors: Peng Han, Xiaochen Xie, Yunjiang Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Quadruped robots operating in dynamic, unstructured environments demand robust fault detection to ensure safety and autonomy. Traditional model-based approaches, while interpretable, often fail to capture the complex nonlinear dynamics of legged locomotion due to unmodeled interactions and environmental uncertainties. Data-driven methods, particularly deep learning-based techniques, have shown promise in extracting fault patterns from high-dimensional sensor data. However, existing methods often struggle with complex spatio-temporal fault patterns, limited adaptability, and catastrophic forgetting when learning new faults. To address these challenges, we propose a Dynamic Spatio-Temporal Learning framework for Fault Diagnosis in Quadruped Robots, integrating Heterogeneous Graph-Augmented Spatiotemporal Convolutional Network (HG-STCN) and Deep Reinforcement Learning. The HG-STCN explicitly models the robot's topological structure for accurate spatio-temporal fault diagnosis, while a DQN dynamically optimizes computational resource allocation to balance real-time performance and reliability. In addition, Layered Elastic Weight Consolidation) enables incremental learning, allowing the system to adapt to new faults without forgetting prior knowledge. Experimental results demonstrate superior detection accuracy, robustness, and adaptability compared to baseline methods. This work advances the reliable, long-term autonomy of quadruped robots in challenging environments.

title: Augmenting Human Hazard Situational Awareness With Haptic Interface for Heterogeneous Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Yang Xing, Ziyue Wang, Xiangqi Kong, Weisi Guo, Antonios Tsourdos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Human-Machine Systems
abstract: As vehicle autonomy increases, human operators become more susceptible to distractions and a loss of situational awareness (SA) due to cognitive limitations. Rapidly enhancing human SA in hazardous situations is, therefore, critical for timely hazard perception and collision avoidance, particularly in human-vehicle teaming contexts that demand fast, accurate hazard reasoning. This study evaluates the efficiency of a low-cost vibrotactile interface for enhancing hazard SA of human operators when teaming with heterogeneous autonomous vehicles, including both ground and aerial autonomous vehicles. To do so, we evaluate the effectiveness of a vibrotactile interface in challenging time-critical scenarios considering adversarial attacks to better understand the cognitive constraints faced by human operators. Our quantitative analysis, based on the data collected from 39 participants, demonstrates that: first, haptic cues can significantly enhance human hazard SA across various metrics for the ground and aerial scenarios; second, perception of aerial attacks in a 3-D environment is more challenging than ground risk perception.

title: FGAIM: Identifying Drug-Target Activation and Inhibition Mechanisms via Inductive Graph Neural Networks Based on Fine-Grained Interaction Strategies
authors: Yi Tang, Yongxian Fan, Guicong Sun, Mengxin Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Distinguishing the activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets can reveal the potential regulatory pathways of target functions, which is crucial in drug discovery and development. Although numerous deep learning based computational methods have been proposed, most of them extract drug and target features independently while neglecting interactions between drug molecules and protein residues. In addition, existing methods approach drugs in a relatively simple way and predominantly rely on protein sequence information. Deep learning, particularly graph neural networks (GNNs), has demonstrated unique advantages in processing data with complex graph-structured relationships. Motivated by these limitations, this study proposes a novel computational computational method named FGAIM, which designed to effectively identify activation and inhibition mechanisms between drugs and targets. First, a multi-scale GNN module in FGAIM is employed to learn expressive drug molecular embeddings. At the same time, protein representations are constructed by integrating pre-trained language model (PLM) embeddings and structural information derived from 3D conformations. Subsequently, FGAIM leverages a GraphSAGE module to extract features from both the primary drug graph and the fine-grained drug-protein interaction graph. Finally, the resulting drug and target embeddings are fused and fed into a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) for classification prediction. Comparative experiments on two public datasets demon strate that FGAIM significantly outperforms existing computa tional approaches and exhibits strong generalization capabilities. Further case studies reveal the advantages of FGAIM in min ing previously unrecognized activation/inhibition relationships. Building upon the fine-grained interactions, we further explored the model's interpretability at the molecular structural level through analysis of attention weights. Additionally, visualization analyses confirm that FGAIM effectively captures underlying structural patterns in the data and identifies discriminative features across different categories.

title: Smart Hiring Platform: An AI-based Resume Analysis and Candidate Matching System
authors: Selva Sheela K, Lokeshwar L, Teja Swaroop N
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Hiring via the internet has now become extremely quick and it is becoming difficult to choose the right people, identify any counterfeit content and make the process fair. The majority of Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) are still only searching for keywords and basic rules, therefore, lacking context, latent skills, and emerging job requirements. This paper is all about an AI-based Smart Hiring Platform which OCRs resumes, employs a mixed NLP architecture, fancy semantic embeddings, and scores a candidate using a composite Smart Matching system. Transformer embeddings and similarity checks accomplish all the skill digging, and even the AI is capable of grading role-specific tests with just the right amount of difficulty. We tested the real and the fake resumes and find it to be more accurate, more relevant suggestions, and a significant reduction in the time spent in screening as compared to the old ATS and the National keyword systems. The entire process achieves an approximate accuracy of 91 per cent and can be supported by the maxed out resumes attempting to cheat the system with keyword cramming or other false gimmicks. So system works based on the Combining semantic insights with a solid scoring rubric provides a scalably fair and data-driven method of managing contemporary hiring.

title: Transistor-Level Optimization and Performance Analysis of a CMOS Op-Amp-Based Comparator for Biomedical Signals
authors: Mansi Jain, P. P. Bansod, Rajesh Khatri, Disha Sharma, Mohit Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: In this article, we introduce the design, simulation, and layout implementation of a two-stage CMOS operational amplifier employed as a comparator, using SCL 180 nm technology, targeting low-frequency biomedical applications. The design approach emphasizes transistor-level width and length optimization, aiming for an optimal balance between gain, stability, and power efficiency, keeping simplicity in design structure. The proposed design offers an impressive gain of 100 dB, ensuring proper detection of low-amplitude differential signals, and boasts an exceptional phase margin of 179°, verifying extremely stable operation, irrespective of oscillation and noise effects. Transient analysis verifies proper operation of the proposed comparator design, while a mere measured peak transient power of 1.955 µW verifies its compatibility for implementation in power-restricted biomedical applications. The proposed design has been implemented at the layout level employing standard CMOS layout design principles, verifying DRC and LVS checks, ensuring proper correlation of the layout layout and technology consistency. The results obtained verify the efficacy of our proposed approach, and it proves that our proposed design qualifies as an apt, stable, and energy-efficient candidate for biomedical analog front-end circuits and process for evaluation.

title: Enterprise Resilience of Semiconductor Supply Chains With Critical Materials and Extreme Weather
authors: Davis C. Loose, Negin Moghadasi, Zachary A. Collier, Elvie Sellers, Igor Linkov, James H. Lambert
publish: IEEE Systems Journal
abstract: Supply chains of semiconductors are integral to the global digital economy though are vulnerable to disruptions. This article examines the cascading effects of disruptions to semiconductor supply chains through a discrete event simulation (DES) framework. The paper explores two scenarios: 1) Weather-induced disruptions, such as extreme weather events that affect logistics and material availability, and 2) material supply shortages coupled with natural disaster risks, such as flooding and high winds. The approach describes how weather-related disruptions affect transportation and logistics, potentially leading to increased operational costs and delays, while material shortages impact production capacities directly. By employing DES, we assess the effectiveness of several mitigation strategies, including inventory management and supplier diversification. The results highlight the importance of adaptive strategies to enhance the resilience of semiconductor supply chains. The article, thus, illuminates complexities and interdependencies of these supply chains and provides insights for assuring supply chain functionality in the face of disruptions.

title: Discrete Time Delay Control for Dexterous Hand Joint Position Tracking Under Unknown Kinematics
authors: Gan Wang, Hongjie Li, Changliang Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Precise joint position control in tendon-driven dexterous hands with unknown kinematics is challenging due to the difficulty in accurately mapping joint angles to electric cylinder displacement. Furthermore, existing time delay control methods present inherent trade-offs, such as large overshoot due to integral windup in step responses or nonzero steady-state errors during time-varying signals tracking, limited by fixed forgetting factors. To address these issues, this article establishes a dynamically accurate mapping model by lumping hard-to-model parts into an uncertainty term, which is estimated in real time. After analyzing the causes of the limitations of existing methods, an enhanced method incorporating a forgetting factor and a prediction term is proposed, immune to tendon wear and elasticity. The forgetting factor is adjusted automatically to suppress integration during large errors and restore it near steady state, thereby balancing overshoot and accuracy. The prediction term, designed based on the residual error, adds an immediate proportional compensation component that accelerates system response. Then, the compensation mechanism is theoretically analyzed, and parameter tuning guidelines are provided under explicit stability constraints. Comparative experiments on a dexterous hand validate the superiority of the proposed method in achieving low overshoot, short settling time, and high tracking accuracy.

title: Fault Level Considerations in Tomorrow’s Wind and PV-Rich Power Grids: A Review
authors: Rafat Aljarrah, Mazaher Karimi, Hesamoddin Marzooghi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The increased penetration of Renewable Energy Sources (RESs), like Photovoltaic (PV) and wind energy, in transmission and distribution networks is changing features of current and future power systems. RESs may alter the characteristics of power systems due to their different dynamic behavior and limitations compared to centralized conventional Synchronous Generators (SGs). This is mainly attributed to Power Electronics (PE) interfaces (i.e., converters) utilized for integration with the power grid. As PE-based RESs show low and significantly different fault current contributions compared to SGs, fault level reduction is deemed to be one of the main challenges in future power systems. Besides, such RESs may bring new dynamics to short-circuit (SC) current characteristics of power systems, which in turn might cause challenges related to the strength, stability, operation, and protection of the grids. To tackle such challenges, it is essential to understand better the SC characteristics of the different RESs, specifically Wind and PV systems. In addition, evaluating their impact on the system fault level is necessary, especially in future power scenarios where most of the power is expected to be met by RESs. Hence, this study provides a comprehensive review of the SC current contributions of RESs and their impact on SC dynamics and the system fault level, addressing key challenges and gaps in the literature for researchers and utility engineers.

title: Cognitive and Fuzzy Models to Understand Learning Behaviors: AI for Enhanced Learning Pedagogy and Decision Making
authors: Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Srijana Karnatak, Kabulov Kosimboy, Teshaboyev Dilmurod, Rakhmatov Avazbek, Mahliyo Khaydarova
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The reason why it is important to know the behavior of the learners is so that adaptive and efficient learning systems will be developed. The conventional learning systems are largely learning platforms that are founded on the outcome measures and have no way of modeling the mental states and uncertainty that prevail in human learning. The current paper suggests the use of the framework steered by artificial intelligence which would integrate cognitive modeling and fuzzy logic to understand the learning behaviors and assist the pedagogical decision making. The cognitive models, that involve the knowledge acquisition, engagement, and the psychological burden of learners and the fuzzy models that address the ambiguity in behavioral and emotional indicators by relying on linguistic reasoning. The proposed system to be discussed involves a fuzzy inference-based mechanism of cognitive states to adaptive instructional decisions viz. content personalization, modification of feedback and intervention strategies. The use of experimental evaluation has demonstrated higher predictability of learner performance, high levels of engagement and decision making, as opposed to the traditional rule-based and machine learning methods. These findings justify the appropriateness of using the combination of cognitive and fuzzy models in improving the pedagogy of learning in the intelligent educational system.

title: Research on Electromagnetic Measurement and Assessment of Fast-Moving Dynamic Wireless Power Transfer System
authors: Yanling Li, Qin Zhang, Linxin Zhou, Yuxiao Fan, Shunpan Liu, Yong Li, Zhengyou He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) systems have been widely used in electrified transportation, raising concerns about electromagnetic exposure as a key public safety issue. Existing research provides extensive guidance for quasi-static field measurements in electric vehicles. However, the DWPT systems exhibit non-uniform electromagnetic field (EMF) distributions and transient current surges, posing challenges for advancing electromagnetic assessment. This paper aims to analyze the impact of dynamic speed and segmented coils on the EMF distribution in the DWPT system. By identifying areas with significant field variations, the sizes and functions of sampling and evaluation areas are determined. By ranking all sampling points using Pearson correlation analysis, the key measured points for predicting the spatially averaged and maximum magnetic flux density (MFD) in the DWPT system are proposed. A rotating prototype of the fast-moving DWPT system is constructed, and the experimental values at key measured points are fitted to compute the spatially averaged and maximum MFD in the evaluation area. Compared with the scanning values, the maximum errors for the fitted spatially averaged and maximum MFD are 8.9% and 8.4%, respectively, validating the effectiveness of the proposed key measured points.

title: Analysis of Dynamic Response of VSG-Controlled Grid-Forming Inverter Under Fault Conditions
authors: Aminu Jibrin Aliyu, Ramani Kannan, Ho Tatt Wei, David Enemali Abah, Rabby Md Afser Jani, Nguyen Gia Minh Thao
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
