publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is critical in medical image segmentation, where model predictions directly impact clinical decisions. However, widely used UQ methods like Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensembles rely on sampling strategies that increase inference time and complicate deployment. To address these limitations, we propose ENSE, a Dirichletbased framework tailored for multiclass segmentation. ENSE replaces the conventional softmax head with a Dirichlet formulation, enabling simultaneous prediction of class probabilities and voxel-wise uncertainty in a single forward pass. Built upon a 2.5 D Attention U-Net, ENSE is trained using a composite loss that balances segmentation accuracy and uncertainty calibration. We benchmark ENSE against deterministic and Bayesian-inspired baselines across three anatomical regions, showing strong segmentation performance and wellcalibrated uncertainty maps. These results highlight ENSE's potential to improve the reliability of automated segmentation systems in clinical environments.

title: Design of Low-Power, High-Performance Hybrid Full Adder Circuit
authors: K J Khavin, V Lalith Vishnu, T Ravi, Bhuvana B P, Yokesh V
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Applications based Hybrid logic styles in low-power VLSI design. Hybrid logic styles have received considerable attention in system design because they provide the benefits of more than one circuit design technique. The hybrid full adder presented in this work is a hybrid design based on the Pass Transistor Logic (PTL) and complementary CMOS design in 90 nm CMOS technology. The suggested architecture is created based on an effective XOR-XNOR module, which is used as the main component of the sum and carry generation circuits. To improve the output voltages, two design variants are used, namely, a 13-transistor (13T) hybrid adder without swing restoration and a 15-transistor (15T) hybrid adder with swing restoration. The operation of the proposed adders is assessed in terms of average power analysis of the operating state when working at low power and in different operating frequencies. Simulation findings indicate that the 13 T design will be less power, its transistor count was very low and the 15 T design is better in signal integrity with full the swing outputs with a small power increment. The given hybrid adder provides a good compromise between power consumption and voltage swing, which allows its use in the low power and high-speed arithmetic of deep submicron VLSI based systems.

title: Hybrid Cryptography Approaches for Securing Data Transmission in Wireless Body Sensor Networks
authors: M. Shamila, Swati, Shitanshu Jain, A. Gopi, Yogesh Ramaswamy, Manish Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The Wireless Body Sensor Networks (WBSNs) require security measures, which are capable of resisting the ever-changing cyber-threats without violating the tight computational and energy limits. The conventional hybrid cryptography products are based on the combination of fixed symmetric primitives and asymmetric primitives, which can cast some vulnerabilities to key-predictability, small entropy, and even increasing post-quantum threats. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper presents a new security architecture nicknamed Adaptive Multiphase Cryptographic Fusion (AMCF) which is aimed at fostering a dynamically fashioned cryptography landscape that is optimal towards wearable physiological sensing systems. AMCF combines three fundamental innovations: Physiological micro-variation and chaotic micro-reactor combining physiological micro-variation and chaotic dynamics to produce per-segment ephemeral keys; Multiphase Encryption Core for switching between various encryption “phases” from a lightweight symmetric cryptography to hybrid elliptic curve encryption (HEC) and finally to quantum resilient lattice-based encryption; Micro-Segment Transport Encoder to chop up the transmissions into independently encrypted pieces called “micro-units” to avoid interception and reconstructing under interception. A decisionmaking system that is sensitive to energy is used to select a phase that enables a robust security but does not impact the battery life. Cooperation of adaptive cryptographic depth, physiological entropy harvesting and probabilistic engagement of postquantum components in AMCF, improves significantly confidentiality and resistance to interception, spoofing, and future cryptographic attacks. The suggested approach lays the groundwork of safe, scalable, and energy-congruous information security in wearable healthcare systems of the next generation. The proposed AMCF technique attained an overall accuracy of 97.4%.

title: Dynamic performance analysis of BESS in grid disturbance scenarios
authors: Pola Soma Sekhar Reddy, J. Sreedevi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This paper investigates the dynamic response and stabilizing capabilities of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in a power grid under various disturbance scenarios using PSS®E software simulations. Four cases are examined: sudden loss of a large hydro generator, abrupt load shedding, a three-phase fault, and generator loss under different BESS droop settings. The study demonstrates that BESS plays a critical role in maintaining grid stability by supporting both frequency and voltage regulation. In the case of generation loss, BESS discharges active power to limit frequency decline, while during load shedding, it absorbs power to mitigate over-frequency conditions. Under fault conditions, BESS helps reduce rotor angle oscillations and enhances voltage recovery. Additionally, the impact of droop settings on frequency control performance is analysed, highlighting the need for optimal parameter tuning. The results confirm that BESS significantly enhances system resilience and dynamic performance, making it a valuable asset in modern power systems with high renewable penetration.

title: An Optimized Graph Neural Network Framework Using Whale Optimization Algorithm for Patient Medication Cost Prediction
authors: Dhinesh Kumar K.S, R. Annamalai
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of patient medication costs is important for healthcare budgeting, insurance risk assessment, and personalized treatment planning. Traditional machine learning models often fail to capture the complex relational dependencies amongst patients, diseases, prescriptions, and healthcare providers, which can lead to inaccurate predictions of medication costs and hinder effective healthcare decisionmaking. To address this limitation, this paper proposes an optimization-driven Graph Neural Network (GNN) framework improved with the Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) for accurate medication cost forecasting. The proposed model represents healthcare data as a heterogeneous graph, where nodes correspond to patients, medications, diagnoses, and healthcare providers, while edges signify their interactions and prescription relationships. The GNN learns structural and contextual dependencies through message-passing mechanisms, allowing accurate representation of multi-relational healthcare patterns. WOA is used to modify important hyperparameters such as the learning rate, embedding dimensions, number of graph convolution layers, and regularization coefficients to improve prediction accuracy. WOA's global search capacity helps to avoid local minima and progresses convergence stability. In terms of RMSE, MAE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> metrics, results on real-world healthcare spending datasets show that WOA-optimized GNN performs better than conventional regression models and nonoptimized graph-based models. All things considered, the suggested approach offers a scalable and clever way to support data-driven financial decision-making in clinical settings and economical healthcare planning.

title: A Novel Diagonal Convolutional Network for Industrial Polluting Gas Recognition via E-noses
authors: Changfu Zhang, Jia Yan
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: Electronic nose (e-nose) systems face challenges in achieving high accuracy and robustness when complex volatile compounds are analyzed due to limitations in traditional machine learning methods. To address this, we propose the diagonal convolutional network (DiagCN), a novel deep learning framework that incorporates multidirectional convolution and residual downsampling for enhanced feature extraction from time series of gas sensor data. By converting raw signals into Gramian Angular Field (GAF) images and employing direction-adaptive convolution kernels, DiagCN effectively captures diagonal-dominant patterns while maintaining computational efficiency. The experimental results on an industrial gas dataset demonstrate that DiagCN achieves 97.22% accuracy, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art models. The proposed method offers a lightweight yet high-performance solution for e-nose applications in environmental monitoring.

title:
Multimodal MRI and
                    <sup>18</sup>
                    F-FPIA PET for Non-Invasive IDH Mutation Prediction Using MRI-Foundation Model Embeddings
authors: Marianna Inglese, Shah Islam, Laura McLeavy, Francesco Mauri, Tara Barwick, Adam D Waldman, Kevin O'Neill, Matthew Williams, Eric O Aboagye, Nicola Toschi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Non-invasive prediction of isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) mutation status in gliomas remains a major challenge. We assessed the applicability of an MRI-based foundation model for IDH classification in two complementary settings. (1) MRI-only setting: XGBoost classifiers were trained on the BraTS dataset and externally validated on an independent clinical 18F-FPIA PET-MRI (mpFPIA) cohort using features derived exclusively from MRI sequences (T1, FLAIR, ASL, ADC). (2) Multimodal PET-MRI setting: static 18F-FPIA-PET-derived embeddings were integrated with MRI embeddings, and performance was evaluated via 5 -fold crossvalidation within the mpFPIA cohort. In the MRI-only analysis, the T1+FLAIR+ASL combination achieved the strongest external validation (accuracy 0.80, AUC 0.90) after dimensionality reduction to five principal components. When PET embeddings were incorporated, the multimodal model reached its highest accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.90 \pm 0.20)$</tex>, driven primarily by PET. These findings provide preliminary evidence that MRItrained foundation model embeddings transfer effectively to PET, supporting unified multimodal pipelines for molecular glioma characterization.

title: On the Robustness of Foundational 3D Medical Image Segmentation Models Against Imprecise Visual Prompts
authors: Soumitri Chattopadhyay, Başar Demir, Marc Niethammer
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: While 3D foundational models have shown promise for promptable segmentation of medical volumes, their robustness to imprecise prompts remains under-explored. In this work, we aim to address this gap by systematically studying the effect of various controlled perturbations of dense visual prompts, that closely mimic real-world imprecision. By conducting experiments with two recent foundational models on a multi-organ abdominal segmentation task, we reveal several facets of promptable medical segmentation, especially pertaining to reliance on visual shape and spatial cues, and the extent of resilience of models towards certain perturbations. Codes are available at: https://github.com/ucsdbiag/Prompt-Robustness-MedSegFMs

title: Labor Relationship Management Based on Big Data and Perception Algorithm Control
authors: Yao Tang, Hongbing Yi, Lingzi Zhang, Xiuqin Lv
publish: 2025 Asian Conference on Artificial Intelligence Technology (ACAIT)
abstract: In order to ensure the maintenance and development of labor relations and improve recruitment efficiency, an innovative labor relations management method has been developed.This method first collects structured information through big data technology and calculates its similarity.Then, a classification method based on Bert and feature embedding is used to classify positions, and a perception algorithm is used to match positions with job seekers.The experimental results show that this method achieves 85.4% in matching accuracy and 82.0% in F1 Score metric, both of which are higher than other algorithms.The ablation experiment further confirmed the contributions of each component in the model, especially when the bidirectional long short-term memory network Attention, Word2Vec, bidirectional gated recurrent unit, and convolutional neural network module are used in combination to achieve optimal performance.The above results indicate that the proposed method has strong potential in labor relations management, especially in improving the accuracy of job seeker job matching.

title: Soft-Start Control for SS-WPT in EV Charging
authors: Amirreza Mizani, Azam Jaman, Sajad Saberi, Jaber Abu Qahouq
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The Series-Series (SS) compensation topology is widely adopted in wireless power transfer (WPT) systems due to its simple structure and load-independent characteristics. However, it is highly susceptible to short-circuit and open-circuit conditions, particularly during startup, which can cause severe inrush currents and voltage spikes. In electric vehicle (EV) applications, these transients pose significant safety and reliability challenges when transferring power from the transmitter to the receiver. Specifically, short-circuit conditions can lead to excessive inrush currents on the receiver side, while open-circuit conditions may result in high currents on the transmitter side, consequently producing overvoltage on the receiver side and potentially damaging semiconductor devices. To address these issues, the proposed method regulates the voltage applied to the transmitter coil through gradual switch activation and switching frequency adjustment. This soft-start strategy effectively mitigates both short-circuit and open-circuit risks during system startup. The proposed control approach is thoroughly analyzed, and its effectiveness is experimentally validated using a 1.4-kW laboratory prototype.

title: A Real-Time Speech-to-Sign Language Translation Framework Using Transformer-Based Gesture Synthesis
authors: Hemantha Kumar B N, Vijay Kumar S, Suchithra H N, Sachitha M S, Shivaprasad B M, Prashanth R Kaigaddi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Effective communication between hearing individuals and the deaf community continues to be a great challenge because of the linguistic complexity and the visual-spatial element of sign languages. This paper introduces a real-time speech-to-sign language translation framework based on Transformer-based gesture synthesis for natural, expressive and interactive communication. The proposed system includes the Conformer Automatic Speech Recognition module for converting speech to text accurately, and then the Transformer linguistic reordering model to generate sign gloss sequences in accord to the sign language grammar. An editable, intermediate, and human readable representation in a format of editable interview with elements that allow a form of human-in-the-loop correction to allow users to “refine” hand shapes, facial expressions, timing and emphasis. For the task of generating gestures, a Transformer - Mixture Density Network is used to generate smooth and probabilistic joint-level movement trajectories in which both manual and non-manual sign components are taken into consideration. The system utilizes motion statistics based upon motion data from the How2Sign dataset to increase the expressiveness and realism of the system. Experimental evaluation shows the overall accuracy is 92.8% with low end-to-end latency of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 3 m s}$</tex> guaranteeing real-time performance. User studies have further shown that there is an improvement in comprehension along with an increase in trust and decreased cognitive load. The framework as proposed is a comprehensible and scalable way of providing a practical solution for inclusive speech-to-sign language communication.

title: Comparative Analysis of EMI in Full-Bridge Inverters with Si and SiC Power Devices
authors: Sarvvesh R, Dinakar R, Saniya Ahamad, Sai Charan K, Soniya Agrawal, Prema V
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: The versatility and simplicity of the full-bridge inverter make it essential for modern systems like electric vehicles and solar inverters. Silicon (Si) devices remain prevalent due to their cost-effectiveness, reliability, and mature manufacturing. Conversely, silicon carbide (SiC) devices offer higher thermal performance, greater breakdown voltage, and enable higher switching frequencies, reducing losses and system size. However, the fast switching of SiC devices introduces increased electromagnetic interference (EMI) and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) concerns due to higher dv/dt rates. This paper presents a comparative EMI analysis of a full-bridge inverter using Si and SiC power devices. The study further focuses on the separation and evaluation of common-mode (CM) and differential-mode (DM) EMI components to better understand their individual contributions to total conducted noise. LTSpice, an open-source circuit simulation software, is utilized to model and analyze EMI behavior at a switching frequency of 50 kHz.

title: PulmoNet: A Deep Learning Framework for Pneumonia Detection in Chest X-Ray Images with Feature Analysis
authors: Amjad Albashayreh, Maria Yousef, Loai Al-Alnemer, Alhareth Abdelmuhdi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Smart Multidomain Integrated Learning Environments (ICSMILE)
abstract: Pneumonia remains a major global health challenge, underscoring the need for accurate and automated diagnostic solutions. Previous studies have widely applied transfer learning–based deep learning models for pneumonia detection; however, many rely on a limited range of architectures and provide insufficient analysis of model interpretability and decision-making behavior. To address these limitations, this paper proposes PulmoNet, a comprehensive deep learning framework for pneumonia detection that systematically evaluates multiple high-capacity architectures while integrating feature-level explainability analysis. The proposed framework encompasses data collection, preprocessing, feature engineering, model training, performance evaluation, and explainable feature attribution. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that among the evaluated architectures, ResNet152 achieved the best performance, attaining an accuracy of 94.39% and an F1-score of $94.31 \%$, with a detection time of 13.31 seconds. Beyond classification accuracy, the framework incorporates explainability techniques to enhance transparency and clinical interpretability. These findings suggest that PulmoNet provides a structured and interpretable evaluation pipeline that can support reliable and efficient pneumonia diagnosis in clinical settings.

title: Realizing High-Q Quasi-BIC Through Monolithic Dimer Metasurface in SOI Platform
authors: Abdullah Bin Shams, Shadman Shahriar Sharar, J Stewart Aitchison
publish: 2026 IEEE Silicon Photonics Conference (SiPhotonics)
abstract: We introduce a novel monolithic dimer meta-atom design that exhibits a coupled resonant behavior characteristic of a conventional dimer. Such metasurface, relative to dimers, demonstrate higher Q-factor, lower fabrication error and less optimization parameters. These attributes are ideal to achieve an experimental high-Q Quasi-Bound-States-in-Continuum.

title: Positional Segmentor-Guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning for Spatially Localized Image Synthesis
authors: Tian Xia, Matthew Sinclair, Andreas Schuh, Fabio De Sousa Ribeiro, Raghav Mehta, Rajat Rasal, Esther Puyol-Antón, Samuel Gerber, Kersten Petersen, Michiel Schaap, Ben Glocker
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Counterfactual image generation enables controlled data augmentation, bias mitigation, and disease modeling. However, existing methods guided by external classifiers or regressors are limited to subject-level factors (e.g., age) and fail to produce localized structural changes, often resulting in global artifacts. Pixel-level guidance using segmentation masks has been explored but requires user-defined counterfactual masks, which is tedious and impractical. Segmentor-guided Counterfactual Fine-Tuning (Seg-CFT) addressed this by using segmentation-derived measurements to supervise structurespecific variables, yet it remains restricted to global interventions. We propose Positional Seg-CFT, which subdivides each structure into regional segments and derives independent measurements per region, enabling spatially localized and anatomically coherent counterfactuals. Experiments on coronary CT angiography (CCTA) show that Pos-Seg-CFT generates realistic, region-specific modifications, providing finer spatial control for modeling disease progression.

title: Ranking Xai Methods for Head and Neck Cancer Outcome Prediction
authors: Baoqiang Ma, Djennifer K. Madzia-Madzou, Rosa C.J. Kraaijveld, Jin Ouyang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: For head and neck cancer (HNC) patients, prognostic outcome prediction can support personalized treatment strategy selection. Improving prediction performance of HNC outcomes has been extensively explored by using advanced artificial intelligence (AI) techniques on PET/CT data. However, the interpretability of AI remains a critical obstacle for its clinical adoption. Unlike previous HNC studies that empirically selected explainable AI (XAI) techniques, we are the first to comprehensively evaluate and rank <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3}$</tex> XAI methods across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}$</tex> metrics, covering faithfulness, robustness, complexity and plausibility. Experimental results on the multi-center HECKTOR challenge dataset show large variations across evaluation aspects among different XAI methods, with Integrated Gradients (IG) and DeepLIFT (DL) consistently obtained high rankings for faithfulness, complexity and plausibility. This work highlights the importance of comprehensive XAI method evaluation and can be extended to other medical imaging tasks.

title: Geometric, Photometric, and Semantic Multi-Layer 3D Mapping Towards a Forest Digital Twin
authors: Dinis S. Simões, Arush Mendon, M. Eduarda Andrada, Afonso E. Carvalho, Mário P. Cristóvão, Tobias Marsh-Hulland, Duarte Cruz, André M. Silva, Luís M. Gaspar, Paulo Peixoto, David Portugal, Carlos Viegas, Domingos X. Viegas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Autonomous Robot Systems and Competitions (ICARSC)
abstract: Accurate, rich 3D forest representations are essential for perception, analysis, scene understanding and decision-making in woodland and off-road rugged terrain scenarios. This paper presents an approach for multi-layer 3D mapping for forest environments, integrating geometric, photometric, and semantic representations towards unified spatial models.The geometric layer is constructed using a state-of-the-art factor graph–based SLAM formulation with global georeferencing, enabling consistent large-scale mapping. The photometric layer is obtained through precise multi-sensor registration between LiDAR and RGB imagery, allowing the projection of color information onto the 3D structure. For the semantic layer, a transformer-based deep learning model is employed to perform dense semantic segmentation, enriching the map with comprehensive classification of entities, terrain, and structures.Experimental results in a real-world wildland-urban interface environment highlight the capabilities and limitations of each layer, as well as their combined suitability for off-road rural scene representation. The proposed mapping methods constitute a key step towards interpretable digital twins for forest management.

title: A Multimodal Slice Discovery Framework for Systematic Failure Detection and Explanation in Medical Image Classification
authors: Yixuan Liu, Kanwal K. Bhatia, Ahmed E. Fetit
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Despite advances in machine learning-based medical image classifiers, the safety and reliability of these systems remain major concerns in practical settings. Existing auditing approaches mainly rely on unimodal features or metadata-based subgroup analyses, which are limited in interpretability and often fail to capture hidden systematic failures. To address these limitations, we introduce the first automated auditing framework that extends slice discovery methods to multimodal representations specifically for medical applications. Comprehensive experiments were conducted under common failure scenarios using the MIMIC-CXR-JPG dataset, demonstrating the framework's strong capability in both failure discovery and explanation generation. Our results also show that multimodal information generally allows more comprehensive and effective auditing of classifiers, while unimodal variants beyond image-only inputs exhibit strong potential in scenarios where resources are constrained.

title: Immersive 3D Virtual Laboratory with an AI-Powered Pedagogical Avatar for Remote Practical Learning
authors: Abdelali El Gourari, Mustapha Ezzini, Mohammed Ennejjar, Mustapha Raoufi, Mohammed Skouri
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Global Aeronautical Engineering and Satellite Technology (GAST)
abstract: Remote practical education faces significant challenges in replicating hands-on laboratory experiences. This paper presents a novel immersive 3D virtual laboratory integrated with an AI-powered pedagogical avatar for electronics education. The system combines a web-based architecture (Next.js) with an interactive Three.js environment featuring realistic component manipulation on a virtual breadboard. A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) avatar provides contextual assistance using a specialized electrical engineering knowledge base. Preliminary results demonstrate significant improvements in student engagement and comprehension compared to traditional remote learning approaches.

title: SFENet: Shallow Feature Excitation for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Lei Zhang, Zihuang Wu, Xinyu Xiong, Ming Li, Guanbin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Retinal vessel segmentation is crucial for ophthalmic diagnosis, yet deploying deep learning models on mobile devices remains challenging due to high computational complexity. We propose a lightweight framework, SFENet, that achieves accurate segmentation with minimal resource requirements. Unlike traditional U-Net-based models that rely on deep, computationally intensive encoders, SFENet employs a streamlined single-branch architecture and emphasizes shallow features. Its core innovations include three modules: a hierarchical receptive field module (HRFM) for efficient multi-scale feature extraction, a feature refinement module (FRM) for enhancing vessel detail through dual-branch fusion, and a spatial context mining module (SCMM) for modeling long-range dependencies to ensure vessel continuity. This design significantly reduces model parameters and inference time while maintaining high accuracy, making SFENet suitable for real-time applications on edge devices. Experiments on multiple public datasets confirm that SFENet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Learnable Total Variation with Lambda Mapping for Low-Dose CT Denoising
authors: Yusuf Talha Başak, Mehmet Ozan Unal, Metin Ertas, Isa Yildirim
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: While Total Variation (TV) excels in noise reduction and edge preservation, its reliance on a scalar regularization parameter limits adaptivity. In this study, we present a Learnable Total Variation (LTV) framework coupling an unrolled TV solver with a LambdaNet that predicts a per-pixel regularization map. The proposed framework is trained end-to-end to optimize reconstruction and regularization jointly, yielding spatially adaptive smoothing. Experiments on the DeepLesion dataset, using realistic LoDoPaB-CT simulation, show consistent gains over classical TV and FBP+U-Net, achieving up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 3. 7} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> PSNR and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 \%}$</tex> relative SSIM improvement. LTV provides an interpretable alternative to black-box CNNs for low-dose CT denoising.

title: An Ultra Compact RGB LED Driver with Only One Off-Chip Component, 97.25% Peak Efficiency, and Nearly Full-Range Color Gamut Coverage
authors: Wangkang Lan, Caiyu Tong, Yuan Gao
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: The proliferation of intelligent electric vehicles imposes stringent demands on energy efficiency, integration, and reliability for on-board LED drivers, especially in RGB multi-color applications (e.g., automotive ambient lighting) requiring precise color mixing. Conventional LDO or DC-DC buck-boost solutions cannot simultaneously achieve high efficiency and high integration. This paper presents a highly integrated 3-switch RGB LED driver that requires only one off-chip inductor. Its topology merges DC-DC converter switches with RGB channel control switches, reducing switching losses and enhancing integration. The proposed design ensures robust operation against input voltage variation. An integration-comparison control scheme enables stable regulation of RGB color ratios. The driver maintains high efficiency across a wide color gamut and offers an efficiency–color gamut trade-off, enabling expanded gamut coverage at an acceptable efficiency cost. Fabricated in a 180nm CMOS process, the prototype IC achieves a peak efficiency of 97.25% under 5V input with mixed-color light output, demonstrating its potential for energy-efficient, space-constrained automotive LED applications.

title: An IoT-Enabled Deterministic Computational Model for Real-Time Water Quality Assessment of the River Ganges
authors: Yugansh Sachdeva, Achintya Singhal, Manoj Kumar Singh, Vimal Dungdung
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Emerging Computational Intelligence (ICECI)
abstract: The River Ganges is under enormous pressure due to rapid urban expansion, industrial effluent discharge, agricultural runoff, and increasing hydrological variability. Conventional laboratory-based water-quality monitoring, while analytically sound, suffers from limited temporal resolution and is often unable to record short-term and rapidly evolving changes in water quality. This paper presents an Internet of Things based deterministic (rule-based and reproducible) computational framework for water-quality assessment of the River Ganges. The system integrates in-situ sensing with a computational pipeline aligned with the Central Pollution Control Board guidelines. The pipeline performs parameter transformation, sub-index generation (parameter-wise quality scoring), and weighted aggregation to compute the water quality index. The primary contribution of this work is the development of a transparent, interpretable, and operationally viable framework intended to serve as a foundation for future large-scale deployments. The framework was validated under controlled laboratory conditions across three water-quality scenarios, confirming correct Central Pollution Control Boardaligned Water Quality Index computation across full classification range. Field deployment along the River Ganges is currently in progress. The developed framework provides a structured basis for continuous water-quality surveillance and offers a scalable approach to the development of digitally enabled river management practices.

title: Fault Classification in Power Transmission Lines Using Entropy-Aided Temporal Windowing and Hybrid Distance Measures
authors: Nirmalya Mallick, Chandan Kumar Shiva, Vedik Basetti, Sachidananda Sen, V. Mukherjee, Shriram S. Rangarajan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: The present power system requires a fault detection system which can identify faults with both speed and accuracy to maintain system stability while avoiding equipment damage. The research studies a fault classification method which uses entropy to classify faults through its application of optimized wavelet transforms and hybrid distance measurement techniques. The method uses temporal windowing to extract important fault data through its implementation. The adaptive wavelet selection tool delivers its most suitable frequency output. The development of hybrid distance classification framework enables automatic parameter optimization to function as its matching component. The test-bed achieved exceptional results with 95% total accuracy and perfect area-under-curve results and F1-scores which ranged from 0.80 to 1.00.

title: Intelligent Correlation of Indicators of Compromise Using AI for Proactive Threat Detection
authors: Orieb Abualghanam, Hadeel Alazzam, Malik Al-Essa, Wesam Almobaideen, Mohammad Qatawneh
publish: 2026 1st International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Engineering Systems (ICETES)
abstract: The increasing complexity of cyber threats, characterized by sophisticated multi-stage attacks, exposes a fundamental weakness that conventional Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems are suffering from in term of their reliance on reactive, and isolated alerts. This limitation increases false positives alerts, leads to operational fatigue for analysts, and delays in identifying genuine threats. Proactive defense in SIEM can benefit from the integration of external Threat Intelligence (TI) in providing essential context. Indicator of Compromise (IoC) repositories can be used to support this via enabling the correlation of internal security events with data related to global threats. In this paper, a systematic methodology for integrating multi-source Threat Intelligence into SIEM platforms is proposed. The methodology implementation is done via a custom IoC repository within the Splunk SIEM environment, designed to facilitate the ingestion, normalization, and contextual correlation of external threat data. Additionally, correlation rules are developed using an autoencoder-based technique to synthesize this external intelligence with internal telemetry from diverse log sources and to identify complex attack patterns. An empirical evaluation of the proposed system is conducted using three distinct log sources: Windows Event Logs, Firewall Logs, and Web Server Logs. Results indicate a significant improvement in detection accuracy across all evaluated datasets when compared to traditional, intelligence-agnostic correlation. These results demonstrate that the strategic integration of TI substantially reduces alert noise and enhances the detection of novel, multi- vector attacks, thereby advancing SIEM functionality from basic log aggregation to contextual, proactive threat intelligence.

title: Automated CRYPT Architecture Categorization Using Advanced Endoscopy
authors: Ujwala Bhangale Chaudhari, Bisi Bode Kolawole, Cecilia Pugliano, Irene Zammarchi, Giovanni Santacroce, Rocio Del Amor, Pablo Meseguer, Valery Naranjo, Subrata Ghosh, Marietta Iacucci, Enrico Grisan
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Alteration of mucosal structure of the colon is one of the major hallmarks of disease activity in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) such as Ulcerative Colitis (UC) and Crohn Diseases (CD). Ultra-magnification Endocytoscopy (ECS) enables real-time, in vivo assessment of mucosal microstructure, but its clinical application remains limited due to the complexity of image interpretation. This work focuses on the assessment of the crypt architecture of the colon mucosa using Endocytoscopy data leveraging recent foundational models in endoscopy, we classify ECS frames as showing normal crypt architecture or necrotic mucosa, domain aware pretrained ResNet-50 model with GastroNet weights is used to extract the features; using 5-fold cross-validation, we achieve a frame level classification accuracy of 79 % and when aggregating the frame scores to obtain a video-level classification, the overall accuracy obtained is 89 % with 83 % sensitivity, 90 % specificity and 91% AUC.

title: Image Quality Assessment of Imputation-Induced Biomarker Distortions in Multiplex Immunofluorescence
authors: Priyanka Rana, Xingnan Li, Tuba N. Gide, Nurudeen A. Adegoke, Yizhe Mao, James S. Wilmott, Sidong Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Stain imputation in multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) aims to reconstruct missing biomarkers but can inadvertently distort inter-marker dependencies essential for biological interpretation, leading to misleading downstream analyses. Conventional metrics such as Structural Similarity Index Measure or Mean Absolute Error quantify pixel-level fidelity but do not ensure the preservation of biologically meaningful relationships. We present a biologically informed image quality assessment (IQA) framework that assesses imputation-induced distortions in inter-marker relationships. Images with imputed CD8 generated by OS2CR-Diff and Stain Imputation in Multiplex Immunofluorescence Imaging (SIMIF) are labelled according to changes in CD8-CD16 colocalisation (inflated, deflated, or preserved states), and a deep learning model is trained to identify these states directly from the imputed images, enabling quality control of stain-imputation outputs. To address class imbalance in the curated dataset, we introduce a Correlation-Aware Augmentation (CAA) approach that perturbs CD8 spatial distributions relative to CD16, simulating imputation-relevant inflation and deflation patterns. CAA consistently improved classification performance (AUC and macro-F1) across both datasets, outperforming conventional augmentation approaches.

title: RTFGM-Net: A Deep Learning Model for Auto-Detection of Hypsarrhythmia EEG in West Syndrome
authors: Chenghao Xue, Yifei Bai, Yuanning Li, Dake He, Lin Xu
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: West syndrome (WS) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by developmental delays and motor spasms, often diagnosed through visual detection of hypsarrhythmia in long-term electroencephalography (EEG) recordings. However, manual analysis is time-consuming and prone to errors, underscoring the need for automated diagnostic approaches. In this paper, we propose ResNet-based Time-Frequency Gating Mamba Network RTFGM-Net, an efficient deep learning framework for automated hypsarrhythmia detection in EEG signals. The model integrates a Bidirectional Mamba (BiMamba) and an Efficient Frequency-Time Gating (EFTG) module. The EFTG module combines point-wise and depthwise convolutions with a learnable frequency-domain filter for effective feature extraction, while the BiMamba module captures long-range temporal dependencies through bidirectional processing. Experimental results demonstrate that RTFGM-Net outperforms existing methods, achieving an accuracy of 91.01% and F1 score of 90.33%, highlighting its potential to facilitate reliable WS diagnosis in clinical applications.

title: Bridging the Density GAP: Diffusion Model for Stepwise Generation of Dense Cell Images from Sparse Data
authors: Masashi Tahara, Kazuya Nishimura, Shumpei Takezaki, Ryoma Bise
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: High annotation costs are a significant barrier in time-lapse cell imaging, as cell density increases over time, leading to numerous adjoining and difficult-to-distinguish boundaries. Our work is based on the observation that sparse images, which are less costly to annotate, still contain local regions with cell densities comparable to those in dense data. We aim to generate dense data from these sparse images for data augmentation. While attempting to bridge the significant distribution gap between the training (sparse) and target (dense) data in a single large step is a viable approach, it can lead to instabilities in the generation process, resulting in unrealistic images. We find that generation is more stable when this gap is bridged incrementally. Therefore, we propose stepwise generation, a diffusion model based method that incrementally bridges this distribution gap. This approach successfully generates realistic images usable for augmentation. Furthermore, a cell detector trained with data augmented by the generated images achieved high detection accuracy (F1 score) on dense data, surpassing conventional methods. This code is available at https://github.com/Masashi-Tahara/Bridging-the-Density-Gap.

title: A Unified Multi-Task Learning Framework for Ultrasound Image Analysis
authors: Hongshun Ling, Jinjing Wu, Shao Tong, Li Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound imaging involves diverse anatomical targets and variable acquisition settings, which complicates the creation of broadly applicable analytical tools. Conventional deep learning approaches are often narrowly tailored to individual tasks, which restricts their potential to serve as versatile foundation models in clinical contexts. In response, the Foundation Model Challenge for Ultrasound Image Analysis (FMC_UIA) 2026 establishes a comprehensive benchmark aggregating 27 distinct tasks-including segmentation, classification, detection, and regression. This paper describes our proposed solution for FMC_UIA 2026, which adopts a unified Multi-Head Multi-Task Learning (MH-MTL) architecture with hard parameter sharing. A MiT-B5 backbone pre-trained on ImageNet provides rich feature representations. For different tasks, we use multiple task heads for prediction, and we use a routing mechanism to control the task heads. Training proceeds in two phases-initial multitask joint learning, followed by targeted fine-tuning per task group. The experimental results have confirmed the feasibility and stability of this unified method. Finally, our total score on the test set was 0.9215, and we ranked third. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/linghs/ISBI-FMC-UIA-Challenge.

title: Hybrid LightFM Framework for Personalized Gamified Learning: A Novel Recommender Approach for Toddlers
authors: Vijayalaxmi N Rathod, R.H. Goudar, Dhananjaya G.M.
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Research Methodologies in Knowledge Management, Artificial Intelligence and Telecommunication Engineering (RMKMATE)
abstract: Personalized learning has become an essential paradigm in technology-enhanced education, yet most existing recommender systems are designed for older learners and rarely address the unique needs of toddlers. Early childhood learning requires approaches that account for developmental diversity, short attention spans, and the importance of engaging stimuli. This paper presents a hybrid LightFM-based recommendation framework that integrates toddler behavioral attributes from autism screening datasets with gamified content descriptors such as difficulty level, sensory load, and adaptive rewards. The goal is to deliver developmentally appropriate, motivating, and inclusive learning pathways for young children. A synthetic engagement simulation protocol was introduced to overcome the absence of large-scale toddler interaction data, enabling the generation of reward signals based on completion rates and time-on-task thresholds. Experimental evaluation compared the proposed framework against traditional baselines, including random, content-based, and supervised predictors, as well as ablated versions of LightFM. Results demonstrate that the proposed model achieved superior predictive accuracy (AUC 0.83, Precision@1 0.62, NDCG@5 0.71) while also maximizing cumulative rewards, minimizing regret, and ensuring higher diversity and faster convergence during simulated interactions. These findings validate the methodological soundness of the approach and highlight its potential to support personalized gamified learning in early childhood. Future work will involve real-world user studies, multimodal feature integration, and privacy-preserving deployment strategies to strengthen the practical applicability of the framework.

title: Technology, Talent, and Taxation: A Framework for Sustainable Financial Outcomes Through Audited Statements in Circular Business Models
authors: Rajeswaran Ayyadurai, J. Poongodi, Moses Adah Agana, Muhammad Sajjadur Rahim, Oluyemi Adewole Okunlola, Bibhu Kalyan Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Multidisciplinary Innovations For Smart &amp; Sustainable Future (MISSF)
abstract: This work further elucidates the manner in which talent, technology, and tax converge to effectuate sustained financial success for companies in a circular economic mode. This study investigates 272 companies across manufacturing, service, and distribution sectors in reference to how emerging technologies, a skilled and competent labour force, and open-book accounting for financial reporting affect profit generation and long-term viability. The relationships among the core variables considered, namely, total assets, liabilities, social sustainability income, revenue, and financial income, were investigated using multivariate techniques including factor analysis, multiple regression analysis, and ANOVA. The results obtained affirm the fact that the complementarity of advanced technologies in synergy with a competent and skilled workforce raises resource efficiency and profitability; alongside a fair taxation system and audited books of accounts, this significantly enhances the credibility of financial transparency and propels the process of sustainable development. The findings would serve as a guide for these companies to earn every single extra dollar in the circular economy paradigm.

title: AC-powered Dual-Active-Bridge (DAB) EV Charger with a Reduced-Current Stress Converter Using an Adaptive DC Link in a Three-Phase System
authors: Ranjeet Kumar Sah, Shashank Kurm, Shailendra Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Power Electronics, Smart Grid, and Renewable Energy (PESGRE)
abstract: This article presents a converter topology for electric vehicle (EV) charging systems, comprising a three-phase Active Front-End Converter (AFEC) and a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) converter. In conventional configurations, the AFEC maintains a fixed DC link voltage, resulting in a significant voltage difference across the DAB transformer's inductor. This leads to a triangular inductor current waveform with a high RMS value, causing increased conduction losses and reduced system efficiency. In the proposed architecture, the control strategy of the AFEC is enhanced to generate a variable DC link voltage that dynamically adapts to the EV battery’s voltage condition. This ensures the DC link voltage remains sufficiently above the AC input voltage while aligning the voltage levels of the DAB transformer's primary and secondary windings. As a result, a load-dependent DC link is established, effectively reducing the RMS current by approximately 8% to 53%. This reduction minimises current stress on the transformer, lowers conduction losses, and significantly improves overall system efficiency. Simulation results obtained using MATLAB/Simulink validate the effectiveness and performance gains of the proposed EV charging system.

title: A Medium-Voltage Bidirectional Isolated DC Transformer Using 3.3 kV SiC Devices
authors: Junjie Li, Hongyu Lin, Peng Jiang, Hao Feng, Li Ran
publish: 2026 IEEE Applied Power Electronics Conference and Exposition (APEC)
abstract: As DC distribution network attracts more attention for its ability to efficiently and reliably integrate distributed generation systems, bidirectional isolated DC transformers are recognized as a critical solution for interconnecting low-voltage (LV) DC and medium-voltage (MV) DC. To reduce cost, it is important to minimize such DC transformers’ volume and weight. SiC MOSFETs with higher voltage ratings readily achieve this objective by reducing the number of series-connected devices or modules. This paper presents a 3.3 kV SiC MOSFETs-based neutral-point-clamped (NPC) type dual-active-bridge (DAB) converter. Key design aspects of NPC inverter, including the main power loop, isolated gate driver, and medium frequency (MF) transformer are elaborated, with details on thermal management and insulation challenges. A back-to-back test with an LLC converter achieves 900 V to 3 kV conversion. At 26 kW cycling power, the efficiency of DAB converter is estimated at 98.7%.

title: External Spatial Adapter Tuning: Memory-Efficient Adaptation of Foundation Model for Sddh Diagnosis with Ultrasound Images
authors: Xueying Zhou, Jing Shi, Jun Du, Qian Wang, Jun Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 23rd International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI)
abstract: Ultrasound-based computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) has indicated effectiveness for developmental dysplasia of the hip (DDH). Recently, foundation models have shown promising potential to further improve the performance of a CAD model. However, their deployment in clinical applications remains challenging due to the high computational cost and overfitting risk of full fine-tuning. To address this issue, a new memory-efficient adaptation framework, named External Spatial Adapter Tuning (ESAT), is proposed for the CAD of DDH. The ESAT develops spatial adapters based on lowrank depthwise separable convolution at each layer of foundation model, so as to extract and refine hierarchical spatial features. These adapter outputs are further fused externally for the classification task, so that the gradient flow is fully decoupled from the foundation model. In real-world DDH diagnosis experiments, the ESAT requires only 30.16 % and 0.304 % of the memory and parameter usage on ViT-Base (as foundation model) compared with the full fine-tuning, yet achieves higher diagnostic accuracy than the full fine-tuning and other parameter-efficient tuning methods.

title: Better Together: The team approach to implementing virtual office hours
authors: Mary Beth Nipp
publish: eLearn
abstract: None

title: Toward Agentic Software Project Management: A Vision and Roadmap
authors: Lakshana Assalaarachchi, Zainab Masood, Rashina Hoda, John Grundy
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Workshop on Agentic Engineering
abstract: None

title: Utilizing In-Orbit CYGNSS Roll Maneuvers For Improved Level-1 Calibration - Part II: Application To Reflected Signal Channels
authors: Mohammad M. Al-Khaldi, Scott Gleason, Christopher Ruf, Joel T. Johnson, Darren S. McKague, Anthony Russel, William L. Wells, Tianlin Wang, Dorina Twigg, Patrick McEnaney, Joshua Synowiec
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: This work develops a methodology for using commanded roll maneuvers by Cyclone Global Navigation Satellite System (CYGNSS) constellation observatories to improve the determination of the nadir antenna patterns that receive GNSS signals reflected from Earth’s surface. The method relies on observation geometry properties in differing roll states that are used as a diagnostic tool to reveal Level-1 calibration errors associated with prelaunch mis-characterization of the nadir antenna patterns and as numerical constraints on algorithms to derive improved patterns that are more representative of in-orbit gain levels. It is shown that the updated gain estimates differ by -3 to 3 dB in a non-uniform fashion. In the companion work (Part III), the effects the use of these updated patterns has in terms of advancing the consistency of the calibration quality of observables is explored. Initial assessments presented here highlight how observable anomalies attributable to inconsistencies in the characterization of in-orbit nadir may be reduced by over 50% using the proposed methodologies.

title: Self-contrastive Learning to Boost Weakly Supervised Anomaly Detection
authors: Jingyou Chen, Zhangfa Wu, Yi Hua, Hongqi Li, Yilei Shi, Hongping Gan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Weakly supervised anomaly detection methods (WADMs) can effectively utilize incomplete label data to address the issue of imbalanced samples, thereby reducing the reliance on the quantity of labeled data and demonstrating superior anomaly detection capabilities in network and service management. However, when confronted with significant label noise and missing labels, existing WADMs still struggle to adequately extract the deep feature information of samples, leading to a decline in model performance. To tackle this challenge, we propose a self-contrastive enhanced weakly supervised anomaly detection framework, called SEAD-Net, which enhances the feature representation capability of data in the model’s feature space, thereby improving the accuracy and robustness of anomaly detection. Specifically, we first design a personalized data enhancement module that augments data representation by applying various transformations to the raw data. Subsequently, a self-contrastive enhanced learning module is introduced to impose hybrid constraints on the augmented samples, constructing the overall distribution structure while learning deep sample feature spaces under complex scenario disturbances. Finally, we extract contrastive enhancement features within the deep sample feature space and perform probabilistic generation to enable effective decision-making via an anomaly probability generation module. Experimental results on a series of public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our SEAD-Net outperforms the second-best WADM by 5.95% in average AUC-ROC and 16.28% in average AUC-PR.

title: Complex-Valued 2-D Residual ADMM-Net for Robust Super-Resolution ISAR Imaging
authors: Suijun Zhong, Quan Huang, Junxu Wang, Lei Zhang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Deep unfolding networks based on the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) have demonstrated significant potential in super-resolution inverse synthetic aperture radar (ISAR) imaging. However, in dynamic sensing scenarios characterized by varying sparse apertures and low signal-to-noise ratios, existing methods often suffer from limited adaptability and insufficient feature representation. To address these challenges, this letter proposes a complex-valued 2-D residual ADMM network (CV-2D-Res-ADMM-Net) for robust super-resolution ISAR imaging. Distinct from conventional implementations that rely on hand-crafted shrinkage operators, we design a learnable complex-valued 2-D residual module to implicitly learn the proximal mapping, thereby improving the reconstruction accuracy of complex structural details. Furthermore, a multisampling augmentation strategy is introduced during training to enhance the network’s generalization capability against diverse sparse sampling conditions. Experiments on measured data demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior imaging performance and robustness compared to existing ADMM-based methods.

title: Mixture-of-Experts Multi-View Large Vision-Language Models for Multimodal Fake News Detection
authors: Guofan Liu, Yannan Sun, Qiang Liu, Xiaolong Zhang, Zhe Zhao, Junfei Wu, Shu Wu, Liang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Existing multimodal fake news detection methods based on traditional small models are prone to learn superficial features while struggling to perform deep reasoning and truly perceive fine-grained image-text consistency. Recently, fueled by Large Language Models and large-scale multimodal pretraining data, Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) have seen significant progress in these aspects. In our pilot study, domain-specific fine-tuned LVLM (FT-LVLM) Qwen2-VL-2B has achieved superior performance for multimodal fake news detection. However, we still find two weaknesses within it: (1) The model tends to rely only on textual modality while learning little from visual modality. (2) Directly outputting predictions hinders the model from exploiting the powerful reasoning capability of the LVLM. To address the above issues, we further propose a novel framework of Mixture-of-Experts Multi-View Large Visual-Language Models (MMVM) to explicitly disentangle multi-view features within the LVLM and adaptively fuse them for fake news detection. Specifically, we first extract representations of three distinct views, i.e., main branch, visual branch and reasoning branch, from the LVLM respectively. Then, considering that a given piece of fake news may not contain clues of equal importance in all the views, View-Aware Mixture-of-Experts networks (VAMoE) are proposed to dynamically reweight single-view representations and route the most informative features for the final prediction. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the significant effectiveness and generalization ability of our proposed framework.

title: Strategic Construction of Initial Datasets for Active Learning: Leveraging Self-Supervised Learning
authors: SekJin Hwang, Daeyoung Heo, JinWoo Choi, Joonsoo Choi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Deep learning has demonstrated remarkable achievements across various fields. However, performance heavily relies on the availability of large-scale labeled data. Data labeling is time-consuming and costly, which has prompted numerous studies aimed at reducing these expenses. Active learning is a prominent data-efficient learning methodology that has garnered considerable attention. Active learning methods iteratively select data that are most effective for training models, thereby gradually constructing a compact dataset. These methods typically assume a small labeled dataset at the start of training, and experiments generally use randomly composed initial labeled datasets. Although the importance of initial dataset construction is well recognized because of its impact on model training in most active learning methods, practical research in this area remains limited. In this study, a data initialization method is proposed from an active learning perspective using self-supervised learning. This method focuses on constructing a small initial dataset that maximizes learning efficiency by utilizing an unlabeled dataset. The impact on active learning is evaluated using a representative image classification dataset, in which considerable performance improvements are achieved.

title: Rule-Based Detection of Renal Calculi from CT Images Using Classical Image Processing
authors: B Lakshmi Sirisha, K Hima Varshini, A Rehithson, M Harini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: The proposed method offers a rule-based method for detecting renal calculi from CT images without the use of machine learning. Additionally, the approach employs a structured filtration framework with three stages for removing irrelevant anatomy.The soft tissues are removed by intensity-based thresholding, followed by area-based filtering to eliminate bones and the spinal column. The artifacts from the outside, such as the CT bed, are then eliminated by location-based masking. The renal calculi are then separated using Hounsfield Unit (HU) thresholding, allowing for the calculation of stone number, position, and size (in mm). The results are then displayed with colored overlays on top of the original CT images.The proposed technique is non-invasive, computationally inexpensive, and hardware-independent, making it a viable candidate for real-time applications in healthcare environments.

title:
Enhanced Polarization Switching in HfZrO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    MFS FeFET Using WO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Interlayer
authors: Eunjin Kim, Hyoungjin Park, Jiae Jeong, Jiyong Woo
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: We demonstrate that the memory window (MW) of HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO)-based ferroelectric field-effect transistors (FeFETs) with an InZnO channel/HZO/W gate stack can be noticeably widened to over 2 V by introducing an amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> interlayer (IL) into the gate interface. Although an orthorhombic (o-) phase is clearly observed in HZO using microscopic images and diffraction patterns, a narrow MW of 0.5 V is obtained from the FeFET. Unlike those obtained from the HZO FeFET, a large 2 remnant polarization of 19 μC/cm<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and intense diffraction peaks corresponding to the o-phase are obtained from the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> FeFET. Surface topology and elemental analyses reveal that most of the phases of HZO can be uniformly transformed into the o-phase by the amorphous WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL with a low thermal expansion coefficient of ~1 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–6</sup> K<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–1</sup>. In addition, the WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL prevents unwanted defect generation at the HZO/W gate interface. Therefore, the number of dipoles in the HZO/WO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.7</sub> IL FeFET participating in switching increases without charge trapping, thereby improving cycling endurance and low-temperature (–150 °C) operation.

title: MDV: Resolving the Auxiliary Data Dilemma in Model Extraction Defenses
authors: Chuang Liang, Jie Huang, Shuaishuai Zhang, Zeping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Current studies have discovered that model extraction attacks (MEA) can steal the functionality of deep learning (DL) models, thus causing economic loss and other security threats. Extraction attackers can build a clone model locally that has a different structure but similar functionality to the victim model. To counter MEA, defenders utilize realistic auxiliary data to enhance the victim model and produce misleading predictions for attack data. However, these defense methods have three critical problems caused by utilizing realistic auxiliary data. First, in some scenarios, realistic auxiliary data is absent and difficult to obtain. Secondly, the defense effectiveness brought by realistic auxiliary data is unstable. Finally, the realistic auxiliary data did not protect all categories of training data, resulting in higher clone accuracy for some categories. To address these issues, we propose Model Defense Variational Autoencoder (MDV) to generate virtual auxiliary data as a replacement for realistic auxiliary data. MDV combines the Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and classifier, compelling the latent features to obey different multivariate Gaussian distributions according to the categories. Then, MDV samples deep features from low-likelihood regions of different distributions as realistic auxiliary data. During the experimental phase, we apply our auxiliary data to different defense methods that use auxiliary data and compare the defense effects in different scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our method effectively addressed the three aforementioned issues.

title: A Multi-Domain Relational Data Analysis Model with Varying Latent Dimensions
authors: Kenta Mikawa, Manabu Kobayashi, Tomoyuki Sasaki, Akiko Manada
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Consumer Technology (ICCT-Pacific)
abstract: In recent years, analytical methods for relational data, where relationships between entities are represented as numerical values, have gained significant attention with the advancement of the information society. While most existing approaches focus solely on relational structure, there has been limited research incorporating associated attribute information. Although some studies have proposed latent structure analysis methods for relational data with attribute information, existing multi-domain methods have limitations. These methods assume identical latent dimensionalities across all domains, which limits their applicability to real-world scenarios where domains naturally exhibit different complexities. To address this limitation, this study proposes a generalized multi-domain relational data analysis model that accommodates varying latent dimensionalities across domains. The key innovation is the introduction of a transformation matrix that enables flexible modeling of asymmetric interactions between latent variable blocks across domains, capturing complex relationships that conventional symmetric models cannot represent. We develop efficient estimation algorithms for both latent dimensions and model parameters without requiring iterative optimization procedures. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through comprehensive simulation experiments using synthetic data, showing superior performance in dimension estimation, latent variable recovery, and parameter estimation compared to existing approaches.

title: Statistical Analysis of Electromagnetic Field Radiated by Printed Circuit Board Inside the Perforated Shielding Enclosure in Time-Domain
authors: Yuan Zhao, Jing Li, Huapeng Zhao, Jiqian Wang, Chenyang He, Guohong Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: Within a shielding enclosure with apertures, the electromagnetic field radiated by the built-in printed circuit board (PCB) will form a complex internal electromagnetic environment (EME), which potentially impacts the operational performance of the PCB itself and the others. In this article, the electromagnetic compatibility issue is studied through statistical method in time-domain. First, a near-field scanning system was developed to measure the radiated electromagnetic field, which was compared with simulated results to verify the consistency with each other. Second, the statistical characteristics of the internal EME was analyzed based on kernel density estimation and statistic parameter estimation to identify its statistical properties under three distinct scenarios: PCB is excited by different signals; PCB is located at different positions inside the enclosure; and different observation time. It was found that under the aforementioned different conditions, the time-domain magnetic field inside the perforated shielding enclosure exhibits similar statistical characteristics when the PCB is positioned at the same location. These characteristics are independent of the excitation signal and time, and all approximately follow a lognormal distribution.

title: Design and Implementation of an Adaptive Control for Seamless Operation of Multiple Rooftop Solar PV Systems for Remote Zones With Local Grid
authors: Subhadip Chakraborty, Bhim Singh, B.K. Panigrahi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: In remote regions, access to a stable utility grid is often unreliable, necessitating development of autonomous power solutions. Integrating multiple rooftop solar photovoltaic systems (SPVSs) with centrally coordinated battery energy storage (BES) enables cooperative power sharing and energy self-sufficiency through BES-based power levelling and backup support, forming a local sub-grid. Such integration introduces significant challenges in maintaining seamless operational continuity and ensuring high power quality during transitions between grid-connected and standalone modes. Guaranteeing voltage stability and maintaining high-quality injected currents from each SPVS are crucial to safeguard sensitive loads and minimize line losses. Moreover, compliance with stringent voltage and current quality requirements of the IEEE Std. 1547 2018 is essential for interoperability and reliable performance. To address these challenges, this research presents a multisource rooftop SPVS architecture interfaced with a BES-supported local grid that facilitates seamless mode transitions and reliable synchronization with utility grid based on its availability. Proposed control strategy ensures stable and disturbance resilient operation by providing uninterrupted power during periods of low solar generation or a complete grid outage. Effectiveness of proposed control is thoroughly validated through detailed simulation studies under extreme operating scenarios, and through real-time results. Results justify conformity with power quality standards

title: Securing Medical AI: Adversarial Attack Detection and Mitigation in CAD Systems
authors: Nirmal Joseph, Sudhish N George, Ameer P.M, Kiran Raja
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Frequency-constrained adversarial attacks introduce imperceptible, high-frequency distortions that undermine the reliability and safety of AI-assisted medical diagnostics, posing a serious challenge to trust and resilience in socially-interactive, human-AI healthcare systems. To address this, we introduce Sentinel, a pioneering framework that detects, classifies, and mitigates adversarial attacks in medical image analysis without requiring training or model specificity. Sentinel integrates a lightweight detection module that uses a confidence scorer to analyze predictions from multiple low-rank approximations of the input image, enabling precise classification of attacks as either gradient-based or frequency-constrained. Uniquely, Sentinel includes a recovery module specifically designed for frequency-constrained attacks, employing adaptive Robust Principal Component Analysis-based denoising to suppress perturbations concentrated in smaller singular values while preserving essential image structure. This allows for effective image reconstruction, an area overlooked by existing methods. Evaluated across four diverse medical imaging datasets, Sentinel demonstrates consistently high detection accuracy, classification performance, and adversarial recovery without the need for model retraining or GPU support, making it a highly practical and scalable solution for integration into clinical AI systems. Additional explainability analyses confirm the reliability and interpretability of Sentinel's predictions, establishing it as a significant advancement in adversarial defense.

title: GWO-driven fractional order-based fuzzy logic controllers designed for a lower-extremity rehabilitation robotic exoskeleton
authors: Richa Sharma, Ravi Verma, Jyotindra Narayan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Processing, Hardware, Electronics, and Radio Systems (CIPHER)
abstract: Due to aging, the risks of falls and deterioration in movement capabilities are among the critical issues faced by older adults. On the other hand, people with some movement disabilities due to a disease, such as spinal cord injuries, stroke, or neurological disorders, may also face the risk of falls and loss of movement capabilities. The lower-extremity exoskeletons are recent developments in collaboration with robotics and rehabilitation engineering. These devices can help the person regain their leg movements and enhance/assist their strength. The rehabilitation using an exoskeleton could also help in speedy recovery. Therefore, a robotic lower-extremity exoskeleton requires attention to excel further in the field of rehabilitation engineering. This paper investigates the control of robotic lower-extremity exoskeletons (RLEE) using a fractional-order-based fuzzy logic with proportional integral and derivative (FF-PID) technique. The optimized control parameters are obtained using a nature-based grey wolf optimization (GWO) algorithm. The simulation results of the FF-PID-based control scheme are presented, and its results are equated with that of controllers such as PID and traditional fuzzy logic control (F-PID) approaches. The robustness testing and validation were investigated for the external noise. It is observed that the FF-PID controller can be effectively utilized for rehabilitation applications at the RLEE plant. Thus, this work helps design and control the new robotic exoskeleton device to rehabilitate older adults and people with movement disorders.

title: Ameliorated Cyber Security for Power Electronic Converters Serving Asynchronous Hydro Generating Systems
authors: Rupesh Kumari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: In recent years, the threat of cyber-attacks on industrial process controllers and power electronics converters (PECs) has highlighted the need for enhanced research into the control systems of hydropower plants. These systems have garnered significant attention due to their critical role in energy infrastructure. The IEC Standard 62433, Industrial Automation and Control System Security, provides key recommendations to address these concerns. With PECs now being network-capable, they hold significant potential to integrate into the Internet of Energy (IoE). However, this connectivity also increases their vulnerability to cybersecurity risks. This study proposes a parallel controller strategy (PCS) to protect PECs in hydropower plants from cyber-attacks. The PCS combines a digital and an analog controller to manage large variable hydropower systems effectively. A 250 MW hydroelectric generating unit serves as the basis for this research. The simulation results are analyzed to assess the system's performance against various cyber-attacks across different operating modes of the machine. The simulation and experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed technique.

title: Nonlinear Model Predictive Wide-Area Frequency Control for Inverter-Dominated Systems with IEC 61850-Based Communication Validation
authors: Manuel Bravo-López, Alejandro Garcés-Ruiz, Walter Gil-González, Juan S. Gómez, Catalina González-Castaño, Carlos Restrepo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources in modern power systems has significantly reduced system inertia, posing major challenges for frequency regulation. While advanced control strategies have been extensively studied, most existing approaches rely on simplified models, linearized formulations, or purely numerical simulations, limiting their practical applicability in real power system environments. This paper presents a real-time wide-area nonlinear model predictive control (NMPC) strategy capable of enhancing inertial, primary, and secondary frequency performance in inverter-dominated systems. To ensure practical viability, the proposed wide-area NMPC framework is validated through an IEC 61850-compliant communication architecture, facilitating interoperability with digital substations and SCADA systems. The proposed strategy is validated through real-time hardware-in-the-loop implementation of a system fully dominated by grid-forming inverters. Experimental results demonstrate that the controller can be executed within real-time constraints and significantly improves transient frequency performance and robustness under severe disturbances. These results confirm the practical feasibility of the proposed control for future low-inertia power systems.

title: A Neurosymbolic Multi-Agent System for Explainable and Traceable Decision-Making in Organizational Contexts
authors: Asimina Mertzani, Panagiotis G. Giannopoulos, Vangelis Malamas, Panos T. Chountalas, Thomas K. Dasaklis
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The increasing use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Human Resource Management (HRM) raises critical challenges for transparency, explainability, and accountability. While Large Language Models (LLMs) offer powerful natural-language interfaces, their tendency to hallucinate and their non-transparent reasoning limit their suitability for decision-making in HR contexts. We propose a transparent-by-design neurosymbolic decision-support system that integrates a provenance-aware knowledge graph with a modular multi-agent architecture and a constrained LLM interface. A scenario-based evaluation on a public HR dataset using a set of operational, strategic, and evidence-retrieval HR analytics queries shows that the system achieves mean fact-level performance of F1 = 0.677, mean traceability completeness of 0.680, hallucination resistance of 0.733, and mean end-to-end latency of 0.067 seconds, alongside a query-level accuracy of 0.733. The paper contributes to Information Retrieval and Generative AI research by illustrating how neurosymbolic grounding, provenance-aware retrieval, and agent-based architectures can enable trustworthy and governable information systems.
